name: 11 Cadogan Gardens
description: Whether planning a romantic weekend, a family getaway, a stay with friends or a business trip, the quirky British atmosphere of this boutique hotel, ideally located just between Sloane Square and Knightsbridge, is sure to delight. Inside the four interconnected Victorian townhouses lie fifty-six rooms and suites, each unique in design and offering an extraordinary level of comfort — perfect for complete relaxation after an active and exciting day in London. The superb Chelsea Bar centred around a Negroni menu, the drawing room where you can enjoy traditional afternoon tea, as well as the excellent Hans’ Bar & Grill — an address that has become a must in the area with its all-day seasonal menu rooted in British produce — complete this historic establishment known for its discreet and welcoming service.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
Breakfast Continental/English, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: Hans' Bar & Grill Restaurant
restaurant.description: A stone's throw from lively Sloane Square, discover the soothing atmosphere of Hans' Bar & Grill restaurant, home to chef Nigel Mendham and a simple, authentic and friendly style of cuisine. Keen to occupy a place at the heart of restaurant life, he likes to personally meet guests to convey his passion to them.

name: A Quinta da Auga Hotel & Spa
description:
Entering A Quinta da Auga is to arrive in a world of sensations where you immediately feel at home. This ancient paper mill from the 18th century has been lovingly restored by its owners. Embracing traditional Galician architecture, all the rooms provide views over the beautiful gardens. 
The property boasts a stunning spa and wellness area where guests are invited to relax. Its treatment menu combines oriental and occidental rituals using high quality natural cosmetics. At the Filigrana restaurant, food lovers will savour a contemporary cuisine with Galician influences, with a special presence of fresh local products and regional wines.

restaurant.name: Restaurante Filigrana
restaurant.description: Discover the culinary richness of Galicia. Through the unique menu of Restaurante Filigrana, Federico López Arcay reveals a passionate and authentic approach to the region's cuisine. Fish from Atlantic estuaries, meat from the surrounding mountains and vegetables from the kitchen garden fully reveal their flavors in original dishes inspired by Spanish tradition.

name: Ahãma
description: On a private bay of turquoise waters, surrounded by a fifty-acre forest, the Ahãma hotel has guest rooms that are sprinkled about the property in row houses and cabanas, all with soothingly understated interior décor. Looking out over the private beach or tucked beneath the trees, each promises a stay in harmony with nature, protected from mass tourism. Cuisine crafted with fresh, local ingredients is served in the four restaurants, providing a delightful variety of atmospheres and styles: Mediterranean, Mexican and Latin-inspired, or typically Turkish dining featuring Aegean mezze. In addition to relaxing on the beach, strolling through the forest, and enjoying non-motorized water sports, the Ahãma hotel offers a unique selection of holistic disciplines to explore, including Tai Chi, Qi Gong, Watsu, and sound therapies offered in the Sound Temple.

restaurant.name: Ege Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef blends Aegean flavors with Japanese culinary techniques. Combining seasonal produce, wild herbs, delicately prepared fish and robata cooking, each dish reveals a subtle and inventive harmony. A meal at Ege is a refined experience, uniquely shaped by the sea and the sun.

name: Aïda Hotel & Spa
description: Nestled in the prestigious ski resort of Crans-Montana in Switzerland is Aïda Hotel & Spa, a charming boutique hotel for adult only and whose warm and welcoming ambience blends beautifully with the natural scenery of mountains, lakes and fir trees that has made the area so popular. The decor of this hotel — which is owned by the Bestenheider family, who also owns the Hostellerie du Pas de L’Ours in Crans-Montana — combines traditional woodwork with contemporary furniture from leading designers and reused materials from local craftsmen. The spacious rooms and suites each recall the authentic and intimate character of a mountain chalet. With large windows that open out to the immense south-facing terrace, they are perfect for taking in the fresh air and spectacular views of the Swiss Alps and valley below. This feeling of space and plenitude continues in the sumptuous 300-square-metre spa. With a heated pool, sauna, jacuzzi and hammam, this special setting dedicated to well-being is ideal for relaxing after a day of skiing or hiking. To cap the experience, discover Aïda Hotel & Spa’s restaurant “Le Partage”, where the chef elevates local flavours to new heights.

restaurant.name: Le Partage Restaurant
restaurant.description: Le Partage lives up to its name: Chef Franck Reynaud offers a unique menu of small plates designed for sharing, within an authentic Alpine setting as refined as it is welcoming. Inspired by the seasonal flavors of the local terroir, her cuisine reveals a warm and inventive personality. The panoramic view is the finishing touch to each meal.

name: Akelarre – Restaurant & Hotel
description:
“Successful creations start with the best products. Each dish must be approached with the daring of a young chef, the experience of a mature man and the wisdom of an elder.” The personality of Chef Pedro Subijana, always striving to do better and his constant quest for innovation have made him an ambassador for new Basque cuisine. He adds a "personal touch" to traditional recipes: hake and its "kokotxa", oyster leaf and "mussel beans". Enjoy the impressive panoramic views of the Cantabrian sea from Mount Igeldo while dining at this restaurant located just five minutes from the centre of Saint Sebastián.

(In order to guarantee the tranquility of all our guests, we inform you that Akelarre does not recommend booking a stay with children under 14 years old. The Spa is available from 18 years old.)

restaurant.name: Restaurante Akelarre
restaurant.description: Our renowned restaurant Akelarre is a true icon of the New Basque Cuisine, where the care of local product, modernity and innovation have lived in harmony for more than fifty years.

name: Alex Lake Zürich
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: It is in the relaxing atmosphere of Switzerland’s Zurich where you will find Alex Lake Zürich, a splendid boutique hotel bordered by the calm waters of the lake and majestically overlooked by the Alps. In osmosis with its beautiful natural surroundings, this exceptional hotel offers 43 contemporary studios and suites harmoniously decorated and furnished with natural materials. Fitted with either terraces, balconies or French balconies, all are perfect for taking in the pure air and surrendering to the magnificent views over the verdant shores. With direct access to Lake Zürich, the private marina is ideal for a wide range of water sports. A haven of peace and relaxation, Alex Lake Zürich is the ideal place to enjoy a restorative stay that benefits the whole family. Last but not least, it is an exceptional address for the finest epicureans. Refined and simple, its restaurant Alex offers a creative and aromatic cuisine blending influences from across the world with hints of urbanity.

restaurant.name: Alex Restaurant
restaurant.description: Alex Restaurant is at the heart of Alex Lake Hotel and creates uncomplicated yet elegant and aromatic cuisine, with just a little urban flair. This new hotspot in Thalwil invites you to savor its dishes-whether for breakfast, a casual lunch or dinner.

name: Amberley Castle
description: Nestled beside the River Arun at the foot of the South Downs, Amberley Castle is a 900-year-old fortress turned countryside retreat–one of the oldest castles in the United Kingdom reimagined as a hotel. Behind its portcullis and ancient battlements lies a world of timeless charm, where beautifully designed rooms, elegant lounges and manicured gardens create a sense of serene escape. Guests can enjoy croquet, tennis or a gentle stroll through grounds wrapped in centuries of history. With interiors curated by the Brownsword family and cuisine crafted with seasonal, locally sourced ingredients, Amberley Castle seamlessly blends medieval grandeur with modern comfort, offering a stay that is as indulgent as it is unforgettable.
services: Packed lunch for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Queens Restaurant
restaurant.description: The esteemed reputation of Amberley Castle hotel is only enhanced by that of Queens, its gourmet restaurant. Local ingredients and fresh produce form the basis of chef Paul Peters' innovative approach to cuisine. Enjoy dishes while traveling back in time in its elegant 12th-century dining room, with guests warmed by an open fire.

name: Anne de Bretagne
description: On the sand dunes, facing the charming port of La Gravette, Anne de Bretagne is a contemporary villa, perfectly placed to enjoy the best of Brittany. There are breath-taking views over the ocean from the suites and the Italian-style terraces. In the excellent restaurant, Mathieu Guibert’s cuisine is inventive with a touch of the sea, in perfect harmony with the wines selected by Michèle Vételé, a highly-recognised sommelier. In season, you can try wild clams with shredded leek. Fish and shellfish create a perfect match: “slow-cooked” line-caught sea bass is served with a sardine concassé, a string of winkles and a sea lettuce emulsion.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Anne de Bretagne Restaurant
restaurant.description: My speciality? The sea air The sea is my inspiration, and I cook a lot of fish and shellfish. You have to create a cuisine which you like. For us, the restaurant is a whole: the cuisine, the atmosphere, the food and wine pairings... It's a passion we want to share with our guests...

name: Assiette Champenoise
description: Appearances are sometimes deceptive! The walls of this beautiful mansion hide a contemporary interior based on straight lines, trendy furnishings and design furniture. Founded nearly 40 years ago by their parents, the Assiette Champenoise is run today by Arnaud Lallement in the kitchen, his wife Magali in the dining room and his sister Mélanie, who are very keen to continue the family tradition of hospitality. Deeply rooted in the region, the property invites you to discover the best champagnes in the cosy bar or the highly acclaimed cuisine of the young Chef, who celebrates the quality of the products. Park, terrace and pool are a harmonious complement to this fine property.

restaurant.name: L'Assiette Champenoise Restaurant
restaurant.description: Cuisine is an incredibly unique process intimately bound to each chef's DNA. When you cook, you never forget who you are: where you come from, who you are cooking for, and especially where you're cooking. I have two signature dishes. The first is my blue lobster, which is a tribute to my father. It's a festive dish that Dad used to whip up on special occasions. The second is my Breton jumbo shrimp, which is my absolute favorite. When I start designing a dish, I'm always looking to capture the authentic taste of each ingredient, its richness and depth of flavor as well as its emotion.

name: Atrio Restaurante Hotel
description: Located inside the walled town of Cáceres with its extraordinary Almohad, Gothic, Renaissance and colonial buildings, Atrio boasts sublime architecture by Emilio Tuñón Álvarez and Luis Moreno Mansilla, masters of modern design. In the heart of the old stone buildings of this medieval town, Chefs José Polo and Toño Pérez prepare cuisine with unique, authentic flavours in a heavenly, extremely contemporary setting. Truffles, pasta and mushrooms, razor clams with cured Iberian ham, pig’s trotters, oyster and other gourmet creations are in complete harmony with the splendid building.

restaurant.name: Atrio Restaurante Hotel
restaurant.description: I'm a down-to-earth person and as a chef I like to use local products, such as Iberian pork, with all the cooking possibilities it allows. Cooking traditional food with today's techniques is a daily challenge that pushes me to constantly grow and learn. I want all guests to feel at home, and I want the dining experience to make them want to come back to Atrio again and again.

name: Au Cœur du Village Hôtel & Spa
description: The beauty of the alpine surroundings of the Aravis ski slopes, the creativity of its cuisine and the way every guest’s expectations are listened to turn this chalet with its elegant design into an exceptional destination in the heart of the village of La Clusaz. After a vigorous day on the slopes, relax in the superb Cristal Spa where, in addition to the fantastic treatments, guests can enjoy the hammam, sauna and pool. Gourmets will adore Le 5 restaurant, which offers dishes made from responsibly sourced local and regional products. After dinner, cap the night off with a cigar in the English-style smoking room before retiring to a rustic but refined guest room.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Gastronomique le Cin5
restaurant.description: The chef of Cin5 invites guests on an enchanting gastronomic journey that leads from Savoy to the Indian Ocean. With a passion for Mauritius, he uses spices in combination with sustainably produced, local ingredients to reinterpret the culinary heritage of this mountain terroir. A memorable gastronomic experience-and an exercise in subtlety.

name: Auberge de l'Ill - Hôtel des Berges & Spa
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Established over 150 years ago by the Haeberlin family, this country inn on the banks of the River Ill has risen through the dining ranks to become one of the country’s finest restaurants. Marc Haeberlin, now at the helm of L’Auberge de l’Ill - Hôtel des Berges & Spa, is from the fourth generation of the famed culinary family working at this institution in Alsatian cuisine. The food lets local ingredients shine in compositions combining classic regional specialties with more modern creations, all paired with an exceptional wine list. Meals are served in one of the poetically decorated dining rooms or outside in a garden encircled by weeping willows. Savor the experience longer by staying in one of the rooms or suites at L’Hôtel des Berges & Spa:  The accommodations dot the expanse between the main residence and the elegant wooden structure designed by architectural duo Patrick Jouin and Sanjit Manku, home to several light-drenched suites overlooking the magnificent Spa des Saules and the outdoor pool.

restaurant.name: Auberge de l'Ill Restaurant
restaurant.description: Like the three generations of chefs that preceded him, Marc Haeberlin perpetuates a tradition of quality and savoir-faire that this century-old establishment has become renowned for. His approach blends historic creations of Alsatian gastronomy with his own culinary compositions and contemporary creative touches.

name: Auberge des Glazicks
description: It began as a blacksmith’s forge, then an inn stocked by the adjoining farm where workers and villagers met. Today, it is where Olivier Bellin, who took over from his grandmother and mother, interprets his “modern” version of a “land and sea” cuisine steeped in history. The fish are wild varieties, the vegetables are organic and the meat comes from small producers. Magnified in dishes boasting intense flavours, they continually seduce local Bretons and attract travellers who enjoy staying in the rooms adorned in highly contemporary decor. On sunny days, the magnificent view over the Bay of Douarnenez adds the final touch.
services: In at least one room at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible
Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: L'Auberge des Glazicks Restaurant
restaurant.description: The fruit of a meeting between the sea and the land in the Glazik district of Brittany, my culinary world gave birth to the tripier marin. This healthy and natural cuisine is full of sea flavors to offer you a unique and wonderful meal.

name: Auberge des Templiers
description: Back in 1954 the Dépée family turned this former post house into one of the first Relais & Châteaux properties. Charming and tranquil, this top culinary destination is surrounded by Loire châteaux and the vineyards of Sancerre and Pouilly. Experience the rare pleasure of sleeping under a thatched roof, and allow yourself to be seduced by the cuisine of the chef in the restaurant, looking out on century-old oak trees. On the menu is a magical ravioli of wild mushrooms and velouté with a truffle jus, and John Dory in a La Ratte potato crust and sweet red onion sauce. This delicious food is complemented by wine from the highly acclaimed cellar.
services: at least one room and the main restaurant are accessible
Visits to regional wineries with tasting

restaurant.name: La Table des Templiers Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a charming setting, La Table des Templiers perpetuates the spirit of French culinary heritage. Chef Grégoire Touchard skillfully reinterprets classic recipes, using local produce to create authentic, generous, finessed cuisine. A destination of understated elegance, in the heart of a wooded park.

name: Auberge du Jeu de Paume
description: The Auberge, nestled in the Domaine de Chantilly, between the château gardens and the Grandes Écuries, takes its name from what was the Jeu de Paume, which now displays the monumental paintings from the Condé Museum. Its sober lines and elegant appointments, designed by Le Nôtre and Mansart, echo a very particular French-style art de vivre. Its restaurant, Le Jardin d'Hiver, serves inventive traditional cuisine, and the libraries and the open fire at the bar create a truly warm and welcoming atmosphere. The spa’s subtle lighting and coloured mosaics add the finishing touches to this perfect hideaway, just a few kilometres from Paris.
services: In at least one room at extra cost
at least one room is accessible
Visit to the Château de Chantilly

restaurant.name: Le Jardin d'Hiver Restaurant
restaurant.description: The authenticity of ingredients, mischievous pairings and silky sauces: the culinary signature of chef Clément Le Norcy at Le Jardin d'Hiver injects excitement into his cuisine. Overlooking the gardens of the Domaine de Chantilly, the Auberge du Jeu de Paume restaurant benefits from a remarkable setting, in harmony with the refinements that await you on your plate.

name: Avaton Luxury Beach Resort
description:
This is where the Titans fought the gods, where Mount Athos soars into the sky, and where the Virgin Mary, accompanied by John the Evangelist, came and blessed the mainisland’s shores, leading to the many monasteries scattered along its hillsides.
Avaton Luxury Beach Resort’s contemporary villas are a reflection of this mythological spectacle set within the Mediterranean’s garden and overlooking the Aegean Sea.
Owner Themistoklis Chantzis affectionately presides over this estate perched above a private beach of cerulean blue. As for the chef, he adds his own touch by flavouring the freshly caught seafood with wild herbs from his extraordinary garden. The sleek style of the private residences provides a wonderful contrast to the unique panorama, whose splendid history is within easy reach.

restaurant.name: Óros Monastic Fine Dining Restaurant
restaurant.description: Perched on a verdant hillside facing the sea and Mount Athos, restaurant Óros Monastic Fine Dining celebrates the emblematic flavors of the Greek landscape. The chef offers refined Mediterranean cuisine, made with fresh, artisanal local produce, which can be savored in the open-air restaurant or in a more intimate setting alongside your private pool.

name: Aya Estate
description:
Just a few miles from the border with Greece, Aya Estate unfolds over 500 acres of rolling vineyards hills and stunning geological formations that characterize the Melnik region. This Tuscan-inspired property perfectly embodies a relatively undiscovered side of Bulgaria, bringing together its typical gastronomy and culture with wellness, for an ideal family getaway.

The property’s close connections to this landscape are at the center of the guest experience. Wine is produced biodynamically from indigenous Wide Melnik grapes cultivated on site. The property’s two restaurants offer an authentic, natural menu made with estate-grown ingredients–including livestock, vegetable crops, even truffles. The same commitment to quality is found in the spa, where beauty treatments are formulated with grapes and honey from its hives. Aya Estate also includes an equestrian center, a large thermal mineral pool, and a dedicated cultural space, which hosts exhibitions and events, themed around art and wine. Everyone is free to enjoy their vacation at their own pace, amid 360-degree nature.
services: In at least one room and common areas
Group cooking classes given by the chef
Visits to regional wineries with tasting

restaurant.name: Aestivum Restaurant
restaurant.description: The chef at Aestivum works almost exclusively with estate-grown produce and is passionate about showcasing flavors from ingredients that thrive naturally here. Truffle takes center stage on the menu, and can appear in every course–from appetizers to desserts. The wine list carries nearly 300 wines from around the world, including accomplished local bottlings.

name: Ballyfin Demesne
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: “Yesterday, I saw a most delightful place indeed, much beyond any place I have seen in Ireland – Ballyfin”. The observation by an aristocratic 18th century visitor is still there today. The 600 acre demesne is a place of tranquility and great natural beauty. Created and refined over the past 400 years with its woodlands, lake, water features, grottos, follies and walled gardens, it provides an idyllic private world for guests to explore and enjoy a range of activities. At its centre is the late Georgian House which has been carefully and thoroughly restored to provide an exceptional level of comfort and luxury combined with all the facilities to be expected in a grand hotel.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: State Dining Room Restaurant
restaurant.description: The produce and ingredients that go into Richard Picard-Edwards's dishes are inseparable from the gardens of Ballyfin Demesne, from which they are directly harvested. They can be enjoyed amid the friendly atmosphere of the State Dining Room restaurant. The chef, on a constant search for new flavors, likes to chat with guests, who in turn inspire his cuisine.

name: Ballynahinch Castle
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Amidst the dreamy landscape of western Ireland, Ballynahinch Castle welcomes you for an exclusive stay surrounded by the stunning views that exemplify the region of Connemara. Set against a magnificent natural backdrop of rolling hills, this exceptional hotel is nestled between picturesque green forests and the tranquil waters of the Owenmore River, which flows at its feet. It is a setting made for magical walks, memorable bike rides and great fishing. A 19th-century edifice standing within an estate of nearly 700 acres, Ballynahinch Castle prides itself on being both majestic and warm, refined and simple. Its cosy rooms, with large windows to make the most of the views, provide the perfect accent to the surrounding Irish countryside. Its restored walled garden invites you to enjoy a sense of tranquillity, and its private collection of Irish art is truly one of the finest in the country.
services: Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Owenmore Restaurant
restaurant.description: From the art collection that adorns the walls, this Irish dreamscape extends outside: this is the setting of Owenmore, the gourmet restaurant of Ballynahinch Castle. Chef Danni Barry's cuisine is based on seasonal products, in dishes recognized for their contemporary approach and eye-catching style.

name: Baumanière Hôtel & Spa
description: At the foot of Les Baux-de-Provence, in an area nicknamed the ‘Valley of Hell’, discover a haven of tranquility. Nestled among the landscape’s rocky terrain and olive groves, Baumanière Hôtel & Spa has been a family-owned landmark here since 1945. In harmony with the nearby Alpilles mountains, the property authentically showcases the region’s slow-paced approach to life. Guest rooms are spread across several characterful buildings, where each unique space tells its own story, featuring paintings from the family's private collection alongside locally made furniture and other crafts. The cuisine completes your immersion in this evocative destination: Chef Glenn Viel uses ingredients from the kitchen garden and local artisans to compose a gourmet symphony at L'Oustau de Baumanière, while Chef Michel Hulin at La Cabro d'Or offers a finessed culinary interpretation of the terroir. Combining raw nature with refined elegance, Baumanière Hôtel & Spa embodies a timeless interpretation of Provence.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible
Group cooking classes given by the chef
Breakfast Buffet, Continental/English, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: L'Oustau de Baumanière Restaurant
restaurant.description: A story of cooking, a story of men. In 2015, I asked Glenn Viel to join me as head of the kitchens at the Oustau de Baumanière. First and foremost, it was his cooking that captivated me. True and simple, it respects local products from the terroir. It's also unpretentious, which is how I perceive my own cooking to be. A kitchen in which superfluous frills don't hide the essentials. He brings youth and creativity to the table while preserving the traditional spirit of Baumanière. I also like the man. He possesses a simple straightforward character, with no fuss or pretenses. He is from Brittany, which may explain this.

name: Beau-Rivage Hotel
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Located in a historic mansion built in 1862, the hotel Beau-Rivage is a haven of peace on the shores of Lake Neuchâtel. An ideal starting point to discover the cultural riches of the millenial city and the enchanting scenery around Neuchâtel, the cradle of Swiss watchmaker know-how. Cuisine based on local produce can be savoured at the restaurant “O’Terroirs”, with a panorama view of the lake and the Alps. And do not miss our traditional absinthes. You are very close to the picturesque city of Neuchâtel, whose beautiful yellow sandstone buildings made Alexandre Dumas write that the “city was like a town carved in butter”.

restaurant.name: O'terroirs Restaurant
restaurant.description: Combining tradition and innovation, the cuisine of restaurant O'terroirs could be said to traverse epochs of time and flavor. The chords are harmonious: the score striking a rare accuracy between new tastes and nostalgic reminiscences from the past. Enjoy beautiful regional products on the plate, in a lakeside setting amid the Alps.

name: Bela Vista Hotel & Spa
description: Situated on the beach of Praia da Rocha in this sunny region in the south of Portugal, a 19th century family house overlooking the ocean became the first hotel in the Algarve In 1934. The Bela Vista Hotel & Spa has now been refurbished and is a preserved resort on this busy coastline, where you can find relaxation and privacy amidst vibrant colours. Basking on the recliners around the very fine pool, or lounging on a beach with direct access, this is the perfect place to while the time away. The murmur of the waves is a constant companion, whether sipping a cocktail at the bar at sundown or enjoying a romantic dinner on the huge terrace overlooking the beach.
services: Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: VISTA Restaurante
restaurant.description: Inspired by Mediterranean flavors, chef João Oliveira's gourmet cuisine has its roots in the culinary traditions of Portugal. The freshness and taste of regional products are highlighted in the creative dishes of VISTA Restaurante, where the dining room offers guests breathtaking views of the ocean.

name: Bellevue Hotel & Spa
description: Nestled in the heart of the Aosta Valley’s Gran Paradiso National Park, at the crossroads of Italy, France and Switzerland, Bellevue Hotel & Spa stands encircled by pristine nature. With stunning views that will leave you speechless, Cogne is the ideal destination for outdoor enthusiasts. Summer welcomes hikers to unparalleled panoramas of the national park; while winter is nirvana for cross- country skiers and ice climbers. Amid all the splendour is Bellevue. Renowned for its intimate atmosphere, the hotel offers refined gastronomy in its four restaurants, a vast collection of wines, a superb spa and above all a warm welcome.

restaurant.name: Le Petit Bellevue restaurant
restaurant.description: The views of the Gran Paradiso glacier enjoyed from Belle Vue suggest how closely the cuisine of chef Niccolò De Riu is connected to the surrounding landscape. In this warm and intimate place, he celebrates his Italian heritage by working with only the freshest of seasonal products.

name: Bellevue Parkhotel & Spa
description: Overlooking the magnificent winter sports resort of Adelboden, the Bellevue Parkhotel & Spa stands proudly in the heart of a valley with some of the most beautiful alpine scenery in Switzerland. It is here, just south of Bern, where this boutique hotel, with its charming mid-century design and elegant silhouette, has found the perfect balance of harmony with its surroundings. Even the spacious rooms, distinguished by their generous windows, seem to invite the landscape in. And right outside, the cosy garden encircled by sprawling old maples is the ideal starting point for the many activities available in summer and winter. Whether as a couple or as a family, enjoy exploring the 210 kilometres of ski slopes, hiking in the area’s sumptuous nature or discovering the centre of Adelboden with its deliciously charming chalets. For those looking to relax, look no further than Bellevue’s spa. From its size to its indoor and outdoor pools, steam rooms, saunas and state-of-the-art equipment, it is sure to impress. Try a custom treatment or simply take a soak in the outdoor baths while enjoying the breathtaking panorama. And for a stylish dining experience, head to the acclaimed Belle Vue gourmet restaurant, where chef Jiri Urban delivers a creative Swiss cuisine.

restaurant.name: Belle Vue Restaurant
restaurant.description: Tranquil and awash with natural light, Belle Vue restaurant is the ideal setting to enjoy a gourmet experience overlooking the magnificent alpine landscape. Inspired by French cuisine, its refined menu also pays tribute to Swiss classics, showcased in elegant dishes made with the best local produce.

name: Bellevue Syrene 1820
description: There is an eternal, timeless beauty to the Gulf of Naples, with its jagged coast and deep-blue sea, and the Vesuvius rising in the background. In the most picturesque corner of Sorrento, the Bellevue Syrene was opened in 1820, having been originally designed in 1750 as a private residence built on the foundations of a Roman villa. The location, decor, comfort and sense of history at this hotel make it an extraordinary place. The fishponds full of water lilies evoke the Roman times, while frescos painted as a tribute to famous guests adorn the walls. The recent refurbishment of the hotel has balanced respect for the glorious past with contemporary comfort.
services: Daily excursions to Capri - Pompei - Amalfi Coast
Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: La Pergola Restaurant
restaurant.description: The spectacular view over the Bay of Naples serves as inspiration for the chef at La Pergola. Produce from across the Sorrento Peninsula, including fish, citrus fruits, olives and local wines, are at the heart of his contemporary and refined cuisine, where Neapolitan tradition meets the latest culinary techniques.

name: Bij Jef
description: An hour and a half from Amsterdam, twenty minutes by boat from Den Helder — between the land and the sea — lies Texel, an island in the Frisian archipelago. It is where chef Jef Schuur, hand in hand with his wife Nadine Mögling, has chosen to open a Michelinstarred restaurant and several beautiful modern suites in what was once a presbytery. With the changing tides, he cooks local products such as Wadden Sea cockles with Texel lamb bacon, parsley root cream and a jus of puffed celery. Nadine, a sommelier, is an expert in European wines and uses her extensive knowledge of organic and natural wines to create subtle and unique pairings to compliment the cuisine's harmonious contrasts of tastes and textures.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Bij Jef Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Jef Schuur's creative, modern cuisine is based on a constant quest for balance between the flavors and textures of the finest local products. Occupying a former presbytery with minimalist decor, Bij Jef restaurant offers a wonderful gastronomic experience, which the sommelier, recognized by the Michelin guide, excels in food and wine pairings.

name: Borgo dei Conti Resort
description: The luxuriant green countryside of Umbria, the “Garden of Italy”, is the perfect setting for this ancient mediaeval fortress. It is an impressive collection of neo-Gothic buildings, a Baroque chapel, surrounded by parkland planted with trees over one hundred years old and an olive grove, which offers outstanding hospitality. Outside, the infinity pool, the flower-bedecked courtyards and the shaded paths offer a cool haven of peace. Inside, the contemporary décor of the rooms and the elegance of the lounges also combine to create a wonderfully relaxing atmosphere. The restaurant serves traditional dishes in a vaulted dining room or on a terrace overlooking the hills.

restaurant.name: Cedri Restaurant
restaurant.description: Located in what was once a winter garden, Cedri is the elegant contemporary restaurant of Borgo dei Conti, where guests can enjoy a dining experience which is both innovative yet uniquely Umbrian. The staff is committed to working with specialist local suppliers who follow ethical and sustainable production methods to provide exceptional seasonal ingredients which fully respect nature's cycles. Tracing a path between the land, rivers and sea, menus at Cedri incorporate diverse culinary techniques and combinations to revive the flavours of the past, creating a modern expression of the tastes, aromas and traditions of the region.

name: Borgo Pignano Volterra - Tuscany
description: On the grounds of a traditional village that dates back to the 12th century, between Volterra and San Gimignano, Borgo Pignano Volterra – Tuscany rises with its honey-colored stone buildings amid 740 acres of forest and private gardens. With vaulted ceilings, columns and medieval frescoes, the hotel and its rooms feel steeped in history. Accommodation is spread across the grand main house, eight villas with private pools, and ten maisonettes that occupy former farm buildings. The estate’s organic farm supplies all the vegetables, olive oil and wine that feature on the menu. His refreshing take on traditional Tuscan cuisine is served at Villa Pignano restaurant, which overlooks the beautiful infinity pool, on the terrace of Al Fresco or at the large communal table at Il Camino. Whether you spend your vacation hiking along the estate’s trails, in cooking classes, relaxing at the spa, or truffle hunting in autumn, everything here invites you to savor la dolce vita.

restaurant.name: Villa Pignano Restaurant
restaurant.description: The classic flavors of Tuscan cuisine are on show at Villa Pignano, the hotel's restaurant. The estate's own farm provides Chef Angelantonio Cioffi with all the organic produce, olive oil and aromatic herbs that anchor his interpretation of traditional regional dishes, which are beautifully complemented by wines from a local vineyard.

name: Borgo San Felice Resort
description: Located in the heart of the Chianti Classico, Borgo San Felice Resort combines all of the pleasures of la dolce vita. The property is made up of an entire medieval hamlet including its old chapel, large pool, cellars and its vineyard surrounding it. Infinite pleasures can be discovered: authentic Mediterranean cuisine, an exceptional wine cellar, and the wellness centre. A bike excursion to explore the environs will enhance the enjoyment of an unforgettable stay in this medieval hamlet. Amongst other things, the beauty of the picturesque villages of Chianti, just a few kilometres away, will guarantee an eternal attachment to this region of Italy with its light and magic.

restaurant.name: Poggio Rosso Restaurant
restaurant.description: A rare culinary journey awaits you at Borgo San Felice Resort, in the heart of Chianti. The simple and elegant setting of Restaurant Poggio Rosso is home to the Mediterranean dishes of Head Chef Stelios Sakalis. He reinterprets tradition through innovative cuisine with distinct Greek - Turks influences.

name: Borgo Santandrea
description: Immerse yourself in the beauty of the infinite blue surrounding Borgo Santandrea, a mythical beachside hotel on the Amalfi Coast. Nestled between Amalfi and Positano, this absolute gem overlooks the Mediterranean from almost 300 feet above sea level, offering breathtaking views. Its 30 rooms and 19 luxury suites, connected by panoramic terraces, are an exquisite blend of Italian tradition, modernism, and mid-century design. It is one of the few hotels on the Amalfi Coast to have its own private shingle beach, which can be accessed directly via an elevator or a scenic path. In the heart of unspoiled scenery, it makes Borgo Santandrea an idyllic and much-loved destination, heaven on earth to enjoy alone or with others. This charismatic hotel's exclusivity can also be appreciated at the spa, in the stunning Mediterranean gardens, at the bar or by the pool, sipping a signature cocktail in the setting sun. Finally, its three restaurants—looking out over the sea to the horizon—invite you on a journey of sensations, flavors, and colors. You'll enjoy modern and sustainable cuisine showcasing reimagined traditional dishes and local ingredients.

restaurant.name: Alici Restaurant
restaurant.description: Alici, the gourmet restaurant at Borgo Santandrea, is a gastronomic tribute to the legendary Amalfi Coast, where the chef is from. From his home island of Ischia to the Bay of Naples via Capri, produce from around the region makes for particularly vibrant dishes, where culinary authenticity is complemented by delicious creativity.

nearestStation: Salerno Centrale 15 km/ Napoli Centrale 63 km

name: Brindos Lac & Château
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: In the heart of the Basque coast, in Anglet, Brindos Lac & Château majestically sprawls across the shores of one of the largest private lakes in France. Imbued with Hispano-Moresque charm, this exceptional hotel, close to Biarritz, offers guests a fascinating immersion into this mythical setting located between the Atlantic Ocean and the Basque hinterland. Its 39 rooms and suites, which elegantly incorporate elements and furniture from regional artists and craftsmen, provide the ultimate sense of relaxation. And for a stay unlike any other, its ten floating lodges on the lake offer guests the added pleasure of unparalleled intimacy. Nestled amidst a lush backdrop, its vast spa, featuring Gemology products and treatments, is a unique and magical interlude to any stay. An ambassador of fine living, the restaurant is deeply rooted in its strong cultural heritage and proudly promotes its Basque identity with a cuisine in which the chef expertly showcases the treasures offered by the land and sea. From gustatory delights to long walks that are both exhilarating and relaxing, and through the discovery of the land and its ancestral traditions, Brindos Lac & Château is a truly enchanted kingdom in which a thousand mesmerising experiences await you.

restaurant.name: Restaurant de Brindos, Lac & Château
restaurant.description: An intimate setting, convivial atmosphere and panoramic lake views set the scene for a unique culinary experience at the gastronomic restaurant of Brindos Lac & Château. Passionately committed to a locavore approach, chef Hugo De La Barrière produces truly audacious cuisine rooted in Basque traditions.

name: Brittany & Spa
description: Formerly a smuggling port and refuge, Roscoff has remained a unique destination, with its waves of emerald, granite cottages and manor houses nestled amongst the cliffs. This hideaway is an ancient trader’s property with a swimming pool, spa and direct access to the beach. The chef will treat you to an exceptional quality of fish and seafood, directly sourced from the local fishermen. His cuisine is a reflection of modern Brittany; it is a real pleasure to see, to feel, to taste! A 15 minutes boat trip is all that is needed to discover the island of Batz, a “tropical” pearl with palm trees and beaches of fine sand that has been magically misplaced in the English Channel.

restaurant.name: Nori Restaurant
restaurant.description: To experience a meal at Nori restaurant is to immerse yourself in the freshness of the region. With its panoramic views of the ocean, the location is ideal to highlight the creative cuisine of chef Loic Le Bail, which stands out for the quality of fish, seafood and shellfish on the menu.

name: Buckland Manor
description: Tucked beneath the Cotswold escarpment and surrounded by ten acres of enchanting gardens, Buckland Manor is a 13th-century retreat steeped in charm, heritage, and timeless English beauty. Once a cherished family home, its mullioned windows, crackling fireplaces, and timeworn Cotswold stone whisper stories of centuries past. Guests are invited to unwind in light-filled lounges, stroll croquet lawns and wildflower meadows, or explore the rolling countryside beyond. Dining is a celebration of seasonal, locally sourced produce–served with elegance in the manor’s candlelit dining room or al fresco beneath rose-clad walls. With golden stone vistas, peaceful seclusion, and heartfelt hospitality, Buckland Manor offers a rare escape into stillness, romance and classic Cotswold allure.
services: In at least one room and common areas
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Buckland Manor Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the dining room overlooking the gardens of Buckland Manor, guests enjoy elegant cuisine that celebrates the flavors of the English countryside. Menus highlight seasonal produce from the nearby Vale of Evesham along with herbs grown on the manor's own grounds.

name: Bülow Palais
description: With the Semper Opera House, the Zwinger Palace and the Frauenkirche, Dresden is one of the most fascinating places to visit in Germany. Right in the centre of the Baroque district it is home to Bülow Palais. This residence, which is run with passion, offers a taste of paradise to stylish and demanding guests. It boasts an elegant atmosphere, with a touch of originality designed to appeal to those who love all that is truly personal. Relax and enjoy breakfast in the light-filled winter garden, savour french inspired cuisine at Brasserie Caroussel Nouvelle, or meet up with your friends at the cigar lounge.

restaurant.name: Brasserie Caroussel Nouvelle Restaurant
restaurant.description: From fine dining to more brasserie-style dishes, Chef Thorsten Bubolz accompanies guests wherever their heart desires, depending on the mood and occasion. Using fresh, seasonal ingredients cultivated or selected with the greatest of respect to nature, his contemporary style unlocks a world of wonder at Brasserie Caroussel Nouvelle, in the Baroque quarter of Dresden.

name: Burg Schwarzenstein
description: Burg Schwarzenstein is tucked away in Mediterranean-style parkland surrounded by vineyards overlooking the Rheingau wine-growing region with stunning vistas of the Rhine Valley. This magnificently restored castle, which dates back more than 150 years, with its thick walls, is a haven of hospitality and the epitome of sophisticated authenticity. Rooms and suites alike are appointed with luxurious furnishing fabrics and silks in delicate hues of cream and beige which create a warm contrast with the dark tones of the wenge wood panelling. Food lovers will delight in the exquisite cuisine served in the elegant gourmet restaurant and the supremely romantic Burgrestaurant.
services: Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Burg Restaurant
restaurant.description: With its beautiful terrace overlooking the vineyards, Burg restaurant is the ideal place to sit back and relax with a glass of Riesling. Its accomplished dishes will satisfy the most demanding palates, with modern interpretations of classic regional cuisine made using locally sourced, seasonal ingredients. The chef also offers a dedicated menu for vegetarians.

name: Caesar Augustus
description: The hotel, perched on a cliff over the sea in a breathtaking position in Capri, offers from everywhere one of the most beautiful views in the world. Once a summer house of a Russian Prince, it is now a boutique hotel with an exquisite décor much loved by King Farouk of Egypt who was booking his suite for the year. Here you can enjoy the stunning infinity pool overlooking the Bay of Naples, the flavours of the restaurant with one of the best views on the island, the music on the terrace at sunset and the wonderful gardens. Feel welcome to the land of aesthetes and start discovering the blue legend of Capri!
services: Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: La Terrazza di Lucullo Restaurant
restaurant.description: Nestled between sky and sea, dining on the terrace of La Terrazza di Lucullo is a unique experience, not least as the cuisine of the chef is certified zero carbon', with Mediterranean dishes made with produce from the organic gardens of the Caesar Augustus hotel.

name: Cap d’Antibes Beach Hotel
description: Halfway between Nice and Cannes, in a setting where the Mediterranean takes centre stage, the Cap d’Antibes Beach Hotel is a contemporary architectural jewel. Its minimalist lines are in perfect harmony with the lush Mediterranean vegetation, with the charm and freshness of a garden by the sea, a fine private sandy beach facing the Lérins Islands, and a delightful infinity pool. The original décor of the rooms and suites transports guests on a sensory voyage. The enchantment continues in the restaurant Les Pêcheurs, which looks out onto the sea and offers a feast for the palate as well as the eyes, with a view of the Esterel massif in the distance. The chef offers high-flying, delicate and refined cuisine, made with seasonal products, from local producers, with whom he works hand in hand.

restaurant.name: Les Pêcheurs Restaurant
restaurant.description: The refined style of Cap d'Antibes Beach Hotel and its palm-fringed Côte d'Azur setting are reflected in the cuisine of chef Nicolas Rondelli. At gastronomic restaurant Les Pêcheurs he combines exotic flavors and the richness of the surrounding terroir, immersing diners in a unique culinary interpretation of a Mediterranean garden.

name: Cap Menorca
description: On the southern Menorcan coast, high on a cliff overlooking the Mediterranean, Cap Menorca is surrounded by a 75-acre pine forest that has been transformed into grounds of lush tropical vegetation by more than 15,000 planted trees, shrubs, and flowers. Encircled by dry-stone walls, like the traditional Menorcan country homes called fincas, the suites are tucked away in gardens, each with a private swimming pool sheltered by century-old olive trees. The décor is elegant and simple: whitewashed walls, mahogany woodwork, verdigris tile in the bathrooms. A stay at Cap Menorca is made all the more memorable by the many experiences to choose from. The cuisine features Mediterranean flavors and is crafted with local, in-season ingredients. Meals are served in the hotel restaurant, but can be enjoyed beside your swimming pool for a tête-à-tête dinner. The spa offers relaxing massages either outside, where you can gaze upon the blue waters of the Mediterranean or in your suite. Yoga classes, as well, can be enjoyed outdoors. If you prefer to contemplate the coast from the open sea, set off on a sun-drenched cruise aboard one of the hotel yachts to discover secret coves and beaches of fine, soft sand.

restaurant.name: Cap Menorca Restaurant
restaurant.chef: Chef Miquel Àngel Muñoz & Robert Stevens
restaurant.description: Chefs Miquel Àngel Muñoz & Robert Stevens explores Menorca in their own way, sourcing coves and vegetable gardens, local traditions and historically French techniques. They craft their own uniquely expressive cuisine at Cap Menorca, dishes centered on freshness, honoring the seasons and harvests. Land and sea dialogue in food that is unfussy, unexpected, yet always true to its origins.

name: Capofaro Resort
description: Salina is the greenest of the seven Aeolian Islands that emerge from the deep blue Tyrrhenian Sea. Capofaro’s small whitewashed buildings and fresh water swimming pool stand regally amid the vineyard of the Tasca d’Almerita family, at the foot of one of two extinct volcanoes. Simply adorned, each room opens onto a terrace facing the sea where the fascinating silhouettes of neighbouring Stromboli and Panarea take shape. The chef leads guests on a sun-filled gastronomic journey, infused with the salty and sweet flavours of the produce grown in the property's rich volcanic soil. Following the grand monsù tradition befitting this Sicilian land of aristocracy, he proposes pairings with the Malvasia wine produced in the family vineyard.

restaurant.name: Sira Ristorante Capofaro Restaurant
restaurant.description: Sira restaurant–which means “tonight” in Sicilian–celebrates the joy of gathering around the table. Chef Matteo Manco blends his Salento roots with Salina’s volcanic energy in gourmet dishes, where estate-grown seasonal produce and the catch of the day bring to life the unique spirit of this destination.

name: Casa da Calçada
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: The lemon-yellow facade of the Casa da Calçada hints at the sunny stay that awaits at this hotel. Situated in the heart of the town of Amarante, between the bustle of Porto and the sun-dappled countryside of the Douro Valley, both less than an hour away, this building dating back to the 16th century, with its golden baroque interiors, is a favourite with those who love beauty, golf, great wine and gastronomy. Nearby you find a splendid 18-hole golf course. The hotel also produces “Vinho Verde” wines, and on-site activities include a tour of the region’s best wine “quintas” that will ensure that you never forget the differences between Tawny, Ruby, White or Vintage Port.

restaurant.name: Largo do Paço Restaurant
restaurant.description: Home cooking reaches a new dimension at Largo do Paço restaurant. The chef reinterprets Mediterranean dishes with Portuguese influences, combining the best of culinary tradition with modern gourmet sophistication. The warm service and convivial atmosphere add to the pleasure of a delicious, authentic experience.

name: Casa de São Lourenço
description: Casa de São Lourenço is nestled in the Serra da Estrela, a mountain range that is home to the UNESCO-recognized Estrela Geopark. Perched at an altitude of 4,100 feet among pine trees, this former pousada (traditional guesthouse) enchants outdoor enthusiasts all year round. The architects who led its modern makeover succeeded in preserving its original character, while enhancing its connections to the land by adding beautiful panoramic terraces. The hotel’s colorful decor bears the signature of Portuguese artist Maria Keil, blending 1940s design with contemporary artisanship, such as ceilings lined with burel wool. Cozy, inviting rooms and suites feature large picture windows framing views of the glacial Zêzere Valley. Beyond these visual delights, guests can unwind in the soothing spa or savor authentic cuisine at Restaurant Fatiga, where chefs draw inspiration from the traditional cuisine enjoyed by shepherds in the region.

restaurant.name: Fatiga Restaurant
restaurant.description: Creative but hearty cuisine, made with local, seasonal ingredients is on the menu at Fatiga, the hotel's restaurant. In harmony with the rustic authenticity of the valley, Chefs Miguel Ramos and Marco Lageo pay homage to the traditional recipes of the Beira region, while diners enjoy panoramic views over the mountainous scenery.

name: Casa Velha do Palheiro
description: On the island of Madeira the “Pearl of the Atlantic”, the Casa Velha do Palheiro welcomes you from its setting above the busy city of Funchal. The original building was a hunting lodge built by the Conde de Carvalhal in 1804 and fully restored in 1997. The Casa Velha is a haven of calm surrounded by the famous Palheiro gardens and the 72 par Palheiro golf course. It’s an ideal place to unwind, enjoy the gardens and walk the levadas (irrigation channels) in the foothills above the Estate. Relax in the Spa, play golf and lunch at the Club House overlooking the Atlantic or visit Funchal for a glass of Madeira at the Wine Lodges and check out the Casa Velha motor yacht “Balancal” lying in the marina.

restaurant.name: Oxalis Restaurant
restaurant.description: Our dishes' flavours are testament to the balance between our experience and our knowledge of international and regional cuisine. The team's spirit and the freshness of the produce used are also essential ingredients for our cuisine's success.

name: Cashel Palace
description:
At the foot of a hill, in the shadow of Ireland’s most iconic medieval site, the Rock of Cashel, stands another architectural masterpiece, Cashel Palace. Built in 1732 by Sir Edward Lovett Pearce, the architect who designed the Parliament House in Dublin, this red brick manor was once the residence of Ireland’s archbishops. It is a brilliant blend of classicism and sophistication, with a façade that hides behind its symmetrical layout a world of surprises: a majestic colonnaded hall, walls covered in detailed wood panelling, staircases with handrails carved in the shape of candy canes, and rooms that seem to have been taken from a fairy tale.
With its English-style gardens, comfortable sofas warmed by the cosy fire throughout the house, and a spa bathed in light, Cashel Palace takes its guests on a magical journey. It is an idyllic refuge where you can recharge your batteries before plunging into the area’s Celtic history or wandering through the enchanting moorland.

restaurant.name: The Bishop's Buttery Restaurant
restaurant.description: Irish cuisine that celebrates food and nature, the ultimate sources of life: this is the motivation of chefs Stephen Hayes and Stefan McEnteer at The Bishop's Buttery. His menus, at the gourmet restaurant of Cashel Palace, follow the rhythm of the seasons and reveal a modern style of cuisine infused with French culinary touches.

name: Castel Fragsburg
description: On the heights of Merano, in South Tyrol, stands Castel Fragsburg, an exceptional hotel from the 17th century majestically perched above the town. Distinguished not only by its style, but also by its unique philosophy and art de vivre, it is a true refuge surrounded by glorious, unspoilt nature. From its elevated terraces, enjoy spectacular views over the Adige Valley; or bask in the heart of its five-hectare fairy-tale garden and reconnect with the beauty of the world through a celebration of the senses. Inside its twenty suites, discover elegant interiors that combine alpine comfort and Mediterranean sensuality for optimal relaxation. This spirit is echoed in the Alchemistic Healing Spa, the first of its kind, which offers marvellous beauty treatments, as well as in the jacuzzi and by the heated outdoor pool where unforgettable moments under the sun are sure to be had. In the gourmet restaurant, the South Tyrolean chef highlights healthy regional products and invites you to savour exquisite aromas that also serve as wonderful elixirs of life.

restaurant.name: Gourmet Restaurant Prezioso
restaurant.description: To create his menu, chef Egon Heiss uses ancestral recipes from the region, prepared with ingredients harvested in the garden of Castel Fragsburg hotel, as well as produce from nearby organic farms. Amid the serenity of gourmet restaurant Prezioso, or on its wooden terrace, prepare yourself for an explosion of color, freshness and aroma.

name: Castello Banfi - Il Borgo
description: A hamlet perched on a hill south of Siena, Castello Banfi - Il Borgo proudly reigns over its prestigious vineyards. Along the rolling landscape of the estate, discover a classic and romantic Tuscan kaleidoscope of grapevines, plum orchards and woodlands.As great vintages call for the finest in hospitality, the decor of the former farm buildings was entrusted to renowned interior designer Federico Forquet. He designed the suites, reading room and restaurants to all be warm and elegant spaces, conducive to the pleasures of tasting the exquisite wines produced by the Mariani family.

restaurant.name: La Sala dei Grappoli Restaurant
restaurant.description: The Montalcino region is the ideal environment to inspire the cuisine of chef Domenico Francone. Here, in the elegant setting of La Sala dei Grappoli, he boldly reinterprets Tuscan culinary traditions. Enjoy his dishes in a delightful setting, on the hill where the Castello Banfi Wine Resort is also to be found.

name: Castello di Guarene
description: With heavy drapes, paintings, lavish period furniture, carefully restored parquet floors, this former summer residence for the Counts of Roero, built on the site of a medieval fortress, is a delight for the eyes. Surrounded by gardens designed in the style of 18th century Italy, overlooking the city of Guarene, it offers splendid views over the Langhe hills, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. A refined Piedmontese cuisine is served in the restaurant, and provides the occasion to taste the prestigious regional wines. At the end of a long brickvaulted hallway, the spa and indoor pool carved into the rock provide a peaceful retreat.

restaurant.name: Ristorante Limonaia
restaurant.description: In the baroque setting of Castello di Guarene, La Limonaia invites you to encounter the culinary delights of Piedmont. Chef Marco Pedde cleverly reinterprets regional classics, showcasing local produce in dishes that carefully balance tradition and modernity, reflecting the elegance of the setting.

name: Central Park Voorburg
description: Set in lush parkland and surrounded by trees, yet just a few miles from the city center of The Hague in the Netherlands, Central Park Voorburg welcomes guests in fourteen elegantly decorated suites. A visceral energy emanates from this former private residence, now a recognized national monument. Among all this greenery, guests can properly disconnect from regular life. Located between Rotterdam and Amsterdam, this is the ideal place for a romantic weekend. Central Park Voorburg is profoundly connected to its surrounding terroir, not least at its gourmet restaurant, where chefs Hette Hettema and Tim Bood present refined cuisine that is both classic and modern – in line with the hotel's ambition to contribute to a better world through food and hospitality – using seasonal, sustainable ingredients enhanced by great wines.

Chefs Hette Hettema and Tim Bood showcase seasonal produce in gourmet dishes inspired by Franco-Dutch heritage. A refined culinary experience just outside The Hague.

name: Chalet Hotel Schönegg
description: With the Matterhorn towering overhead, there is no doubt that Chalet Hotel Schönegg in Zermatt is truly located in the heart of the Swiss Alps. The hotel's design reinterprets traditional alpine style, giving a modern twist to natural materials such as wood and stone, and creating a feeling of harmony with the surrounding environment. From the panoramic terrace or guest-room balconies, the epic landscapes and colossal mountains make a spectacular backdrop. Surrounded by 250 miles (400 kilometres) of pine-lined paths and more than 180 miles (300 kilometres) of ski slopes, this is a paradise for hikers, bikers and skiers alike. After the day's exertions, discover the relaxed atmosphere of gourmet restaurant Saveurs, home to chef Maximilian Latt. His fine cuisine sensitively channels the local terroir, with dishes accompanied by wines from an impressive cellar of over 4000 bottles. Some wines have been produced in the local area for more than four generations by the Metry family, owners of the hotel.
services: In at least one room at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Saveurs Restaurant
restaurant.description: From its home at Chalet Hotel Schönegg, gourmet restaurant Saveurs offers incomparable views of the panorama around the nearby Matterhorn. While gazing at the breathtaking terrain, discover Chef Maximilian Latt's signature four-course menu. Made from fresh local produce, the dishes represent truly authentic regional cuisine that encapsulates the mountainous terroir.

name: Chasa Montana Hotel & Spa
description: Chasa Montana, in the heart of the village of Samnaun, has all the attractions of a beautiful mountain chalet. It boasts a wealth of delightful places to simply linger: under the shady parasols on the terrace, in the seclusion of a private balcony or in the superb spa overlooking the mountains. The hotel’s restaurants offer a variety of fare, from gourmet cuisine at La Miranda to delicious cheese raclette and fondue at La Grotta, which can be paired with any of the 1,500-plus vintages from around the world featured on the wine list. Maître de maison Daniel Eisner and the hotel’s sommeliers will be delighted to give you a private guided tour of the exceptional wine cellar where you can discover more than 20,000 bottles.

restaurant.name: La Miranda Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Bernd Fabian presents contemporary cuisine of Franco-Mediterranean inspiration, using high quality local products cultivated sustainably. In the elegant dining room of La Miranda, just sixteen guests are seated to enjoy an exclusive and memorable culinary adventure.

name: Château Cordeillan-Bages
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Located between the estuary and the beaches of the Atlantic, this classic Gironde charterhouse is a lively place where the Cazes family cultivates the discreet charm and warm elegance of a home surrounded by vineyards and adorned with the works of contemporary artists. The wine list, with more than 1,800 vintages, includes Bordeaux Grands Crus such as the renowned Château Lynch-Bages, which has been with the family since 1939. During a winemaking session, assemble your own wine and start tasting. A few steps away, the village of Bages is an essential stop on the Bordeaux wine route, where you can wander at your leisure and truly experience art de vivre!

restaurant.name: Café Lavinal Restaurant
restaurant.description: Located just a few steps from Château Cordeillan-Bages, the friendly welcome of Café Lavinal articulates the art of living in the Médoc. The traditional cuisine of chef Gabriel Gette is prepared exclusively using local products, and can be enjoyed amid decor inspired by the 1930s or on the terrace, with breathtaking view over the village's cobbled square.

name: Château d’Adomenil
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Just minutes from Lunéville, which Voltaire compared to the Palace of Versailles, Château d'Adomenil presides over an expansive, 17-acre woodland-covered estate, bordered by a river, that beautifully characterizes the undulating landscape of Lorraine. Owners Sophie and Cyril Leclerc welcome guests to this magnificently restored 16th-century residence. Guest rooms, each one unique, are spread across the château and its outbuildings, and comprise cozy sanctuaries that open variously onto the grounds, the main courtyard or the kitchen garden, which provides Chef Cyril Leclerc with daily inspiration. He creates classic and contemporary cuisine using locally sourced produce. Château d'Adomenil is ideal for a romantic getaway or a family holiday, with opportunities to explore the estate on foot, have fun in the swimming pool and visit local cultural attractions that invite you to dive into the region's rich heritage. The Baccarat crystal factory is close by, as is the city of Nancy, with Place Stanislas, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, at its center.

restaurant.name: Château d’Adomenil Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the restaurant overlooking the grounds of Château d’Adomenil, Chef Cyril Leclerc's contemporary cuisine blends local, seasonal produce with a lifetime of travel memories. His bold, creative dishes reflect the finesse and opulence of the property.

name: Château d’Audrieu
description: In the heart of Normandy, located between Caen and Bayeux, Château d’Audrieu offers an enigmatic step back in time. This 17th-century building, officially listed as a French Historic Monument, retains the warmth, welcoming feel and conviviality of a family home. Its formal French and English gardens extend over more than 60 acres, offering both majestic avenues and secluded glades, all a haven of greenery and serenity. Here, every moment celebrates Normandy’s unique art de vivre: from strolls in the estate to excursions to the D-Day landing beaches, via visits to the Bayeux Tapestry, the historic city of Caen–one of the seats of the Dukes of Normandy–or to Mont-Saint-Michel. To fully savor the region, the Château's restaurant, Le Séran, offers authentic yet inventive cuisine that captures the distinctive flavors of Normandy.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost
at least one room and the main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Le Séran Restaurant
restaurant.description: Le Séran restaurant tells a unique story through cuisine that is deeply rooted in Normandy, authentically inspired yet gastronomic in execution. Chef Samuel Gaspar showcases the treasures of the terroir, combining produce from the castle's own kitchen garden with locally sourced meat and seafood, in vibrant, inventive dishes.

name: Château de Berne
description: Château de Berne embodies Provençal art de vivre amid an exceptional setting of vineyards and hillsides, where the light and fragrance of the Mediterranean creates an authentic and evocative atmosphere. Here in the heart of nature, this 1,235-acre wine estate produces wines under the Côtes de Provence appellation. The 18th-century chateau is home to elegant guest rooms and suites, as well as the restaurant, which serves bold and inventive farm-to-table cuisine. Close to the chateau, stone-built villas offer guests more private, intimate stays. Each one has a dedicated butler, and villa guests can also request a private chef to prepare meals using ingredients from the estate. Guests are encourage to enjoy life at their own pace: they can take a wine or cooking workshop, walk or cycle on the surrounding trails, enjoy holistic treatments at the spa or just relax by the pool. As each day unfolds, Château de Berne represents the perfect harmony between nature, wine, gastronomy and well-being.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost

Breakfast Buffet, Continental/English, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: Le Jardin de Berne Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Louis Rameau produces cuisine that pays homage to the nature that inspires and guides him. In the bright atmosphere and contemporary decor of gourmet restaurant Le Jardin de Berne, his seasonal dishes reveal their authenticity, and a sense of poetry, in bringing together all the elements of Provence.

name: Château de Courcelles
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Legend has it that Jean Cocteau designed the staircase of the Château de Courcelles... Regardless of whether it is true, this rumour speaks volumes about the attention to detail in this palace where Jean-Jacques Rousseau once liked to stroll in the French garden and French literary figures Racine, La Fontaine and Dumas stayed between Paris, the Aisne and the Champagne. Which now famous pages did they write here? This can be contemplated while strolling through the magnificent grounds, swimming in the pool, playing a round of golf, or a game of tennis, and dining like royalty, discovering the best local products and excellent Champagnes.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
Individual cooking classes given by the chef
Group cooking classes given by the chef

restaurant.name: La Table de Courcelles Restaurant
restaurant.description: Gastronomic excellence, authenticity and terroir are the keywords of Chef Massimiliano Sena's cuisine. His Menu du marché changes every two weeks: every evening, his gourmet menus give pride of place to seasonal produce. Discover an experience to be savored in the conservatory or on the verdant terrace.

name: Château de Fonscolombe
description: Just a few miles from Aix-en-Provence, Château de Fonscolombe is an elegant, 18th-century national monument, located within extensive parkland, that epitomizes the Provencal art of living. This former family home, with its ochre-colored, Italianate charm, sits on a 123-acre estate that includes its own vineyards. The wines it produces complement the cuisine, with dishes inspired by the daily harvest from the sun-drenched, organic vegetable garden, paired with produce from local artisans. The chef blends the terroir with culinary creativity to truly showcase the region, to the delight of gourmets. Ideal for family vacations, the estate offers spacious rooms and features a children's swimming pool. With numerous activities on site and even more available nearby, it makes for an inspired getaway that’s true to the spirit of the south: free-spirited, sunny and convivial.

restaurant.name: La Table de L’Orangerie
restaurant.description: At La Table de l’Orangerie, Chef Marc Fontanne celebrates Provence with a style of cuisine that’s delicate yet vibrant, served in a setting bathed in natural light. Inspired by the seasons and freshly harvested ingredients from the chateau's kitchen garden, dishes blend color and texture and feel profoundly connected with the region's terroir and culinary heritage.

name: Château de Germigney
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: World-renowned interior designers Roland and Véréna Schön have turned Château de Germigney into a stunning hotel, located in Port-Lesney, a small commune in Jura not far from Besançon. Cedarwood parquet floors, period furniture and silky wall hangings give each room in this former home of a marquis, nestled among pristine lakes, the feel of an art gallery. Enjoy relaxing in its elegant rooms and suites, all brimming with extraordinary character, and swimming in the 100%  natural pool, designed to be environmentally friendly. At Château de Germigney, every chair is an invitation to curl up with a book, or with a glass of the region's best wines. At the Restaurant Château de Germigney, enjoy the creative cuisine of the chef. Creator of emotions and craftsman of flavors, he carefully selects products and local producers while considering biodiversity and the seasons. He also builds on the remarkable culinary legacy of André and Jean-Paul Jeunet, reworking their iconic dishes.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Château de Germigney
restaurant.description: Creator of emotions and craftsman of flavors, the chef Stefano Ciabarri is a bearer of Relais & Châteaux's values and commitments to taste. He carefully selects local producers and products while showing his respect for biodiversity and the seasons. His wish is for diners to feel at home at Restaurant Château de Germigney when they arrive, and to look forward to returning soon when they leave. It's this philosophy of goodness, generosity, and sharing that guides and motivates him while he's working in the kitchen.

name: Château de La Chèvre d’Or
description: This château, built on the steep slopes of the French Riviera, offers breath-taking vistas of the Mediterranean and has plenty of relaxing activities in store: swimming in the pool, fragrant gardens or Indian massages. The charming rooms are all different. The great classics of French gastronomy as well as Mediterranean specialties can be savoured in one of the four restaurants of the Chèvre d’Or, where the pleasure derived from the fine cuisine is enhanced by the stunning views. After the meal, you can stroll through the cobblestone streets of the medieval cliff-side village overlooking the grandiose panorama of the sea.

restaurant.name: La Chèvre d'Or Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Tom Meyer's delicate cuisine draws on products from the foothills of Mercantour to the blue depths of the Mediterranean. The chef celebrates the culinary riches of the region and offers a fusion of sunshine-filled, aromatic and vegetal flavors that subtly integrate mineral and marine touches.

name: Château de la Gaude
description:
Château de la Gaude offers epicureans a sense of escape just ten minutes from the center of Aix-en-Provence. The Sainte-Victoire mountain seems to watch over this 18th-century estate, which features formal gardens classified as historic monuments–just like the main building. Calm and refinement reign supreme here: nature, gastronomy and wine rub shoulders with the works of contemporary artists, notably those of Philippe Pasqua–the property is also a residence for artists and regularly exhibits their works.

In the gardens, stroll along floral avenues that are a haven for butterflies, and lose yourself in the middle of a boxwood maze. The authentic charm of the property's exteriors contrasts with the minimalist decor of its rooms and suites, which are distributed between the main building and estate farmhouses. The hotel also offers more unusual stays in one of three contemporary cabins overlooking its vineyards. 

The Château de la Gaude's four restaurants include Le Art, featuring authentic Provençal cuisine, and Kaiseki, showcasing Japanese cuisine. Wine lovers can taste ‘AOC – Coteaux d’Aix-en-Provence’, estate-grown bottlings. To complete your experience, discover a spa entirely dedicated to your well-being.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible
