Breakfast Continental/English, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: Le Art Restaurant
restaurant.description: Le Art uniquely blends Provençal flavors with Japanese culinary influences, resulting in bold, seasonal cuisine that celebrates local produce. Its enchanting setting and superb terrace provide the perfect tableau for exquisitely presented, confidently created dishes, while the accompanying wine list, including bottlings from the château, is quite simply remarkable.

name: Château de la Treyne
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: The towers of this château are so close to the river that it feels as if they are floating on it. Located between the Lot, known as the “land of marvels”, and the Dordogne, home to more than one thousand châteaux, the Château de la Treyne is the ideal base from which to set off and explore this fascinating region. The Chef adapts the cuisine of the terroir, based on foie gras or lamb from the Causses du Quercy nature park. A delicious dinner can be enjoyed in the Grand Louis XIII Salon, or on the terrace overlooking the Dordogne. Breathe in pure, fresh air and look around wide-eyed to take in this extraordinary setting.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas
at least one room and common areas are accessible

restaurant.name: Château de la Treyne Restaurant
restaurant.description: Dordogne is my source of inspiration. I use the best it has to offer every day: calves suckled by their mothers, truffles, foie gras, and asparagus. My cuisine tells the stories of this region which is so important to me, while remaining consistent with the refinement of the Château de la Treyne.

name: Château de Mercuès
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: A former summer residence of the Count-Bishops of Cahors, and a true haven of peace in Occitanie, Château de Mercuès has presided over the Lot Valley since the 13th century. Its majestic keep and turrets, built on a rocky outcrop, now house guest rooms that offer breathtaking views of the estate's vineyards. No two the same, each space highlights the building's unique character, with stone walls and exposed beams featuring in one direction, and antique tapestries or parquet floors in another. The cuisine, too, celebrates the property’s rich heritage. It tells the story of Occitan terroir through a gourmet, seasonal menu, made with locally sourced, sustainable ingredients. Dishes are designed to pair with the renowned, velvety Malbecs of Château de Mercuès, so emblematic of the Cahors appellation. Walks in the surrounding countryside, wine tastings–and conversations with the winemakers themselves–will transport you to the heart of Malbec art de vivre.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost
main restaurant and common areas are accessible

restaurant.name: La Table de Mercuès Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the heart of Château de Mercuès, Chef Clément Costes creates gourmet cuisine that will delight epicureans. His dishes, influenced by the culinary culture of the Southwest, showcase ingredients such as lamb, truffle, saffron and Cahors Malbec wine. In an elegant setting, meals promise a mix of local authenticity and impressive refinement.

name: Château de Riell
description: In an unspoilt valley in French Catalonia, looking out at the snow-topped mount Canigou, you will find the Château de Riell, perched among parasol pines and oleander trees. This hotel is made for hedonists, with its baroque interiors and a restaurant whose highlights are local and respectful of nature. Sports enthusiasts can choose from the huge range of outdoor activities against a backdrop of breathtaking scenery and the open-air pool set on the castle’s ramparts. Discerning gentlemen travellers will be impressed by the ambiance of the Out of Africa bar and the dacha, where you can sit and read or enjoy breakfast. A splendid escape in Romanesque Roussillon.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: La Table du Château Restaurant
restaurant.description: A decent bottle to share, a meal cooked over a wood fire, some good bread, a view of the forest and a big smile... Good food savored over glowing coal, perhaps even some Vivaldi in the air.

name: Château de Valmer
description:
Nestled in a six-hectare estate, only 300 m from the sea and surrounded by organic vineyards and Provençal countryside, Château de Valmer is a former family guest house which has, over time, been converted into a luxury hotel.
Owners of the property since 1946, the Rocchietta-Montloin family has spent a lifetime cultivating the art of hospitality. Guests can enjoy chic and cosy rooms, tree houses, private residences, and a Michelin-starred gourmet restaurant whose cuisine is inspired by the estate's own organically grown produce.
An invitation to relax and unwind, guests can take a path lined with majestic palm trees to bask on a private beach facing the Îles d'Hyères or pamper themselves in the large heated outdoor swimming pool and splendid spa that opens out to the park.

Electric charging station at extra cost

restaurant.name: La Palmeraie Restaurant
restaurant.description: Beneath the palm trees, La Palmeraie restaurant invites you to embark on a culinary adventure through the surrounding landscape and Mediterranean coastline. Chef Thomas Pezeril creates finessed, harmonious cuisine using local produce and treasures from the property’s own vegetable garden. In an elegant setting which opens onto the gardens, each meal becomes a showcase of the Riviera.

nearestAirport: Toulon-Hyères (Intl) 45 km, Nice (Intl) 100 km, Marseille (Intl) 150 km

name: Château Lafaurie-Peyraguey Hôtel & Restaurant LALIQUE
description: It is in Bommes, in southwestern France, where the Hôtel & Restaurant LALIQUE at Château Lafaurie-Peyraguey upholds an art de vivre of unparalleled refinement. In harmony with the Gironde, a region enriched with a terroir of infinite treasures, this exceptional hotel reveals itself through an unprecedented symbiosis of worlds: Sauternes wine, crystal, gastronomy and hospitality. Located less than an hour from Bordeaux, the hotel boasts ten luxurious rooms and three suites of rare elegance that offer spectacular views over the estate’s vineyards. Enthusiasts and wine lovers alike flock here to celebrate the wonders of oenology with family and friends, pampered by the house’s wine specialists. These same connoisseurs welcome you to the gastronomic restaurant, where the decorative splendour and brilliance of the setting and service are matched only by the bewitching cuisine of renowned chef Jérôme Schilling. Hallmarks of the Hôtel & Restaurant LALIQUE at Château Lafaurie-Peyraguey, a stay here is one of peacefulness, intimacy, epicureanism and a sense of sharing.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Restaurant LALIQUE
restaurant.description: “The more the wine is rich and complex, the more combinations it offers. It involves extolling the qualities of Sauternes in precise gastronomic combinations, of bringing out its aromas, of enhancing its various colors, and of playing with its textures. My menu is designed according to the different products offered by the regional farmers, growers and producers, all rigorously selected.

name: Château Louise de La Vallière
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: A true marvel of the Loire Valley, situated between Tours and Amboise, Château Louise de La Vallière is an invitation to indulge in the delights of the senses and times of yore. The château’s owners have created a world immersed in the age of Louis XIV and thus in the history of France, both culturally and in terms of the building’s architectural heritage. This exceptional property stands proudly in a sixteen-hectare ancient forest, home to a reserve of protected animal species. Surrounded by nature and ornamental gardens, this haven of peace in the Touraine region is perfect for a stay of infinite pleasures. The celebrated architect Jacques Garcia designed the interiors. Exuding the utmost refinement, the decor brilliantly bridges the gap between past and present, and is particularly impressive in the nine rooms and eleven suites. At the gourmet restaurant L’Amphitryon, enjoy the privilege of savouring a cuisine prepared with local and seasonal products, some of which come directly from the château’s vegetable garden. During a romantic stay, why not partake in an unforgettable tasting session at the champagne bar Le Saint-Évremond or relax in the outdoor swimming pool, which is heated all year round? Combining a magnificent journey through time with a delicious caress for the senses, it is no wonder that such moments spent at Château Louise de La Vallière never cease to amaze.

restaurant.name: L´Amphitryon Restaurant
restaurant.description: The chef at L’Amphitryon offers contemporary French cuisine that draws inspiration from the splendor of Louis XIV's court but puts a modern spin on the Sun King's favorite flavors. Dishes are made with seasonal and locally sourced produce–including asparagus, pigeon, langoustine and pike-perch, served with sparkling wine–and served in a particularly theatrical setting designed by Jacques Garcia.

name: Château Monfort
description: In the heart of Milan, discover the splendour of a very unique hotel housed in an Art Nouveau building from the early 20th century. Hotel Château Monfort is inspired by the world of fairy tales and the opera. It is an enchanting place where dream and reality blend together, which is reflected in the retro-chic style that permeates this "Urban Château". The pastel colours that adorn the walls of the guest rooms are enhanced by floral patterns and butterflies. Inside the hotel, you will be captivated by its romantic and refined atmosphere, which extends to our exceptional Rubacuori restaurant, our Mezzanotte lounge bar and our "Amore & Psiche" SPA.

restaurant.name: Rubacuori Restaurant
restaurant.description: Let yourself be enchanted by the unique charm of Rubacuori-known as avoleur de cœurs, or stealer of hearts. This magical setting blends fantasy with elegance, as the chef reinterprets the classics of Milanese cuisine using market-fresh, seasonal ingredients. A delicious, romantic experience that delights the eye as much as the palate.

name: Château Neercanne
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Upholding a proud culinary tradition, Château Neercanne combines gastronomy and hospitality in an enviable setting. With breathtaking views over the bucolic Jeker valley, near Maastricht, this grand, late 17th-century property is home to an exceptional restaurant and accommodation that invites guests to savor its heritage. Located in the original manor house, each guest suite blends historical and modern elements, and offers access to the private baroque gardens, which are listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Beneath the impressive chandeliers of Restaurant Château Neercanne, diners enjoy refined seasonal cuisine, inspired by French gastronomy. The chef prioritizes local ingredients and products from the property's own kitchen garden to create innovative dishes of great delicacy. Separately, the atmospheric vaults and elegant contemporary decor of L'Auberge, the estate's second restaurant, make a memorable setting for celebrations.

restaurant.name: Château Neercanne Restaurant
restaurant.description: Over forty years of cultural and culinary tradition.

name: Château Saint-Jean
description: Just five minutes from the medieval city of Montluçon is Château Saint-Jean, located on the site of a former outpost of Templar knights. From the turrets of this fairy-tale hotel, guests overlook a mature park of century-old trees and a pond filled with swans and ducks – always ready to greet passers-by. Cozy guest rooms and suites combine modernity with touches of Renaissance style, each with breathtaking views of Saint-Jean Park. Rooted in Bourbon heritage, the soul of Auvergne is revealed in this haven of peace run by the Delion family. Treat yourself to a moment of relaxation at the spa, with its view of the adjacent racecourse, or spend some time in the bar, near the warmth of the stone fireplace, enjoying a signature creation from the head bartender. At the gourmet restaurant, housed in the chateau’s 12th-century chapel, discover the creative cuisine of chef Olivier Valade. His carefully conceived dishes wow visually and highlight the authenticity of the region’s products.

restaurant.name: La Chapelle Restaurant
restaurant.description: La Chapelle occupies a captivating setting, with a contemporary copper-domed interior that beautifully complements the chapel’s ancient stone. Chef Olivier Valade's cuisine similarly reflects a balance between tradition and modernity, reinventing local classics using seasonal ingredients and regional specialities, including Montluçon beer or even confectionary like Vichy Pastilles.

name: Château Sainte Sabine
description:
This magnificent 16th-century residence, still possessing all the elegance that its Renaissance architects so lavishly bestowed upon it, lies outside the petite village bearing its name, on 20 acres of wooded grounds, home to thirty free-roaming deer. Château Sainte Sabine is the very embodiment of Burgundian art de vivre. 
The building has retained its period woodwork, its French ceilings, and a majestic neo-Gothic fireplace. Venerable furnishings unearthed at antique shops, toile de Jouy, and prints from eras past give the rooms and suites the understated charm felt in the aristocratic homes of yesteryear. At Lassey Restaurant, which offers sublime views of Châteauneuf-en-Auxois, the chef presents authentic, refined cuisine that reveals the character of each season. These moments of wholesome fine dining enhance the pleasure of a walk through the grounds or sunbathing by the swimming pool – all making your stay at the Château de Sainte Sabine an experience of timeless tranquility.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Lassey Restaurant
restaurant.description: At the heart of the chateau, Lassey Restaurant presents a nature-inspired setting, with fabrics featuring botanical patterns and panoramic views of the grounds. Here, Chef Alexandre Ziane showcases local produce, prepared with aromatic herbs, in a trademark simple yet elegant style. Be sure to try the daily specials, which reflect in-season ingredients at their very freshest.

name: Château St. Gerlach
description: The St. Gerlach Estate is tucked away in the Geul Valley and the Ingendael nature reserve. Extensive parks, a vineyard, herb garden, orchard, rose garden, and its church with splendid 18th century frescoes, secure an enchanting setting for the Château, which dates back to the year 1200 and was once a monastery built to accommodate pilgrims who had come to pay homage to hermit Gerlach of Houthem. The guestrooms, housed in the former farmstead, boast views of Baroque gardens. The restaurant, which has lost nothing of its aristocratic décor, serves fine French cuisine, while the bistro Burgemeester Quicx offers the guests a more informal setting.

restaurant.name: Les Salons Restaurant
restaurant.chef: Chef Guido Le Bron de Vexela
restaurant.description: Serving light, regionally inspired dishes at Les Salons. Open for lunch or dinner. Formerly the living quarters of Baron de Selys de Fanson, the kitchen and dining areas have retained their original décor, adding to the chic atmosphere that is reflected in the service provided by the team. It is a place that blends nostalgia with contemporary cuisine, devised by the Chef de Cuisine and his talented crew, using ingredients that are locally sourced and harvested from the estate gardens. When weather permitting, the outdoor terrace is a lovely setting for lunch, dinner or light bites.

nearestAirport: Bruxelles 122 km, Maastricht Aachen 8 km, Liège 40 km, Eindhoven 87 km, Cologne 95 km, Amsterdam Airport Schiphol 215 km

name: Chewton Glen
description: Cheerful sage shutters and immaculate sweeping lawns welcome visitors to Chewton Glen, a refined country house hotel and spa set in 52 hectares of Hampshire countryside on the edge of the New Forest National Park.Located a short walk from the Solent coast, with fabulous views of the Isle of Wight, it is the perfect resort for a family holiday. Hidden away in a wooded valley are the hotel’s amazing Tree Houses. The Dining Room is a nexus of beautiful conservatories, intimate dining spaces and a stunning wine room, whereas The Kitchen sets the stage for casual dining and learning about food with its bakery and cookery school, a partnership with James Martin. The health centre is dedicated to personalised service and spa experiences.

restaurant.name: The Dining Room Restaurant
restaurant.description: The unique setting of the Chewton Glen Hotel, located between the New Forest National Park and the Jurassic Coast, inspires chef Simon Addison's menus at The Dining Room restaurant. By working with products mainly from the hotel’s own vegetable garden, he creates a mix of typically English and creatively cosmopolitan dishes that recall his passion for travel.

name: Clarance Hôtel
description:
The rooms open out to an enclosed garden, a small oasis of greenery where the organic herbs, fruits, and vegetables that supply the restaurant are grown. Stunningly, this is in the heart of Old Lille, in an 18th century private mansion with beautiful classic lines.
Protectors of an undeniable charm, ceilings, floors, and antique woodwork have been conserved and are boldly married with the contemporary decors of the rooms, as well as works of art scattered throughout the hotel: it was indeed designed to be a genuine cultural space. At the restaurant, the chef produces a creative and "astounding" cuisine emphasizing local and seasonal products.

restaurant.name: La Table Restaurant
restaurant.description: At gourmet restaurant La Table, chef Gaëtan Citerne offers creative cuisine that brings to life the freshness of seasonal products and which represent a distinct culinary inventiveness. Savor the flavors in rooms decorated with traditional woodwork, in the intimacy of the library or on the terrace which overlooks adjacent parkland.

name: Cliveden House
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Cliveden House is renowned for its magnificent 152-hectare estate overlooking the River Thames. Behind its imposing period façade, the 17th-century stately home boasts interiors brimming with English charm. Exquisite antique furniture and paintings contribute to the warm and intimate ambience. Its historical guests have lent their names to the individual rooms, such as Winston Churchill, Charlie Chaplin and Rudyard Kipling. Just outside London and a stone’s throw from Windsor, Ascot and Heathrow, Cliveden House offers fine dining and a promise of relaxation at its beautiful spa, a tranquil oasis hidden away behind the mellow brick walls of the garden. The stunning three-bedroom cottage set on the banks of the river is perfect for families, and a scenic cruise on one of the lovingly restored vintage launches is not to be missed.

restaurant.name: The Cliveden Dining Room Restaurant
restaurant.description: The River Thames and the National Trust gardens that surround Cliveden House's bright dining room lend it a romantic touch. It is the perfect setting to taste the modern and refined French cuisine of chef Chris Hannon. His dishes and menus reveals his passion for fresh and local products.

name: Coquillade Provence
description: Coquillade Provence hotel overlooks something of a living painting–a tableau of vineyards, olive trees and lavender fields amid the gentle hills of the Luberon. This charming Provencal hamlet, with its ochre facades, tiled rooftops and panoramic views of Mont Ventoux, perfectly embodies the soul of the region. Guest rooms–some decorated in an authentic style, others in a more contemporary way–blend in wonderfully with this green setting. In the kitchen, the chef showcases produce from his own vegetable garden alongside local products. His colorful plates reflect the region, which is easy to explore. Several cycle tracks are accessible from the hotel, allowing you to crisscross the lavender fields to the sound of cicadas. Back at the property, its azure swimming pool and 21,500 square foot spa promise intense relaxation, making every stay an enchanting interlude.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Les Vignes & Son Jardin
restaurant.description: At Les Vignes & Son Jardin, cuisine follows the rhythm of the seasons. Locally sourced meats and fish are grilled over glowing embers, and served with herbs and vegetables from the kitchen garden. In warmer weather, the Jardin dans les Vignes offers an idyllic outdoor setting to soak up the charm of Provence.

name: Da Vittorio
description: Nestled amid rolling hills that separate the bustling energy of Milan from the majestic landscapes around the lakes of Lombardy, lies the Da Vittorio residence. Just a stone's throw from Bergamo, the Cerea family warmly welcomes guests to an authentic property in the heart of the countryside. A true haven of peace, the property consists of a 24-acre/10-hectare estate ideal for outdoor pursuits and a villa imbued with a romantic elegance. This atmosphere also extends to guest rooms, whose windows offer bucolic views over  surrounding nature, creating enchanting spaces in which to relax. In the gourmet restaurant, the Cerea brothers, Enrico and Roberto, perpetuate their father's culinary legacy. Da Vittorio restaurant, now well-known in international gastronomic circles, is a showcase of flavor and creativity, where each dish can be paired with one of a thousand vintages from its exceptional cellar.

restaurant.name: Da Vittorio Restaurant
restaurant.description: Our mission is to make every guest feel like royalty and to offer professional service, the best possible cuisine, and, above all, make sure they feel at home.

name: Diepeschrather Mühle
description: A mere half-hour’s drive from Cologne, set in a verdant landscape, the Diepeschrather Mühle hotel is the refined inn of Chef-Owner Nelson Müller. It is a veritable sanctuary offering a delicious, relaxing gourmet getaway in the North Rhine-Westphalia countryside. Refresh body and soul on revitalizing strolls and hikes or admire the half-timbered houses of Bergisch Gladbach. The rooms and suites feature contemporary interiors and lovely terraces looking out onto the forest or the outdoor swimming pool. The gourmet restaurant is where the chef-owner can give free rein to his passion for creative, primarily plant-based cuisine made with in-season ingredients, along with a restaurant featuring the farm-to-table concept. The Diepeschrather Mühle hotel is perfect for a serene break enlivened by superb cuisine in both the restaurants and the cooking classes given by the chef himself.

restaurant.name: Schote Restaurant
restaurant.description: Schote, the gourmet restaurant of Diepeschrather Mühle hotel, invites diners to embark on a journey of taste. Innovative dishes are inspired by the local terroir and conceived with passion using carefully selected ingredients. Enjoy this creative immersion in the culinary universe of talented chef Nelson Müller.

name: Domaine d’Auriac
description: The Domaine d’Auriac is built on the ancient Carsac oppidum, where the story of Carcassonne, a Unesco World Heritage site, begins. For three generations, the Rigaudis Family has reinvented this 19th-century townhouse, built over the cellars of a Carolingian Abbey – ruled over by calm and fine dining in the shade of the 300-year-old surrounding park. The restaurant at the Domaine serves the original recipe for the typical local “Dieu le Fils” cassoulet. In a region where history, mysteries and legends, such as the story of Abbot Saunière’s treasure in Rennes-le-Château, abound, discover Cathare abbeys and castles whose ruins rise from cliff tops and rocky promontories.

restaurant.name: Le restaurant Bernard Rigaudis
restaurant.description: Taste the authenticity of the local terroir in the elegant decor of the Bernard Rigaudis restaurant, dining from terraces facing the Domaine d'Auriac golf course. Chef Philippe Deschamps creates a regional cuisine that pays homage to tradition but which is adorned with contemporary touches.

name: Domaine de Châteauvieux
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: At the tender age of just seven years old, Chef Philippe Chevrier already knew he would become a chef, having inherited a passion for cooking from his mother and grandmother, who prepared regional, family-style, hearty cuisine. He realized a dream when he became the owner of Domaine de Châteauvieux in 1986–ever since, his proprietorship has been an excellent reason to stop in the heart of Satigny, the largest wine-growing region in Switzerland, just a few kilometers outside Geneva. Philippe Chevrier surrounds himself with a talented team, including chef François Periers and pastry chef Manuel Pereira, who work with the finest ingredients. Amid a beautiful natural setting, Domaine de Châteauvieux is an exceptional destination for curious epicureans in search of new discoveries and authenticity. This former winegrower's farm, which dates to the 16th century, also offers guest rooms that combine timeless charm with contemporary touches.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas
Breakfast Buffet for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Domaine de Châteauvieux Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Domaine de Châteauvieux, culinary expertise is shared with joy. Chef Philippe Chevrier upholds an exacting and refined approach to gastronomy, supported by François Periers in the kitchen and pastry chef Manuel Pereira. Breton lobster, Vaucluse truffles, Simmental beef: examples of exceptional produce served in impeccable style.

name: Domaine de Fontenille
description:
In the heart of the Luberon Regional Nature Park, stop and enjoy a break at Domaine de Fontenille. Worthy of a novel by Giono or Pagnol, this mansion wrapped in cedars and century-old olive trees is a celebration of the charm and magnetism of the Provençal countryside, the perfect setting to experience French art de vivre.
Elegantly combining period details and craftsmanship, the property’s decor draws inspiration as much from family homes full of keepsakes as from the contemporary art centre in its former cellar — original works adorn the nineteen rooms and suites overlooking the park, which include an old wash house converted into a romantic little den. Le Champ des Lunes, the Michelin-starred restaurant of chef Michel Marini, adds to the magic of this place, as do the spa and the centuries-old vineyard and kitchen garden that are open to the public.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas

restaurant.name: La Cuisine d'Amélie Restaurant
restaurant.description: Located on the southern terraces of the bastide, the restaurant La Cuisine d'Amélie offers a simple but inventive cuisine based on the foremost products of the region. The menu, which is constantly evolving, is based on the seasons.

name: Domaine de la Bretesche Golf & Spa
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: The boundless charm of this estate works its magic as soon as you cross the gates. A 15th century castle nestles in the heart of magnificent parkland with ancient trees and outbuildings housing the guest rooms. Each of the rooms boasts an original décor and they all exude warmth, authenticity and sophistication: also the hallmark of the Chef’s inventive cuisine at Le Club, and of the décor of the bar in the former stable block which has preserved the ancient marble feed and water troughs. The Cour Carrée spa is a perfect haven of peace offering well-being and peace of mind and the 18-hole golf course, designed by Henry Cotton, provides superb views over the castle.

restaurant.name: Le Club Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a relaxed atmosphere, the Club offers great classics of brasserie-style cuisine and original creations, with a different menu every day. In the summer, twice a week weather permitting, meats barbecued on the grill or cooked “a la plancha are also on the menu. A rotisserie installed on the terrace delivers juicy and deliciously browned poultry.

name: Domaine de Locguénolé & Spa
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Locguénolé is located next to the dark waters of a sea inlet in the heart of a land of legends. The Château and the small Manor House overlook a wide estuary where the light and landscape change with the tides. These buildings from the 18th and early 19th centuries are set in wooded parkland and have remained in the hands of the same family for centuries. They boast a combination of modern comfort and tradition with classical décor and period furniture. The creative cuisine is based on a subtle pairing of ingredients from the Morbihan terroir and the ocean. A private pontoon permits sailors travelling up the Blavet from Lorient harbour to dock at this delightful haven.

restaurant.name: L'Inattendu Restaurant
restaurant.description: The cuisine at l'Inattendu is as surprising as it is subtle and creative. While ocean flavors take the lead in its seasonally conceived dishes, the chef also incorporates produce from across the Breton region. The dessert menu is just as inspired, offering classics as well as house signature creations.

name: Domaine de Primard
description: Just an hour away from Paris, the Domaine de Primard beckons you to the heart of its 40-hectare grounds. Its gardens, designed by Jacques Wirtz, are home to over 250 varieties of rose and a collection of rare trees. Highland cows, Ouessant sheep, ducks and geese fill the landscape with life. Domaine de Primard offers two additional dining experiences: Le Bistrot Martin and La Table d'Hôtes. The kitchens are stocked by Domaine's permaculture vegetable garden. Meanwhile, the spa offers—for the first time in France—the natural spa treatments developed by Susanne Kaufmann.
services: In at least one room and common areas

restaurant.name: Bistrot Martin Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Primard, the cuisine elevates the authentic pastoral atmosphere of the venue for a unique experience every time. The cuisine at Domaine de Primard is seasonal and comes from the best local producers as part of a sustainable approach. The fruits and vegetables come in part from Domaine's organic kitchen garden.

name: Domaine de Rochevilaine
description: Upon discovering Domaine de Rochevilaine, you will be struck by emotion coupled with hesitation over what to admire most: the beauty of the site or the quality of the stones. Passing by the flowered gardens, the typically Breton setting of the property will seduce you as you stand with your feet in the water, with only the ocean in your sights. Enjoy a walk along the Sentier des Douaniers, then savour a bite of the local lobster — the best in the world. Listen to the sound of the waves and indulge in a treatment at the most renowned and oldest balneotherapy and marine spa centre in Brittany, the brainchild of owner Bertrand Jaquet.
services: In at least one room at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Domaine de Rochevilaine Restaurant
restaurant.description: With the encouragement of Bertrand Jaquet, the owner and a chef in his own right, we have defined Rochevilaine Cuisine as a reflection of the extraordinary richness of Brittany's products. This is a kind of cookery beyond trends, without contrivance: we simply wish to express our values through the cuisine that we share with you.

name: Domaine de Rymska & Spa
description: In the heart of Burgundy, thirty kilometres from the lovely medieval city of Beaune, Domaine de Rymska & Spa is the ideal setting for a relaxing stay in the countryside. Beautifully refurbished by local craftsmen, the ten rooms and suites of this intimate residence inspire reverie: their blend of noble materials and soft tones create an exquisite frame for the views over the estate’s ponds and greenery. A natural landscape as generous as it is soulful, you need only to look towards the meadows for a glimpse of the majestic thoroughbred horses from the property’s stud farm to feel a sense of awe. At La Table de Rymska, many of the ingredients that enrich the restaurant’s sophisticated traditional cuisine come from the estate’s livestock farm, vegetable garden and orchard. Moreover, the wine cellar is a tribute to the region, whose famed wine estates can be explored by bicycle along lovely tree-lined paths.

restaurant.name: La Table de Rymska Restaurant
restaurant.description: Benefiting from a warm, cozy setting with exposed stone walls, Burgundy-style ceilings, open fireplaces and immense terraces to enjoy beautiful days and peaceful evenings, La Table de Rymska is definitely a restaurant not to be missed when visiting the region. With its bold local cuisine, it proves to be an exceptional place. Quality products, mostly from the estate (Charolais, Aubrac & Wagyu beef, poultry, eggs, and freshly picked produce from the vegetable garden and orchard), are refined with elegance by the chef and enhanced by the sommelier, who will offer you a wide selection of wines from Burgundy and elsewhere. Both strive to masterfully redefine the perfect match between food and wine.

name: Domaine Le Mouflon d'Or
description: It is Corsica’s inland, the realm of maquis and mountains, that guests at the Relais & Châteaux Domaine Le Mouflon d’Or will experience. This estate of nearly 50 acres, deep in the Alta Rocca region, is home to an early-20th-century aristocratic residence standing grandly among Lebanon cedars and ancient chestnuts. It was painstakingly renovated to preserve its Belle Époque charm, enhancing it with contemporary elegance. Travelers can relish the comforts of the main building with its view of the Bavella Needles or embrace the natural environs by staying in one of the estate’s two converted sheepfolds. With a restaurant that serves Mediterranean cuisine composed by Sébastien Chauchat, Le Domaine Le Mouflon d’Or offers another delicious way to experience genuine Corsican hospitality. Those who love hiking, mountain biking, and canyoning will thrill to the many opportunities that await in the hotel’s surroundings, including the celebrated GR20 cross-Corsica hiking trail, and can relax afterwards in the heated outdoor pool.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: A Nepita Restaurant
restaurant.description: Like the hotel itself, which sits amid the dense vegetation of the Alta Rocca, restaurant A Nepita is an ode to Corsican nature and its produce. Chef Sébastien Chauchat deliberately limits his culinary compositions to three or four ingredients each, in order to preserve the pure taste of produce harvested from his vegetable garden.

name: Domaine Les Crayères
description: Les Crayères is a mythical property in the heart of Reims, nestled discreetly in a seven hectare park full of lush vegetation. This former home of the de Polignac family permanently recreates and modernises the “chateau style” in an atmosphere which is one of elegant sophistication. Perfectly appointed rooms and suites await with a love of detail which is characteristic for pure classical French style. The warm welcome, impeccable service and the gourmet restaurant with its wine list featuring more than 600 champagne labels, make this the ideal place to start discovering all the treasures of this region.

restaurant.name: Le restaurant gastronomique « Le Parc** »
restaurant.description: Le Parc restaurant cultivates a decidedly French art de vivre. Exceptional ingredients, finesse and flavors combine in subtle, refined dishes conceived by Chef Christophe Moret, all set against a sumptuous backdrop. In the heart of a century-old garden, expect a timeless gastronomic experience.

name: Don Alfonso 1890
description: On the stunning headland where the gulf of Naples meets the gulf of Salerno, Alfonso and Ernesto Iaccarino create dishes that reflect his love for this slice of Italy dotted with lemon groves, vineyards and olive trees. Using fresh produce from his own farm, Le Peracciole, Iaccarino creates simple Mediterranean marvels, such as swordfish with chickpeas and thyme, goatfish with rosemary and cucumber, and pasta with clams and courgettes. His wife and two sons give a family touch to the ambience and the combination of exceptional produce, warm hospitality and Iaccarino’s skills make the difference.
services: Visit of winery on the Amalfi coast

restaurant.name: Don Alfonso 1890 Restaurant
restaurant.description: Don Alfonso 1890 embodies a warm, refined style of hospitality. Under Chefs Don Alfonso and Ernesto Iaccarino, the cuisine draws on the generous terroir of Campania with produce from the estate's own organic farm. Its dishes are heartfelt and flavorful, nurtured with a spirit of Italian conviviality that’s been passed down from generation to generation.

name: Dragsholm Slot
description:
For more than 800 years, Dragsholm Slot, a Danish castle that has weathered the test of time, has stood bathed in the exceptional light of the Odsherred Peninsula. It is surrounded by the shallow bays, woods and fields of a Unesco Geopark.
The impressive landscape was created during the ice age, with lateral moraines and rolling hills surrounded by Denmark's finest beaches and criss-crossed by paths that provide breathtaking views.
Inside the castle, discover an ambience of warmth and authenticity, an invitation to continue the experience in the gourmet restaurant. Under the wide, white stone arches, Nordic gourmet cuisine holds court, fostered by the area's exquisite, acclaimed produce.
services: In at least one room at extra cost

restaurant.name: Dragsholm Slot Gourmet Restaurant
restaurant.description: Nature, as it unfolds in there here and now, rules our gastronomy. Presence, contemplation and pleasure - in flavours created by nature, by the Ice Age and the castle baron's reclamation of the Lammefjord. Right here in his kitchen. It is presence, honest and pertinent.

name: Dyvig Badehotel
description: On the island of Als, in southern Denmark, discover Sønderborg — a quaint harbour town where you are sure to fall instantly in love with Dyvig Badehotel. This elegant boutique hotel located on the waterfront is in perfect harmony with the surrounding countryside and the waters of the Baltic Sea, revealing its charm through its 19th century building which itself is a beautiful reflection of the local traditional architecture. Within this timeless and relaxed setting, the seventeen rooms and four spacious suites engage in a sophisticated interplay of original decor, antique furniture and modern elements. An authentic haven of peace, Dyvig Badehotel is the ideal place for a break in which you can find serenity, and appreciate the beauty and wonders of this unique region of Denmark. This includes the culinary delights of chefs John Bech Amstrup and Christian Lebech, which can be enjoyed in the gourmet restaurant or brasserie. Their cuisine is both contemporary and classic, incorporating the best local produce — especially from the sea — in a seasonal menu that will leave you with memories you will soon not forget.
services: Individual cooking classes given by the chef

restaurant.name: Restaurant Amstrup&Vigen
restaurant.chef: Chef John Bech Amstrup & Christian Lebech
restaurant.description: At Amstrup & Vigen, taste is top priority. John Bech Amstrup and Christian Lebech craft dishes with precision and passion, texture and contrast, using ingredients from the Danish island of Als. Sea herbs, smoked fish, and root vegetables blend into straightforward, down-to-earth culinary creations in which Danish tradition is always refined, but never minimized.

name: Echaurren
description: In the north of Spain, in the magnificent region of La Rioja, renowned for its wines, the picturesque village of Ezcaray is the lovely setting in which the Echaurren Hotel is nestled. José Félix and Francis Paniego preside over the destiny of this prestigious gem whose reputation goes back five generations. With its warm and friendly atmosphere, this charming hotel, featuring bright rooms and suites designed with a modern touch, provides the ideal setting for enjoying truly unforgettable moments. Explore the surrounding countryside and savour one of the region’s fabulous wines. A visit to the impressive Santa María la Mayor church, located opposite the hotel, with its famous Spanish-Flemish altarpiece, is a must. Also not to be missed is the gourmet restaurant El Portal de Echaurren and its delicious promise of a feast for the taste buds. Delight in sampling the emblematic products of the region, exalted by the avant-garde cuisine of renowned chef Francis Paniego. It is a phenomenal experience enhanced all the more by the exceptional and ever-evolving wine list of master sommelier José Félix Paniego.

restaurant.name: El Portal Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Francis Paniego draws inspiration from the landscapes, flavors and traditions of Rioja. The fertile orchards of the Ebro river, the vineyards, locally grown produce, specialty meats, tapas and pastries combine in modern gastronomy that’s closely connected to its surroundings.

name: Eliamos Villas Hotel & Spa
description: Eliamos Villas Hotel & Spa resembles a tranquil oasis on its perch above a secluded beach, on the southern coast of Kefalonia. With its elegant private villas, this contemporary establishment – blending with the surrounding olive trees and island scrub that fill the air with their fragrance – pays homage to the spirit of Mediterranean architecture. Using some local stone on some, the villas feature a subdued color palette and natural materials, such as wood and terracotta tile. Each also has a private saltwater swimming pool or jacuzzi and looks out onto the blue waters of the Ionian Sea. In the restaurant, the classics of Mediterranean cuisine are revisited with locally sourced, organic ingredients. Lastly, the Eliamos experience makes well-being a priority, offering yoga, Reformer Pilates, and nature-inspired massages.

restaurant.name: Olais Restaurant
restaurant.description: The fresh and healthy cuisine of Olais restaurant is a delicious tribute to the colors and aromas of the Mediterranean. The chef reinterprets the culinary traditions of Kefalonia, celebrating the terroir in contemporary dishes using the finest local, seasonal ingredients.

name: Elounda Mare Hotel
description: On the native island of Zeus, the King of the Greek gods, sits the Elounda Mare Hotel, with its white cottages that look out onto the turquoise waters of the Mirabello Bay. An ethereal light bathes this retreat and its maze of sweet-smelling gardens. The spacious and intimate bedrooms, the bungalows, all different but all with a private pool, enjoy magnificent panoramic views. The hotel’s location is the perfect place from which to discover Crete and its legends, monasteries and chapels bequeathed by the Byzantine civilization. There is even a chapel overlooking the hotel’s private bay, offering stunning vistas of the Mediterranean.

restaurant.name: Old Mill Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a paradisiac and scented garden, next to the crystal clear sea, the Old Mill blends the unadorned traditional Cretan cuisine, based on countless fresh and healthy products, with the imagination of our dedicated young team, exuding dear memories and endless fragrances.

name: Epoque Hotel
description: Imbued with charm and personality, central Bucharest's Epoque Hotel offers a peaceful retreat in the city centre. Close to Cișmigiu Park, Romanian Athenaeum and the opera house, Epoque Hotel displays a French neo-classical facade; its warm décor inspired by the local history and heritage. At the intimate and stylish L’Atelier, the chef creates his exceptional signature cuisine using local and seasonal produce.

restaurant.name: L’ATELIER Restaurant
restaurant.description: Our culinary team invite our guests to an exploration of the Balkan culinary tradition, on a quest to rediscover the ancestral wisdom of local food and tastes. The menu of L'Atelier represents an ode to Balkan culinary traditions, seasonality, and authenticity.

nearestStation: Bucharest North Main Railway Station 2 km

name: Falsled Kro
description: As you cross the threshold of this traditional thatched-roofed house on the island of Fyn, the birthplace of Hans Christian Andersen’s fairy tales, you will experience the simple pleasures of Scandinavian living. The team at Falsled Kro offers guests the warmest of welcomes–just one encouragement to live in the moment and appreciate its cozy charms. Amid whitewashed walls, and surrounded by antique liquor cabinets, you will also be enchanted by the scent of freshly baked pastries, served throughout the day, drifting from a wood-fired oven. The generously decorated rooms feel like true cocoons and invite families to share a moment together and couples to cuddle up. A charming hotel, set in a verdant, natural environment, the Falsled Kro is also renowned for its seasonal cuisine, enhanced by fresh herbs from its own vegetable garden.
services: Breakfast Continental/English, À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Falsled Kro Restaurant
restaurant.description: Under the thatched roof of this historic inn, nature guides every culinary decision. Chef Simon Juel Petersen picks, prunes and simmers: his inspiration begins in the vegetable garden, then extends to the kitchen, where it is paired with ingredients from across the island of Funen. At Falsled Kro, Nordic tradition and French elegance combine in effortless harmony.

name: Farlam Hall Hotel & Restaurant
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: In the heart of picturesque Cumbria, in the northwest of England, Farlam Hall is a haven of peace bordered by ancient trees, many of which are reflected in the estate's ponds. This discreet yet elegant country manor will delight nature lovers and city dwellers looking for a recuperative countryside break far from the frenzy of modern life. The area is a renowned walker's paradise, with the magnificent North Pennines mountain range and Hadrian's Wall just a short drive away. The park and gardens of Farlam Hall reveal a unique beauty, season after season, while the cozy and warm atmosphere of its shared spaces invite you to forget the passage of time. Decorated with typically English furniture, its bright rooms and suites invite relaxation and rest. In Cedar Tree Restaurant, the chef prioritizes locally sourced, seasonal produce, some of which is grown directly in the property’s own kitchen gardens, to deliver creative and delicious cuisine.

restaurant.name: Cedar Tree by Hrishikesh Desai
restaurant.description: In the radiant, relaxing atmosphere of Cedar Tree by Hrishikesh Desai, the chef creates reinterprets traditional cuisine with contemporary inspiration and modern techniques. With views over the gardens of Farlam Hall Hotel, expect seasonal menus featuring local and regional produce.

name: Faustino Gran
description: Uniting four sumptuous palaces in the historic centre of Ciutadella, as well as 15 hectares of pine forests and a wealth of culture by the sea, Faustino Gran invites you to enjoy a unique experience in Menorca, the wildest of the Balearic Islands. Surrounded by translucent turquoise waters and breathtaking Mediterranean scenery, this exceptional estate displays an unparalleled charm. Delicate decorative and architectural nuances are found throughout its rooms and suites, and the spacious villa, revealing the rich history of the property. At the 17th-century Can Faustino palace, the gourmet restaurant offers cuisine that values nature and local products. Nearby, the Cal Bisbe, Can Llorenç and Can Sebastià palaces benefit from incredible views of the majestic Ciutadella de Menorca Cathedral – and an ambience of complete tranquility. With several pools and sun terraces as well as a spa featuring an indoor pool, gym, sauna and hammam tucked away in an ancient grotto, Faustino Gran is something of a dream getaway.

restaurant.name: Restaurante Faustino
restaurant.description: Local, plant-based and a fusion of flavors: three ways to sum up the delicious cuisine of Restaurante Faustino at the Faustino Gran Hotel. Here, chef Matías Salvia, gives pride of place to Menorcan terroir and sun-drenched products from the hotel's ecological garden.

name: Finca La Donaira
description:
Not far from the Route of the White Villages, the pride of southern Andalusia, Finca La Donaira is perched above the Serranía de Ronda, deep in farming country spanning more than 1,700 acres. A landscape of meadows, ancient oak forests, and Roman and Arab ruins – all creating an atmosphere of contemplative serenity. 
Designed as a holistic, environmentally friendly haven, Finca La Donaira features a farmhouse and outbuildings, carefully renovated with natural materials, such as adobe, wood and stone, while preserving the original beams and frames. In the rooms and suites with whitewashed walls, the polished floors are brightened with colorful, rustic rugs. Every room and suite of this 111 year old “Cortijo” has been uniquely designed, furnished with rare antiques, custom pieces, and original artwork. Finca La Donaira offers a broad variety of experiences: cuisine crafted with ingredients from the estate’s organic farm, relaxation at the spa, yoga classes, and horseback riding with the property’s Lusitanian horses.

restaurant.name: La Donaira Restaurant
restaurant.chef: Chef Nerea Ortiz de Urbina & Manuel Vargas
restaurant.description: Our chefs prepare produce grown biodynamically on the farm or which has been carefully selected from local producers. A stay at La Donaira means you will enjoy Andalusian specialties accompanied by a rich selection of local and international wines, for lunches and dinners.

name: Fleur de Loire
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: This imposing 17th-century building, which stands on the left bank of the river in Blois, is home to the Fleur de Loire hotel. This welcoming property, designed in an eco-responsible way, is a true haven of tranquility. Just two hours from Paris, it is the ideal starting point to explore the region and the châteaux of the Loire in particular. At his gourmet restaurant and at the Amour Blanc restaurant, Chef Christophe Hay shares his passion for regional local products, which he serves with fruit and vegetables grown in his large vegetable garden. Guest rooms in the two wings of the building evoke the gentle way of life and verdant environment of the Loire Valley. Its contemporary decor has been inspired by the tones of the slow-moving river and its sandbanks. As well as the pleasures of the table, Fleur de Loire also offers profound relaxation in the serenity of its well-being area, with its indoor pool and signature plant-based aromatic treatments.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Restaurant Christophe HAY
restaurant.description: As a beacon of free culinary expression, the gourmet restaurant of the Fleur de Loire hotel sees chef Christophe Hay create local cuisine that manifests the emotional power of the Loire Valley. Guided by the cycle of nature, his dishes are prepared using herbs and vegetables grown in permaculture in the vegetable garden.

name: Flocons de Sel
description: The hotel’s exceptional natural setting on the heights of Megève, the intimacy of the spa, the spirit of the cuisine, the creativity of the chef and the quality of the service unite to make this a truly unique place for a romantic dinner, a lovely lunch with friends or a relaxing and cosy getaway. At the restaurant, three- Michelin-star chef Emmanuel Renaut, a Meilleur Ouvrier de France winner, delivers a cuisine as subtle as it is inventive, which can be paired with any of the many vintages on the wine list. Enjoy as the finesse of the ingredients, the technical mastery of the chef and the interplay of textures marry with delicate seasonings to create a masterpiece.

restaurant.name: Flocons de Sel Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Emmanuel Renaut's 'culinary laboratory,' Flocons de Sel, tells the story of the mountains through flavors drawn from lakes, meadows and the forest floor. His unique savoir-faire means each dish explores the terrain, the flavors of the season or a particular view over the landscape with astonishing delicacy and finesse. Enjoyed in the dining room or on the terrace, every meal feels in complete harmony with the surrounding peaks.

name: Fontenille Menorca - Torre Vella
description:
On the island of Menorca, owners Frédéric Biousse and Guillaume Foucher have embarked on a beautiful adventure by resurrecting two traditional Minorcan farmhouses from their ashes, which today compose the Fontenille Menorca. Nestled amidst a vineyard, aromatic plants, olive and citrus groves, and an organic permaculture garden, Torre Vella and Santa Ponsa, its sister property located a few kilometres away, now welcome visitors in search of authenticity.
Decorated with natural materials in shades reminiscent of linen, sandstone and slate, the rooms and suites maintain their cool freshness, while the Mediterranean sun invites you to take refuge under the palm trees that line the pool and stand tall against the azure blue sky. With the sea as its backdrop, this beautiful setting is exactly what you have been yearning for.

restaurant.name: Siempreviva Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Torre Vella, guests who stay in our agrotourism accommodations can order lunch or dinner from a menu renewed each day. The restaurant Siempreviva features “a la plancha cooking, vegetables, and especially shared dishes with an original twist, such as paella, baked fish, and barbecue dishes, all within the intimate atmosphere of the main house's patio.

name: Fortaleza do Guincho
description: Along with the Cabo da Roca lighthouse Fortaleza do Guincho is the most westerly point in Europe. Atlantic waves crash against the rocks at the foot of this impressive building. At the entrance, two cannons turned towards the ocean remind you that you are entering an ancient fortress. This is a building of character and immense charm, with preserved 17th century interiors, delicious cuisine and the beach just two steps away. Try water sports, play golf, or go to the casino in Estoril. For vibrant culture and nightlife, Lisbon is just 30 km away.

restaurant.name: Fortaleza do Guincho Restaurant
restaurant.description: The dining experience at Fortaleza do Guincho continues to be a discovery of Portuguese culture, traditions, and history. Fortaleza do Guincho's philosophy is inspired by the Atlantic Ocean, which is reflected in the hotel's decor. The best fish and seafood from the Portuguese coast, along with the highest-quality local and national products, are ceaselessly extolled in every dish throughout each season.

name: Gallia Palace Beach - Golf - Spa - Resort
description: This elegant property was the first Italian hotel to join Relais & Châteaux in 1973, and the Gallia-Ansaldi family has been welcoming guests for three generations. On the fine sandy private beach, in front of the island of Elba, La Pagoda restaurant reigns supreme, thanks to its unique architecture and spectacular sea views. Completely relax in the Turkish baths, pool or wellness centre and gym. Golfers will enjoy the 18-hole golf course, set in a pine forest that is over hundred years old. A private yacht awaits for memorable excursions to the Tuscan archipelago.

restaurant.name: La Terrazza Restaurant
restaurant.description: Facing the garden of the Gallia Palace Beach hotel, La Terrazza restaurant invites guests to experience a collection of unique taste sensations. Through creative dishes, chef Fabrizio Reffo conveys the culinary traditions of the property and his personal passion, in cuisine that celebrates the purity and flavor of local products.

name: Gardena Grödnerhof Hotel & Spa
description: Gardena Grödnerhof is a haven of well-being in the heart of the Dolomites, where the legendary Monti Pallidi (Pale Mountains) take on magnificent hues of pink in the light of the setting sun. It is a paradise for mountaineers and the perfect destination for skiers in winter. To experience the fabulous natural environment and stunning views of the majestic Dolomites, the hotel offers guided tours. After a day spent in the open air, there is nothing better than returning to its pleasant atmosphere. Gardena Grödnerhof, while known for its fine cuisine, also boasts a superb new spa featuring a beauty and fitness centre. In the evening, relax with a drink at the bar and linger by the lounge’s warm fireplace.

restaurant.name: Anna Stuben Restaurant
restaurant.description: The modern style and warm welcome of this chalet sets the tone at Anna Stuben restaurant, where Chef Reimund Brunner offers creative gourmet cuisine rooted in the South Tyrolean region. Tasting menus invite you to rediscover forgotten ingredients, complemented by inspired wine pairings.

name: Georges Blanc Parc & Spa
description: In Vonnas, Georges Blanc, emblematic Chef and visionary businessman, has developed a veritable Gourmet Village on the river bank, around a reconstruction of the inn of his great grandparents. Together with his family, Blanc constantly reinvents Bresse specialties through creations such as the “crêpe vonnassienne” with salmon and caviar or the legendary Bresse chicken with foie gras. Visits are available upon request to one of the five most exceptional cellars in the world, with more than 135,000 bottles. In just under five hectares of grounds, discover a heavenly spa, an aquatic space next to a pond and a large, landscaped park that is illuminated at night.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: Restaurant Gastronomique Georges BLANC
restaurant.description: My pleasure and passion is not just good food  it is also practising the true art of entertaining and authentic conviviality.

name: Gidleigh Park
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Surrounded by more than 100 acres of pristine Devonshire countryside, Gidleigh Park is a secluded country house hotel on the edge of Dartmoor. Set majestically on the banks of the River Teign, it offers a tranquil escape with scenic walks, lawn games and a celebrated kitchen garden. Interiors draw from the Arts and Crafts movement, enriched by antiques and artwork, and most rooms, each thoughtfully appointed, overlook the river and grounds. With understated hospitality and an atmosphere of profound serenity, Gidleigh Park feels like a cherished novel come to life. Menus celebrate the finest seasonal ingredients with clarity and finesse, making every meal a sensory experience. A timeless haven for those seeking nature, stillness, and the enchantment of elsewhere.

Breakfast Buffet, Continental/English, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: Gidleigh Park Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the elegant, intimate setting of the Gidleigh Park restaurant, Head Chef Ian Webber presents refined, seasonal cuisine inspired by the rich bounty of the West Country. With produce sourced from trusted local suppliers and the hotel's own kitchen garden, Ian's dishes are rooted in sustainability and crafted with a clear focus on flavor.

name: Gilpin Hotel & Lake House
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: In an extraordinary place, the Lake District National Park, you will find Gilpin. With two charming luxury country houses on two separate estates, this very passionate family and team are dedicated to creating lasting memories for their guests, who return time and again for special celebrations and short getaways.At Gilpin Hotel, all the rooms have lovely views, most leading onto the gardens, many with their own hot tubs and private ensuite spas. Just a mile away at Gilpin Lake House, six beautiful rooms enjoy exclusive access to 40 private hectares with a lake, boathouse, pool, hot tubs and saunas; and the stunning Spa Space at Gilpin Hotel boasts its own bespoke organic products.
services: Breakfast Continental/English, À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: SOURCE at Gilpin Hotel Restaurant
restaurant.description: SOURCE at Gilpin Hotel draws inspiration from the pristine nature of the Lake District to create well-defined, contemporary cuisine. Chef Ollie Bridgwater composes dishes using ingredients cultivated in the estate's own gardens or by local artisan producers. A refined culinary experience, in harmony with the wild landscapes of the English Northwest.

name: Glenapp Castle
description: High above the dramatic coastline of southwest Scotland, overlooking the Irish Sea and the iconic Ailsa Craig Island, sits Glenapp Castle. This baronial castle is hidden from the rugged surrounding landscape by 110 acres of magical parkland including an Italian garden designed by the renowned horticulturalist Gertrude Jekyll. Underneath its fairytale towers and turrets are imposing rooms with rich fabrics, art and antiques echoing the splendour of a bygone era. With award-winning international cuisine, Glenapp Castle is the ideal base for country sports and world-class golf. Glenapp’s all-weather boat allows guests to explore the surrounding Scottish isles and whisky distilleries, or to fish and observe the spectacular wildlife.

restaurant.name: Glenapp Castle Restaurant
restaurant.description: Dining in the refined surroundings of the castle's dining rooms is a highlight of a stay at Glenapp Castle Hotel. The stylish and inventive cuisine of chef Peter Howarth reveals its authenticity through three- or seven-course menus. Enjoy dinner while watching the sun set over Ailsa Craig, the Mull of Kintyre and the Ayrshire coast.

name: Gran Hotel Mas d'en Bruno
description: Nestled in the heart of the Priorat region’s vineyards, less than 90 miles from Barcelona, Gran Hotel Mas d'en Bruno promises a stay immersed in nature, in a destination where elegance meets authenticity. Combining oenological experiences and gastronomic delights, each moment celebrates the richness and beauty of the Catalan terroir. Built in 1797, the main building has retained its original façade along with numerous period features, which are complemented by contemporary decor that harmoniously blends wood, marble and stone. Its spacious suites open directly onto the beautiful landscape of Catalonia, which you can explore on foot, by bike, or admire from the infinity pool. The wellness area and heated swimming pool, located in the old cellar, enhance this relaxing break. Gran Hotel Mas d’en Bruno houses two restaurants, which showcase Mediterranean cuisine, served with the best wines of the region. Combining pleasures of the senses with delightful new discoveries, each stay is enjoyed to the fullest, to the rhythm of the seasons.

restaurant.name: Vinum Restaurant
restaurant.description: Nestled in the heart of the vineyards, Vinum celebrates the Priorat terroir with inspired and contemporary cuisine. Combining Mediterranean flavors with bold touches, each dish celebrates the finest seasonal local produce, some grown directly in the Mas d'en Bruno's own kitchen garden, complemented by a choice selection of exceptional wines.

name: Grand Hôtel de Cala Rossa & Spa Nucca
description: Hidden in a peaceful cove in the Gulf of Porto-Vecchio, the Grand Hôtel de Cala Rossa has lived its life as a beautiful family home for four decades. In the heart of a private park that extends along a white sandy beach, international guests of all ages can find what they seek in this place where memories mingle with modernity. Chefs Pascal Cayeux and Gauthier Devillers skilfully combine the island’s best products with fruit harvested in the morning from their organic garden to create a gastronomic journey. At the spa, Hélène and Lise Canarelli invite you to discover Nucca, their natural cosmetics line made from Corsican scrubland plants.
services: Heated indoor pool not specially secured for children

restaurant.name: La Pinède Restaurant
restaurant.description: Find the culinary soul of Corsica at La Pinède, the gourmet restaurant of the Grand Hôtel de Cala Rossa & Spa Nucca. All the riches of the island are on show in the cuisine of chef Pascal Cayeux. Reveal the flavors of the Mediterranean, during lunch on the beach or dinner under the pines.

name: Grand Hôtel du Lac
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: On the shores of Lake Geneva, close to Lavaux with its terraced vineyards, which have been listed as world heritage site by the Unesco, this timeless hotel offers a unique universe, which celebrates the past of this grand historical residence and combines it with subtle contemporary flourishes. An intimate and serene setting, a creative and gourmet cuisine at the restaurant “EMOTIONS par Guy Ravet”, Asian inspirations at the restaurant “Buddha-Bar Beach by Buddha-Bar TM”, a terrace with a panorama view of the lake at the “La Marina”, an outdoor pool and the Spa Valmont, all offering moments of pleasure and relaxation.

restaurant.name: EMOTIONS par Guy Ravet Restaurant
restaurant.description: At EMOTIONS restaurant, a modern culinary approach is balanced with respect for tradition  its dishes express a beautiful, aesthetic manifestation of this harmony. For Chef Guy Ravet, local and seasonal products guide his ongoing quest for new flavors. All your culinary fantasies come to life in this intimate setting overlooking Lake Geneva.

name: Grand Hôtel du Lion d’Or
description: Tucked away amidst the Loire châteaux in the Sologne region's capital of Romorantin, the town that inspired Leonardo da Vinci to visit France, this Renaissance-era mansion has been welcoming in visitors since 1774, and is now run by the Clément family. From the Renaissance wing to the Napoleon III balconies, the 16 bedrooms here are embellished with authentic old materials, mirrors, woodwork and flagstones, all fully renovated by Marie-Christine and Didier Clément. Chef Didier Clément pours his talent and artful touch into developing groundbreaking natural flavors in dishes designed to sit alongside an incredible wine list, in which the wines of the Loire Valley are given pride of place. A delectable moment to be savored.

restaurant.name: Lion d’Or 1774 Restaurant
restaurant.description: With a highly original approach to cooking, Chef Didier Clément’s cuisine brings to life real and imaginary lands. Both inspire him to create dishes of incredible subtlety that redefine the notion of terroir. In the elegant restaurant of the Grand Hôtel du Lion d'Or 1774, discover signature dishes such as millefeuille of ceps.

name: Grand Hotel Parker's
description: Undoubtedly one of Naples most prestigious establishments, ever since it opened in 1870 on a hill in the city’s elegant Chiaia district, Grand Hotel Parker's commands a truly exceptional panorama. Look to the right, toward the tip of Posillipo; to your left, beyond Vesuvius, the city of Sorrento; and on the horizon, the islands of Ischia and Capri, for an idyllic getaway. With their parquet floors, chandeliers, elaborate fabrics and period furniture, rooms and suites at Grand Hotel Parker's offer the discreet charm of a timeless elegance. The hotel's restaurant takes diners on a journey into the heart of local gastronomic tradition. Creative interpretations of classic Neapolitan cuisine are served at George, Chef Domenico Candela's restaurant, while more casual dining can be enjoyed at the Bidder Terrace. The rooftop is the ideal spot to enjoy a cocktail as the sun sets over the Bay of Naples.

restaurant.name: George Restaurant
restaurant.description: In addition to boasting what is arguably the most beautiful view of the Bay of Naples, The George restaurant invites guests to delight in the emblematic flavors of the region, brought to life through the creativity and culinary savoir-faire of Chef Domenico Candela. Tomato, basil and wines from Campania take pride of place on the menu, alongside a tasting menu entirely devoted to white truffle.

nearestStation: Napoli Garibaldi Central Station, Metro Station Piazza Amedeo

name: Grand Hotel Villa Serbelloni
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: This exquisite residence originally built amid a vast tropical garden in 1854, exemplifies the distinguished, sophisticated aristocratic villas lining the shores of Lake Como. The Grand Hotel Villa Serbelloni has been operated by the Bucher family for four generations, keeping alive the grand tradition of luxury properties from the 19th century’s Grand Tour era. With towering colonnades, coffered ceilings, gilding, and precious marble in a décor blending Empire, neoclassical, and Liberty styles, the Grand Hotel Villa Serbelloni is a vacation destination with timeless ambiance. Surrounded by this prestigious setting rich with classic elegance, unforgettable moments await you at the Mistral Restaurant and on the broad terrace of La Goletta, dining on delicious Mediterranean creations prepared by chef Ettore Bocchia. For those who love leisure at its purest, the Beach Club, with its private beach, and the spa, with a swimming pool and state-of-the-art facilities, provide every option for complete relaxation.

restaurant.name: MISTRAL Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Ettore Bocchia is dedicated to working with quality, seasonal ingredients carefully sourced throughout the year. At Mistral restaurant, which boasts spectacular lake views, he serves accomplished regional cuisine with Mediterranean influences.

name: Gravetye Manor
description: Gravetye Manor is a truly enchanting place; both close to the pleasures of London, yet far from its hustle and bustle. This beautiful Elizabethan manor sits proudly amongst the winding pathways and abundant flower beds in the English natural and wild gardens created by William Robinson, the father of the English flower garden and a former owner of the estate. This quintessential English country house’s charm is evident in the wood-panelled sitting rooms with crackling log fires. All year round, the sweeping countryside views, first-class cuisine and attentive yet unobtrusive service ensure an unforgettable experience.
services: Breakfast À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Gravetye Manor Restaurant
restaurant.description: Seasonal produce is at the heart of Gravetye Manor's culinary approach. Vegetables harvested from the Victorian kitchen garden feature daily in the gastronomic experience, hosted in the relaxed atmosphere of the restaurant. The chefs conceive imaginative dishes that bring the signature flavors and abundance of the region to life.

name: Gut Steinbach Hotel Chalets Spa
description:
Hotel Gut Steinbach is the masterpiece of the Graf von Moltke family, who, through their passion and care, has revived Bavarian art de vivre on an alpine plateau. At this estate in Chiemgau, a region bordering Austria, they have created an authentic haven of hospitality, designed to also be fully sustainable.
At 700 m in altitude and set around a natural lake, the modern, charming and comfortable main building and chalets reflect the local architecture while also ensuring that the peace of the surrounding mountains is never disturbed. 
Across 50 hectares of meadow and forest, deer, pigs, poultry, goats and rabbits are raised to serve the rational needs of the Heimat restaurant, where chef Achim Hack showcases his farm-to-fork cuisine.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Heimat
restaurant.description: 80 kilometers: that's the strict radius from within Chef Achim Hack sources all his ingredients. Alpine cheeses, game from nearby forests, meadow herbs: here, each dish proudly asserts its local roots. At Heimat, sustainability isn't just a buzzword  it's savored amid a setting of unfinished wood, the warmth of the hearth and the mountainous horizon.

name: Gutshaus Stolpe
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: This mansion, located at the heart of the Peenetal Nature Park, a European Destination of Excellence, overlooks the protected river. The White-tailed Sea Eagles and the beaver will be your neighbours during your stay in the 19th century manor house. You can appreciate the Chef’s culinary delights during an à-la-carte breakfast, a picnic in the 10 hectare park or a dinner at the restaurant or the historic inn “Stolper Fährkrug”. Exceptional moments throughout the year and not only during the music festival… You can choose between a Wellness Lounge, a bath tub on the bank of the river Peene, a little boat in the natural harbour and a photo safari to the moorland.

restaurant.name: Kurt Restaurant
restaurant.description: Each dish served at Kurt restaurant tells the story of Chef Mehmet Kaan Verdo's passion for nature, the land and the art of conviviality. Some 80 percent of his ingredients come from local artisans located within a radius of up to 60 miles. Discover creative and tasty cuisine, to be enjoyed in the relaxed atmosphere of a modern setting.

name: Hambleton Hall
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: A Victorian house elegantly converted into a prestigious hotel, Hambleton Hall is hidden away in Rutland, in the heart of the Midlands. England’s smallest county is also home to the largest artificial lake in Europe. It is a geographical detail further enhancing the seductive powers of this hotel, which is perched on a hill overlooking the water.You will appreciate the incomparable hospitality of Tim and Stefa Hart and succumb to the charms of the sumptuous interior decor and the cuisine that is quite simply unique. When departing this magnificent setting, you will have only one thing in mind: to return as soon as possible for another interlude of calm and well-being.

restaurant.name: Hambleton Hall Restaurant
restaurant.description: Signature menus inspired by the seasons, and a visual execution that renders them as works of culinary art: at Hambleton Hall, chefs Charlie Jones & James Stone cheerfully toy with British culinary tradition. Driven by passion, and on a perpetual quest for perfection, they work to create innovative dishes that promote local products.

name: Hameau Albert 1er
description: Just footsteps from Chamonix town center, the Carrier family has been writing the history of the Hameau Albert 1er hotel since 1903. This bucolic haven, facing the raw beauty of the mountains, offers a diverse collection of guest rooms: contemporary styled in the original building, wood-paneled in La Ferme, spacious suites and more intimate chalets. All accommodation celebrates the art of mountain living, with views of snow-capped Mont Blanc. This alpine spirit is also expressed in the refined dishes of the gourmet Albert 1er Restaurant, and in the hearty choices available at Le Maison Carrier–either makes for an ideal stop after a day on the slopes in winter or summer hikes in the fresh mountain air. To complete the moment with a little relaxation, the hotel’s spa offers a range of restorative treatments, while the indoor and outdoor pools offer the pleasures of a swim facing the peaks, whatever the season.

restaurant.name: Albert 1er Restaurant
restaurant.description: The inviting atmosphere of the gourmet restaurant of hotel Hameau Albert Ier, as well as its incredible views over the French Alps, offer an incomparable setting. Home to the supremely creative cuisine of chef Damien Leveau, his unique approach interprets tradition with a distinct modernity that's guaranteed to impress.

name: Hardenberg BurgHotel
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: In the heart of the Hardenberg, at the foot of the castle ruin, there is a place which offers a refuge of classic elegance and rural idyll, unspoilt nature and modern comfort: the Hardenberg BurgHotel. Enjoy the calm of the hotel and forget the hustle and bustle savouring culinary moments at the restaurant Novalis. Relax at the Hardenberg BurgSpa with its wellness treatments. Experience the Hardenberg GolfResort, one of the most beautiful golf courses in Northern Germany or visit the Hardenberg distillery.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Novalis
restaurant.description: Novalis was one of the most important poets of German romanticism. His real name was Georg Philipp Friedrich Freiherr von Hardenberg and he is an ancestor of the current count. The BurgHotel's restaurant bears his name. The focus is on perfectly prepared dishes with the best ingredients from the region. Vegetarian can look forward to a completely vegetarian menu in three to five courses.

name: Helguera Palacio Boutique & Antique
description: The Cantabrian countryside is the setting for Palacio La Helguera, a 17th century palace which has now become an adults-only hotel. History buff, the interior designer and owner has given the property a unique soul. Some of the carefully sourced antiques on display are available for sale. Inspired by a historical figure from Spanish or English nobility, the rooms and suites all offer a breathtaking view of the Pasiegos valleys, the bucolic treasure of the Santander region. Its meadows lined with low stone walls invite you to take time and simply contemplate, unless you’d rather cycle through them. With its majestic fireplace and partially covered swimming pool, the wellness area, which is entirely open to the landscape, will make you forget all about time. Your taste buds will also travel with the creative cuisine of Palacio La Helguera's chef, Renzo Orbegoso Hinojosa, who favours fresh, seasonal and local produce.

restaurant.name: Trastámara restaurant
restaurant.description: At Trastámara, Chef Renzo Orbegoso fuses Cantabrian ingredients with the flavors of his native Peru. Pairing local produce with Andean culinary traditions, his cuisine weaves a unique dialogue between gastronomic cultures. In this refined setting, a meal becomes an evocative journey.

name: Herdade da Malhadinha Nova
description: A land of endless horizons, rich in colour and bursting with sunshine, Baixo Alentejo is one of Portugal’s best-kept secrets. It is in the heart of this region, in the charming village of Albernoa, where Herdade da Malhadinha Nova reveals its splendours. Surrounded by 1500 acres of vineyards and wild fields, this family estate blends perfectly into the natural environment. Its bucolic setting even serves as inspiration within its walls, where handcrafted marble details and pieces mingle with contemporary works. As evening falls, enjoy a glass of one of the property’s many vintages by the pool and let yourself be carried away by the sweet breeze floating through the air. Such timeless moments are the essence of a stay at Herdade da Malhadinha Nova.
