restaurant.description: At Herdade da Malhadinha Nova, its vineyards are nurtured alongside vegetable gardens, and olive trees coexist with beehives. Chef Joachim Koerper draws on the rich biodiversity of the estate to create finessed cuisine that’s rooted in the Alentejo region. At the table, oils, honeys and seasonal vegetables speak of the terroir, with understated elegance and honesty.

name: Heritage Madrid Hotel
description: The immaculate façade of the Heritage Hotel stands proudly in one of the most elegant districts of the Spanish capital. The listed building, built at the beginning of the 20th century and decorated in a belle-époque style, today houses a hotel that tastefully marries classicism with the urban environment. Designer Lorenzo Castillo has created a warm decor of lacquered wood, polychrome marble and handmade wallpaper, which serves as a showcase for a magnificent collection of European antiques and artwork dating from the 18th and 19th centuries. Haroma restaurant showcases the finest ingredients through a uniquely eclectic and sophisticated style of cuisine.

restaurant.name: Haroma Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Mario Sandoval reinterprets Spanish classics using the finest local, seasonal produce. Dishes at Haroma restaurant manage to be both simple yet creative, playing on the balance between flavors and textures. The typical Madrid stew served on Wednesdays, and its range of desserts, as indulgent as they are refined, are among the menu's stand-out signatures.

name: Hermitage Hotel & Spa
description: Situated on the south side of the Matterhorn, the Cervinia ski resort is one of the highest in the world, peaking at 4,000 metres, and one of the few to cross the Italian-Swiss border. Choose between slaloming on the Swiss side, or a descent down the trails of the Aosta Valley. The Hermitage, this grand chalet has been designed in the purest of mountain traditions, with interiors of warm brown wood, and equipped with a spa to relax in after a day of intense skiing. The Whymper Suite, designed as a private chalet, boasts breathtaking views of the Matterhorn. Succumb to the beauty of the mountain.

restaurant.name: La Chandelle Restaurant
restaurant.description: Art is expressed in multiple ways at La Chandelle restaurant-appreciated in the kitchen, at the table, and through its sense of hospitality. In a refined style, emblematic of northern Italy, dishes reflect the culinary traditions of the Aosta Valley and Piedmont. Innovative menus awaken your tastebuds in a magical setting, against a backdrop of the Matterhorn and surrounding nature.

name: Hostellerie Briqueterie & Spa • Champagne
description: In the Champagne region, on the slopes of the river Marne, the Hostellerie Briqueterie & Spa • Champagne is a paradise of well-being and good living. Épernay, the capital of Champagne wine, with its 110 km of cellars, including the most prestigious ones, to visit, is just a few minutes away. Here, every possible step has been taken to ensure complete relaxation between serene walks in the landscaped garden and its roses, tranquil baths, essential oil massages at the spa and gourmet meals that delight the palate whilst keeping the figure trim. Don’t resist a glass of champagne.

restaurant.name: Alcôve Restaurant
restaurant.description: Full of flavors and aromatic scents, the creative cuisine of the chef is a culinary invitation to commune with nature. In the warm atmosphere of the gourmet restaurant of Hostellerie Briqueterie & Spa, meals are accompanied by views of landscaped gardens and elegant fountains.

name: Hostellerie de Levernois
description: On the banks of the river Bouzaize sits a beautiful white house with an elegant French-style garden and a majestic 17 acres park with century-old trees. This very charming property skilfully blends tradition and modernity, in the heart of the prestigious Côte de Beaune vineyards. In the gourmet restaurant the chef serves a traditional cuisine with a modern flair. The vegetable garden allows for simple, delicious dishes at the Bistrot du Bord de l’Eau in the ancient kitchen that dates back to 1750. Finally, the cellar boasts a selection of 1000 references from the most prestigious wines from Burgundy.

restaurant.description: With its view of the neighboring park and the river that runs through it, restaurant La Table de Levernois is the destination setting where the chef presents an ode to the surrounding terroir and its delicious produce. His culinary philosophy: to share his passion and elicit emotion in guests through taste, flavor and color.

name: Hostellerie Du Pas de L’Ours
description: Skiing on the world-renowned slopes of Crans-Montana is not the only pleasure in store for you at this charming chalet. The wood façades have been well-preserved as well as the interior stone walls. With views of the snow-covered mountain peaks, the suites have open fireplaces and jacuzzis that serve as lovely compliments to the relaxing therapies offered by L’Alpage Spa. At the hotel’s gourmet restaurant, chef Franck Reynaud invites you to taste his creations in which “emotion” serves as a guiding force, while those desiring hearty cuisine will find what they seek at Bistrot des Ours. And no visit would be complete without admiring the painting of Felix, the hotel’s former resident donkey.

restaurant.description: At Restaurant Gastronomique l'Ours, Chef Franck Reynaud showcases Alpine terroir in seasonal gourmet cuisine, with each ingredient carefully selected to celebrated its authentic flavor. Contemporary styled dishes are paired with a fine selection of the best wines from across the Valais region.

name: Hôtel & Spa du Castellet
description: On the edge of a forest of pine trees overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, the Hôtel du Castellet is one of the region’s most charming properties. Guest rooms are painted in hues of honey, lavender and grape and there are plenty of sun-drenched terraces. For absolute relaxation there is the pool, golf course and spa. When night falls, the gazebos are illuminated, to highlight the beauty of the grounds.

restaurant.name: La Table du Castellet Restaurant
restaurant.description: La Table du Castellet offers a rich, sensorial culinary experience inspired by the sky, the sea and the pine forest. Here in the heart of Provence, Chef Fabien Ferré uses the finest seasonal and local artisanal produce, showcased in an instinctive, sunny and expressive style of cuisine. A gastronomic journey rooted in the terroir and the spirit of the south.

name: Hotel Balzac Paris
description: Two minutes from the most beautiful avenue in the world, at the corner of two quiet streets in the 8th arrondissement of Paris, the Hotel Balzac Paris is located in an elegant building from the beginning of the last century. Previously on this location was a 19th-century property which once belonged to the famous French writer Honoré de Balzac. In order to reinvent the Balzac with Parisian elegance, Charlotte de Tonnac and Hugo Sauzay from the Festen architectural studio, have deployed noble materials and created an intimate atmosphere. “Le Balzac perpetuates a timeless Parisian heritage through its classicism. It’s a place full of atmosphere, with a history that we have respected and extended. You have to experience this place to understand it,” say the architects. 1930s-inspired décor adorns the communal lounge, lit by a large glass roof, as well as the rooms and suites, some of which offer views of the Eiffel Tower. After exploring the city, a Japanese spa provides a calming break, offering a hammam or a traditional massage.

name: Hotel Bareiss
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: This charming hotel in the heart of the Black Forest offers a variety of activities for all ages. The rooms, which all have the charm of an elegant country house, provide the highest standards of comfort. If relaxation is what you seek, enjoy the spacious wellness area and spa, the exclusive Sauna World or the nine pools and natural pond which are perfect for a dip before savouring a delicious meal in one of the three à la carte restaurants. As for Bareiss’ gourmet restaurant, it ranks among Germany’s top ten, with three-Michelin-star chef Claus-Peter Lumpp masterfully leading the kitchen, joined by restaurant manager Thomas Brandt and sommelier Teoman Mezda.

name: Hotel Cappella
description:
Travel to the heart of the Dolomites, facing the mountain panorama of the Sella massif, to discover the Hotel Cappella, a family-owned property with a unique character, where traditions of hospitality have been passed down the generations. Offering exclusive access to the mountain, winter sports enthusiasts only have to step through the door to find themselves on the slopes. In summer, admire the meadows as they bloom to life, or contemplate these majestic landscapes during a hike or on a bike ride.

Enjoy a moment of relaxation in one of two swimming pools or at the spa, which has a large outdoor relaxation area overlooking the mountains. Children will find plenty to be occupied with in the games room and can also participate in numerous activities all year round. In guest rooms, the cozy atmosphere is delivered with a rustic chic décor that celebrates natural wood.

Passing through the corridors and art gallery of Hotel Cappella feels like visiting a museum, with countless paintings, sculptures and original handicrafts sourced by the owners during their travels. From the kitchen, the chef invites you to savor refined Mediterranean dishes, prepared with local produce and herbs grown in the hotel’s kitchen garden.

restaurant.name: Cappella Restaurant
restaurant.description: Regional products and fresh herbs from Cappella Restaurant's kitchen garden combine brilliantly in dishes composed by Chef Paul Mittermair. Aromas and colors combine harmoniously on beautifully presented plates, with meals enjoyed to panoramic views of the Dolomites.

name: Hotel Copernicus
description: Krakow was once the capital of Poland and it still has many impressive monuments from this period, such as the Royal Castle overlooking the Wawel Hill, where the country’s monarchs lived. Copernicus Hotel is located on Kanonicza, the city’s oldest street. For centuries, this building was a part of the cathedral chapel and hosted the intellectual and financial elite of Krakow. It owes its name to the famous Polish astronomer Nicolas Copernicus, who was one of the guests of the house. He, too, must have admired the precious polychrome paintings and inscriptions dating from the 1500s and, in the evening, the flaming sunsets that seem to set the city alight. After visiting the city, relax at the Copernicus Spa by L’Occitane with a range of custom treatments.

restaurant.name: Copernicus Restaurant
restaurant.description: At the Copernicus Restaurant, Guests may taste the dishes prepared according to the old polish recipes interpreted in an innovative manner by the chef Krzysztof Waś. Every meal can be accompanied by excellent wine from our extensive wine list or followed by our Sommelier's suggestion. Each month our Chef invites you to sample the new tasting menu.

name: Hôtel de la Cigogne
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Discovering the beautiful sights of Geneva starts at the Hôtel de la Cigogne, a haven of peace and tranquility in the heart of Geneva’s business centre, only a few steps from the old city, the lake and the cultural district. On place Longemalle, the gilded swan overlooking the door sets the tone of a hotel in which all rooms are different and have been entirely redesigned in a young and dynamic spirit. In culinary terms, you can take a delicious break right in the city centre and sample gourmet treats, now also on the terrace.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: Restaurant de la Cigogne
restaurant.description: For Nicolas Pasquier, cooking is a matter of life-long passion and ongoing commitment. This philosophy is fully reflected in the culinary adventures he conjures at Restaurant de la Cigogne, in the heart of Geneva. Pleasures of the palate come alive through the subtle marriage of seasonal products, which take pride of place on the menu.

name: Hôtel de la Plage
description: Lobster ravioli, crab cakes, farm-raised veal or lamb and turbot fillet–just some of the gourmet dishes offered throughout the seasons, which taste extra special when enjoyed overlooking the water, in the splendid setting of the Hôtel de la Plage. The panoramic restaurant overlooks the ocean and the vast beach of fine sand, only separated by a unique dry stone wall. There are similar views from the rooms, where you can hear the gentle lapping of the waves. Guests with families will enjoy the beach entirely dedicated to children, the water-based recreational activities and walks offered by the Bay of Douarnenez and the Finistère.

restaurant.name: Hôtel de la Plage Restaurant
restaurant.description: At the gourmet restaurant of Hotel de la Plage, enjoy the spectacle of ocean life, nature and the elements. This story comes to life in the authentic cuisine of Chef Yoann Noël. From the dining room or on the terrace, enjoy this perfect alchemy of products from the ocean and the Breton soil.

name: Hôtel de Pavie
description: An essential stop along the famed Route des Grands Crus, the charming medieval village of Saint-Emilion is home to Hôtel de Pavie, an intimate establishment with an elegant atmosphere. With its gastronomic restaurant–whose menu is spearheaded by Yannick Alléno–it’s a must-visit for epicureans that’s just 40 minutes from Bordeaux. Rooms and suites are arranged around three buildings: La Maison du Clocher (or Bell Tower), named for its neighboring, iconic church; La Maison de Village, a historic residence featuring Roman ceramics and English style; and La Maison des Suites, which juxtaposes contemporary decor with the historic character of its 18th-century origins. Its restaurant–La Table de Pavie–offers its interpretation of regional haute cuisine, paired with wines from surrounding vineyards. You can admire the landscape from the raised terrace, which also offers breathtaking views over the city’s rooftops.

restaurant.description: La Table de Pavie creates a subtle dialogue between gastronomy and the landscapes of Bordeaux. Chefs Yannick Alléno and Sébastien Faramond offer two menus-one featuring signature classics from the region, the other focused on plant-based cuisine. Each dish explores imaginative combinations of earth, garden and vine, in a refined and inspired approach to dining.

name: Hotel Dollenberg
description: In the guest book of the Hotel Dollenberg, one visitor wrote: “What a shame you can’t cuddle a hotel.” This hotel offers a hospitable atmosphere and excellent service, both of which engender such warm feelings. The relaxing treatments in the spa, the magical dinners in romantic forest clearings of Bad Peterstal and the delightful landscapes of the Black Forest all make for a wonderful stay. Chef Martin Herrmann, the figurehead of the Dollenberg, focuses on the elements which are absolutely indispensable for the dishes. He creates an inventive cuisine inspired by France and the Baden terroir.

name: Hotel Grad Otočec
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: In a haven of peace where the spirit of the past blends marvellously with the rhythm of modern life, Otočec Castle offers an atmosphere that has retained its character and authenticity. This unique castle is on an island right in the middle of Krka River. This magical setting invites relaxation in the heart of Slovenian nature and cultural history: a gentle morning walk accompanied by birdsong, an unforgettable game of golf at one of the best European golf courses or a gourmet candle-lit dinner and a delicious glass of wine in front of the crackling wood fire. Everything here, right down to the attentive staff keen to fulfil every wish, contributes to well-being.

restaurant.description: Classics of European cuisine and Slovenian-inspired creations share the menu at Otočec Castle Restaurant. The chef draws inspiration from nature and the changing seasons, selecting fresh produce from the garden and surrounding organic farms. A warm, refined atmosphere adds to the pleasure of the moment.

name: Hotel Heritage
description: Only 50 metres from the Place du Marché, this fine 19th century building is the ideal starting point to explore on foot, by carriage or boat the historic, mythical and romantic town of Bruges, its museums and boutique. On returning to the Heritage, relax in one of the guest rooms with their unique, sophisticated décor or in the comfortable lounge bar, Le Magnum. To savour local cuisine, head for Le Mystique restaurant, with its charm that has remained intact since 1869, where you will be privy to the delights of a 14th century wine cellar. The vaulted fitness room has also been wonderfully preserved and dates from the 12th century.

restaurant.name: Le Mystique Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Seppe Swinnen cultivates culinary simplicity: he focuses on the purity of his ingredients, the finesse of flavors and the precision of his techniques. Just a stone's throw from the Grand-Place de Bruges, Le Mystique offers cuisine that’s rooted in Flemish terroir but enriched with culinary influences from around the world. Yet each dish feels effortlessly balanced, with no unnecessary embellishments.

name: Hotel Jagdhof Glashütte
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Nestled in the Rothaar Mountains, far from the hustle and bustle of daily life, this oasis of hospitality and culinary delights suddenly appears from nowhere. Jagdhof Glashütte, one of Germany’s most beautiful countryside hotels, is situated in the heart of a densely wooded region, at the end of the spectacular Lahn Valley. Relaxation, fine dining and romanticism are part of the lifestyle in the forests and former hunting grounds of the Counts of Wittgenstein. The luxurious, rural atmosphere is already evident on entering the magnificent hall with its fireplace for a welcome cocktail. This hotel is ever faithful to its motto: back to nature, but with style.

restaurant.name: Rôtisserie Jagdhofstuben
restaurant.description: The Jagdhofstuben cultivates a taste for spectacle: flamboyant decor, open-flame cooking over charcoal, seasonal game and fine wines combine within a cozy and authentic atmosphere. The chef reinterprets German tradition with refinement inspired by the quality of his ingredients.

nearestAirport: Frankfurt (Intl) 130 km (80 miles), Köln/Bonn 110 km (68 miles)

name: Hotel Katarina
description: At the foot of hills on the Dalmatian coast, surrounded by vineyards and pine forests, Orebić has preserved all the charm and authenticity of a traditional Croatian fishing village–an ideal setting for Hotel Katarina, located right on the seafront in a garden dotted with palm trees. To transform this property into a chic boutique hotel, the owners enlisted renowned American designer Marie Meko to create a stylish vacation atmosphere. Her bright, nautical-inspired decor draws on the colors of the surrounding nature. The sea is ever-present, visible from the suites with balconies and from The Captain’s Terrace restaurant, where chef Frano Vidović serves regional cuisine with a taste of the sea, paired with the finest wines from Korta Katarina, the sister property's estate. Hotel Katarina also promises sublime relaxation and well-being at its spa, which includes a sauna and fitness room.
services: Breakfast Buffet, À la carte, In-room

restaurant.name: The Captain's Terrace Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a picturesque setting overlooking the Adriatic, The Captain’s Terrace invites diners to savor locally caught seafood and regional specialties. The menu, composed by Chef Frano Vidović, focuses on minerality and marine flavors, and reinterprets traditional dishes from across the Pelješac peninsula, paired with the finest wines from the Korta Katarina estate.

name: Hôtel Le Place d’Armes
description: Le Place d’Armes is a charming hotel on the place d’Armes, the historic heart of Luxembourg, a meeting place in summer and winter, close to the “Cercle Cité”, the business and pedestrian shopping district. Past and present are perfectly balanced in the 18th century building, which skillfully blends Art nouveau, a revisited Baroque and contemporary styles. Each of the guest rooms and suites is decorated in a delicate colour palette and has its own style and charm. The gourmet restaurant La Cristallerie focuses on exceptional products and the Plëss has the charming atmosphere of a chic brasserie, whilst the renowned Café de Paris is marked by a bistro atmosphere.

restaurant.name: La Cristallerie Restaurant
restaurant.description: Beneath the sculpted ceilings of La Cristallerie, the Art Nouveau decor sets the tone: elegant, vibrant and precise. Chef Milan Brée draws inspiration from this to create haute couture cuisine, reflecting the seasons and inspired by truly exceptional ingredients. Each dish brings to life an eye for detail that stirs the emotions.

name: Hôtel Les Barmes de l'Ours
description: At the foot of the famous Face de Bellevarde Olympic slope, Les Barmes de l’Ours welcomes you into an elegant and traditional setting. Each of its floors features a unique and warm decor. The Michelin- starred restaurant La Table de l’Ours is renowned for its outstanding regional cuisine; while La Rôtisserie, under its large wooden beams, is admired for its spit-roasted meats. Lastly, it is in the relaxed atmosphere of the Coin Savoyard where a grand array of local specialities can be savoured. The Sofa Bar offers a wide range of drinks to enjoy by the fire; and you will also appreciate the cigar cellar, the bowling alley and its game room, the pro- shop services, the swimming pool and the 1,000 sq m spa, which is an ideal escape for relaxing both body and mind. Active and epicurean... there is something for everyone.

restaurant.name: La Table de l'Ours Restaurant
restaurant.description: ‘Sharing’ is the watchword of Antoine Gras, chef of gastronomic restaurant La Table de l'Ours. In this warm and inviting setting, with a panoramic view of Val d'Isère, discover delightful cuisine inspired by the surrounding nature. Menus entitled ‘Parfum d'hiver’ or ‘Refuge’ reveal poetic and tasty culinary associations.

name: Hôtel Les Roches
description: Like a boat preparing to set sail, Hotel Les Roches seems as though it is moored in the Aiguebelle Bay in Le Lavandou. Respectful of both its history and its environment, the hotel wholly resonates with the spirit of the Côte d’Azur. Though it exudes classic elegance, the restored building still has a modern character that is especially seen in the choice of contemporary materials. This refined style is found in the décor of the rooms and suites, light-drenched and looking out onto the Mediterranean Sea, stretching onto balconies or terraces. The outdoor swimming pool boasts views of both the sea and Le Levant island and its sisters. Any Mediterranean getaway here is made complete at the L’Oursin gourmet restaurant, offering friendly, family-style meals on the rooftop of the Hôtel Les Roches. After a day at the beach or a seaside outing, the L’Oiseau Bleu spa awaits, a serene oasis that is the perfect place for a moment of pure well-being.

restaurant.name: L’Oursin restaurant
restaurant.description: At restaurant L'Oursin, the cuisine naturally draws inspiration from the Mediterranean. Complementing the paintings that adorn the dining room walls, Chef Antoine Gras creates contemporary, creative dishes, finished with flavorful sauces that will delight gourmets. With views over the sea, diners can expect an enchanting seaside escape.

name: Hotel Neri
description: One of the impressive façades of the Neri, tucked away in the heart of the romantic Gothic district, gives onto Sant Felip Neri square, a magical place with Gothic edifices and the church where Gaudí used to come to pray every day. This hotel was created when, in the 18th century, two mansions merged – one dating from the Middle Ages. It boasts a delightful décor, blending period features with ultra-modern design. The chef creates a Mediterranean cuisine with a seasonal menu in this private, welcoming restaurant. At the top of the building, “Roba Estesa” is an oasis in the city, where you can enjoy vistas of the city at nightfall, while trying delicious tapas.

name: Hotel Orfila
description: In the heart of Madrid, just five minutes from the Museo del Prado, where Hieronymus Bosch’s masterpiece, The Garden of Earthly Delights, hangs among the paintings of Vélasquez, Titian and Rubens, another garden of delights awaits. The Hotel Orfila, a small palace built in 1886, has the feel of an art gallery, with its period furniture, feature marble and superb trompe l’oeil effects in the green garden courtyard. In the 1920s, the hotel was made famous by the plays staged within its walls. Today, its flowered patio invites you to dine under the stars and its tea room is imbued with English chic. This is an ideal place from which to explore Madrid at its own rhythm.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost
main restaurant and common areas are accessible
Packed lunch for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: El Jardín de Orfila Restaurant
restaurant.description: Mario Sandoval, executive chef of El Jardín de orfila, delights diners with a gastronomic proposal based on recovering the tastes of traditional cuisine with the most avant-garde techniques and the care and selection of the best raw materials to ensure that each dish is exquisite, an each experience unique.

name: Hotel Plaza e de Russie
description: In Viareggio, an elegant seaside resort on the Tuscan coast, travel through history with a stopover in this majestic art deco hotel. Ideally located along the town’s lovely promenade, this late 19th-century residence, completely renovated in 2019, will envelop you in its warm and romantic atmosphere of stylishly contemporary charming furniture. Looking for peace and quiet? Embrace the ever-present dolce vita by relaxing on the long sandy beaches of the coast or in the outdoor pools at the beach clubs just in front of the hotel, a splendid art nouveau building. Then head to the restaurant Lunasia, where a delicious Mediterranean cuisine awaits you, as well as a breathtaking view of the Liberty Promenade.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: Lunasia Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Lunasia, amid a refined setting that lends itself both to intimate dinners and more convivial occaions, you will encounter the gourmet taste and traditions of the local terroir-stretching from the Mediterranean Sea to the Apuan Alps. For the ultimate dining experience, take a seat at the chef's table and enjoy the culinary ballet that plays out in front of you.

name: Hotel Raphaël
description: With its facade covered in ivy, the Raphael, just a stone’s throw from the Pantheon and St Peter’s Basilica, is the ideal starting point to experience the elegance of Rome. Come and discover this magnificent residence, a small museum itself, with works of art including sculptures, paintings, lithographs, antiques and collections of Picasso ceramics and Mayan art. Above the rooftops, the flower-filled terrace is the place to savour an organic and natural cuisine, whilst contemplating the magnificent panoramic views of the city. Discover the two new modern and original “executive floors” bearing the signature of the renowned American architect Richard Meier.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: Mater Terrae Restaurant
restaurant.description: Mater Terrae defies all of the clichés surrounding vegetarian cuisine. Led by a particularly talented chef, the restaurant presents the richness of organic and seasonal produce in elegant, colorful dishes. In the background, a spectacular view of the Eternal City makes the experience even more unforgettable.

name: Hôtel Restaurant Auberge du Père Bise – Jean Sulpice
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Located on the edge of Lake Annecy and protected by the surrounding mountains, the Auberge du Père Bise is a peaceful and authentic setting conducive to relaxation. A unique spot on the waterfront with mountain views… This stunning property, taken over by chef Jean Sulpice and his wife Magali, has long been witness to the richness of our gastronomic heritage. Passionate about nature, which has become his signature, chef Jean Sulpice traverses the Savoie region so dear to him, exploring it with originality, independence and gourmet enjoyment. Come and discover the new Auberge du Père Bise by Jean Sulpice with its hotel comprising 23 rooms and suites, gastronomic restaurant, bistro and delicatessen.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

Electric charging station at extra cost

restaurant.name: Auberge du Père Bise - Jean Sulpice Restaurant
restaurant.description: Having spent 15 years at altitude already, I am taking over the Auberge du Père Bise with my wife Magali. It's the next exciting chapter of a wonderful story. I hope to build on the heritage of this iconic establishment with pride and leave my mark on it too. Being a chef means creating dishes that reflect your style. Bold, natural flavours, combining bounty and indulgence, the freedom to express this region to which I am so attached. I urge you to come and discover the new Auberge du Père Bise on the waterfront with mountain views.

name: Hôtel Restaurant En Marge
description: On the hillsides of Aureville, in the Toulouse countryside, Isabelle and Frank Renimel have designed a welcoming and authentic home with a touch of whimsy. The brick and pebble walls of the old building have been preserved and stand in exquisite harmony with the new additions of sleek geometric lines. The decor is simple and warm, embellished with splashes of bright colours here and there. As for the five rooms, they each evoke an emotion: fun, indulgence and so on. The magic continues with the cuisine. Composed like paintings, the dishes are each signed with a flower from the garden. Highlighting the best of the region, enjoy such specialities as suckling pig or caviar from the Pyrenees.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: En Marge Restaurant
restaurant.description: Dishes composed like paintings and finished with flowers grown in the garden bring to life the region's unique products. In the calm setting of Restaurant En Marge, chef Frank Renimel opens his heart to you and offers you an unusual journey, both olfactory and gustatory. Discover the culinary treasures of the Midi-Pyrénées.

name: Hotel Restaurant Krone Regensberg
description: Krone Regensberg is an impressive house, rich in history yet perfectly contemporary. In the village of Regensberg, it stands like a natural fortress perched on the sloping vineyards of the Jura Mountains. Solid wood floors and dry stone walls structure the spaces. In the nine suites, handcrafted materials, along with the pieces from the furniture collection, produce a comfortable and modern decor. The attention given by Pascal Gloser, the Maître de Maison, adds to Krone’s charm and intimacy. At the restaurant, the sophisticated menu is nourished by influences from around the world and made all the more delicious by the stunning views over the Swiss Alps.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
Breakfast Continental/English, À la carte for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Taverne Restaurant
restaurant.description: Enjoy your stay in our gourmet-restaurant with a wonderful panoramic view over the Swiss Alps. The chef uses fresh produce to conjure up sophisticated creations in a style ranging from traditional to modern, inspired by influences for all over the world.

name: Hôtel Restaurant Le Pré
description:
On the edge of Clermont-Ferrand, Hôtel Restaurant Le Pré unites the city with the treasures of Auvergne’s countryside. Each morning, the undergrowth, fields, mountains and forests offer Xavier Beaudiment the wild plants and herbs whose praises he sings.
The menu is as invigorating as a walk at the foot of the volcanoes. The plants dictate their desires to the fresh meat and lake fish, rather than the other way around. Carefully listening, the two-Michelin-star chef then creates mischievous associations and poetic variations in which his technical mastery truly shines. For those wishing to enjoy the pleasure of picking fresh herbs in the morning dew, a few bright and natural rooms welcome you for a cosy night’s sleep.
services: Breakfast Continental/English for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Le Pré-Xavier Beaudiment Restaurant
restaurant.description: “A cuisine of a moment and of a desire, rather than that of a season

name: Hôtel Richer de Belleval
description: It is in Montpellier, in the highly coveted Occitanie region of Languedoc, where Hôtel Richer de Belleval reveals its splendour. Meticulously renovated by the finest specialists in historical restoration, this former 17th-century mansion has been transformed into a true family home, and now includes a gourmet restaurant and contemporary art foundation. Carrying on the rich romanticism of the Age of Enlightenment, this exceptional hotel reveals the charm of its ornamental and architectural prowess with the same spirit of heritage and artistic harmony. Past and present intertwine in this unique place, as evidenced by the 20 luxurious rooms tinged with classicism and spiced up with whimsical and bohemian touches. A stay at this peaceful haven near the Mediterranean Sea is one of infinite sweetness and reward. As you enjoy the views of the famous Place de la Canourgue, a taste of the Southern-infused contemporary cuisine of the Jardin des Sens is not to be missed. This restaurant is helmed by twin chefs Jacques and Laurent Pourcel, who have found in Hôtel Richer de Belleval a setting worthy of their culinary expression.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible
Tours to areas of historical interest

restaurant.name: Jardin des Sens Restaurant
restaurant.description: Our pleasure is to share a flavorful culinary experience with our guests that will leave a lasting impression in their minds. It's an experience built around just a few words: nature, identity, taste, meaning, and sharing. We cook with a dash of extravagance and creativity to surprise them. It's a cuisine inspired by our terroir, from the sea to the plate, enriched with knowledge acquired from traveling around the world for over thirty years.

name: Hôtel Saint-Georges
description: In the heart of Megève, Hôtel Saint-Georges exudes Savoyard style. Just steps from the buzzing pedestrianized shopping street, Rue Charles Feige, and close to the ski lifts, this property features interiors conceived by designer Luke Edward Hall. Influenced by traditional Alpine styling, he has brought colorful British flair into the hotel's authentic setting. Wood, deep-pile carpets, hand-painted frescoes, Pierre Frey fabrics, antique furniture and floral tiles create a unique finesse. With dedicated spaces and activities for children, the Hôtel Saint-Georges is the ideal destination for family holidays. After a day of skiing or hiking on alpine trails, the spa offers well-deserved relaxation and recuperation in winter, and becomes a sublime yoga retreat in summer. Your focus on well-being can be followed by dinner at La Table du Trappeur, where the chef creatively reinterprets regional dishes, paying respect to the quality of his ingredients.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Le Trappeur Restaurant
restaurant.description: With wood-lined walls decorated with historic images of Megève and alpine-style wood carvings, Le Trappeur invites you to experience cozy, convivial moments. Chef Jérôme Blanchetière has embraced the traditions of Savoyard cuisine but offers his own creative take on regional classics, using fresh, seasonal produce.

name: Hotel Schwarzmatt
description: Badenweiler, a town of waters par excellence that was given the name of “Aqua Villae” by the Romans, is now more popular than ever because of the therapeutic powers of its thermal springs. At the Hotel Schwarzmatt, renowned for its excellent cuisine, the Sano & Salvo Spa extends an invitation to enjoy the exceptional benefits of thermal baths, steam baths, biosauna and Finnish sauna. Relax in the indoor pool, on the sunny terrace or in the bright pavilion with glass mosaics.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost
Electric charging station at extra cost

Packed lunch for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Restaurant Schwarzmatt
restaurant.description: Restaurant Schwarzmatt promises a cozy atmosphere, combining the warmth of wood with a distinct elegance. Chef creates wholesome cuisine that blends German rigor and French finesse. His dishes reflect his affinity for the land and his devotion to culinary excellence, blending perfectly with the understated, refined decor.

name: Hotel Seesteg
description: Dating back to the 18th century, Hotel Seesteg was once the building in which the wooden boards of the pier were stored during the stormy winters on the North Sea coast. Today, it has been converted from a storehouse into an exceptional, relaxing haven. Everything is designed to offer an unparalleled, intimate atmosphere and smart style and design. Every room faces the sea and the outdoor rooftop pool offers breathtaking views of the Wadden Sea National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The large floor-to-ceiling windows boast a direct view of the white sand beach.
services: In common areas at extra cost
Heated outdoor pool not specially secured for children

restaurant.name: Seesteg Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Seesteg Restaurant, Chef Markus Kebschull’s cuisine focuses on the freshness, flavor and quality of ingredients within an unconstrained culinary approach that’s otherwise free of strict rules or restrictions. Northern German produce takes center stage, enhanced by gentle cooking methods that best preserve the authenticity of the flavors.

name: Hotel Singer
description: Since 1928, the Singer family has imbued this hotel with a family feel, greeting every guest by name and expertly looking after them. The property is an ideal starting point from which to explore the ski resorts and hiking trails around Zugspitze. Rejuvenate your tired muscles in the delightful spa offering an array of treatments — from Tyrolean herbs and mineral salts to Caudalie Vinothérapie® products — waterbeds, saltwater steam baths, a sauna and heated outdoor pool. Singer Hotel & Spa is just a few kilometres from one of “fairytale king” Ludwig II of Bavaria’s beautiful royal castles. It is a worthy attraction any time of the year.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost
Electric charging station at extra cost
Electric charging station at extra cost

restaurant.name: Singer's Tiroler Stube Restaurant
restaurant.description: Spontaneity and freshness make the cuisine of Singer's Tiroler Stube thrum with creative energy. Combining game and wild fish from the region with seasonal vegetables, fruits, plants and spices, the chef reinterprets Austrian and French classics with gusto.

name: Hôtel Splendide Royal Paris
description: Between romance and the Parisian art of living, the Splendide Royal packs in the emotions and experiences. It occupies a mansion house built in 1897 in a historic and cultural district of the capital, steps from Faubourg Saint-Honoré and the Champs-Élysées. Elegant and refined, this authentic family home will appeal to aesthetes and the initiated with its 12 spacious and stylish suites, classically decorated and updated with subtle Italian touches in light colours. Gastronomy-wise, the Tosca restaurant offers modern and adventurous Italian cuisine that promises to delight seasoned gourmets.
services: In at least one room at extra cost

restaurant.name: Ristorante Tosca Paris
restaurant.description: Authenticity is the culinary compass of Chef Paolo Ambrogio of Ristorante Tosca. Inspired by popular and traditional Italian cuisine, his dishes invite you on a culinary journey across the country, from the Alps to Sicily, via Rome and Venice.

name: Hotel Tennerhof
description: The property sits on a hillock in the middle of a vast peaceful garden planted with flowers and fruit trees. The views over Kitzbühel and the surrounding mountains are beautiful. The hotel is at the foot of the Kitzbüheler Horn ski resort and overlooks the legendary Streif ski slope. The furniture and antiques in the rooms of this former farm belong to the family. The pools and steam baths in the spa are ideal places to relax. In summer, you will appreciate magnificent hiking trails, which start directly from the hotel and an exceptional offering of golf courses.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Tennerhof
restaurant.description: In the heart of Austria, Tennerhof restaurant invites you to enjoy an exceptional culinary experience, under the direction of Chef Kai Horitzky. On the menu: seasonal dishes, revisited with a contemporary touch, paying respectful tribute to regional classics. The restaurant also offers breathtaking views of Kitzbühel and, in summer, its terrace overlooking the spectacular panorama of the Alps.

name: Hotel Union Øye
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Discover a place at the edge of the world, where nature’s magnificence takes centre stage in a spectacle unlike any other. It is here, in the Sunnmøre Alps, between the northwestern mountains of Norway, in which the charming Union Øye Hotel unveils its splendour. Nestled amidst the picturesque backdrop of the small hamlet of Øye, where the Norangsfjord reaches its inland home, this wonderful residence preserves its historical charm while living in harmony with its pristine surroundings. Designed with warm Scandinavian decor, the 15 cosy rooms and 22 suites open up to panoramic views that will delight lovers of wide open spaces, for whom stays will be spent exploring unique landscapes and venturing deep into nature. This is the place to discover Norway’s stunning fjords, stroll in pastures or head off for adventure at the foot of impressive waterfalls. When evening comes, enjoy the sounds of the piano in the quaint lounges or share stories of the hotel’s fascinating history by the fireplace. At the Conservatory gourmet restaurant, the chef celebrates the region’s great traditions and some of the best local produce in the world. Gourmets of all tastes will be delighted by his ever-evolving daily menu.
services: In at least one room at extra cost

restaurant.name: Conservatory
restaurant.description: The Conservatory opens onto a majestic landscape, whatever the season. Chef Anette Myrhagen captures its essence in sublime cuisine, inspired by Norwegian tradition and expressed through exceptional regional ingredients. Each dish interacts with the surrounding nature, in a setting steeped in history and bathed in light.

nearestAirport: Ørsta Flyplass Hovden 39 km (1:00 by car), Ålesund Airport Vigra 70 km (2:30 by car).

name: Hôtel Vermelho
description: Over a period of twenty years, Christian Louboutin, founder of the eponymous couture brand, has developed a passion for the south of Portugal, particularly the tranquility of the small village of Melides in Alentejo, ten minutes from the Atlantic coast. The designer has opened Hotel Vermelho, a traditional property whose sky-blue façade is decorated with ceramics by sculptor Giuseppe Ducrot. They give a hint of an interior that is both baroque and joyful, with décor designed by Madalena Caiado. Here in this property, the owner invites guests to taste ‘la vie en rouge’, having called on the best artisans and some of his artist friends to contribute to its aesthetic, creating plaster sconces, magnificent wall frescoes and fascinating optical illusions. Typically Portuguese, but inspired by Louboutin's favorite color, the red tiles lining the floor of the bar come from the famed Azulejos de Azeitão ceramic factory. The hotel is also decorated throughout with furniture, antiques and objects taken from the owner’s private collection. The hotel’s rooms and suites, most with their own terrace that open onto the garden and swimming pool, each boast their own original style. Xtian restaurant serves tasty, bistro-inspired Portuguese cuisine using local artisanal ingredients.

restaurant.name: Xtian Restaurant
restaurant.description: Bringing modern inspiration to traditional Portuguese cuisine is the culinary philosophy of Chef Carla Sousa. Playing with nuances of color and texture, Xtian's dishes are presented on handmade crockery from the nearby village of Melides and served in a colorful dining space or in the gardens.

name: Hotel Walther
description: Enjoy the relaxed, modern atmosphere of this family-run grand hotel. At Hotel Walther, hospitality has been a top priority for three generations, elevated all the more by the fresh and lively new look that the foyer, reception, lobby, restaurants, bar and smoker’s lounge received in 2017. From the comfort of a deck chair or up close during a hike, the surrounding mountainscape is nothing short of breathtaking. The oldest mountain village in Switzerland, Pontresina is home to this fascinating natural beauty as well as an array of sporting and cultural activities year-round. Be it relaxing in the harmonious Aqua Viva spa or savouring a diverse selection of dishes at any of the restaurants, you decide what is best for you. The possibilities are endless.

restaurant.name: Grand Restaurant
restaurant.description: When Art Nouveau meets modernity. The Grand Restaurant is open to all, whether they are hotel guests or not, serving seasonal dishes daily. Gourmets will appreciate the dessert buffet on Sundays, while a bountiful buffet of appetizers features on Thursdays (subject to change). The menu served in the lounge area offers a wide range of dishes for appetites both big and small. Are you ready to order?

name: Hôtel-Spa La Bouitte – Restaurant René et Maxime Meilleur
description:
In an idyllic setting in the heart of the ski region of Trois Vallées, La Bouitte has opened its doors to a sublime and timeless mountain since 1976.
René and Maxime Meilleur, father and son, have built with their hands a unique chalet, an ode to Savoy living. Comforting and pure, their cuisine is the perfect balance between simplicity and elegance, delivering a chiselled and mischievous interpretation of the region's best products. The wine list is immense and the evenings for oenophiles are not to be missed by those who love wine. The lounges and rooms, delicious cocoons sculpted in wood, and especially the very caring family atmosphere make La Bouitte a residence of incomparable charm.

restaurant.name: Restaurant René & Maxime Meilleur
restaurant.description: Working as a two-man team with a supporting cast of 20 cooks and chefs, we're proud to bring you a light, pared-back and playful interpretation of the best ingredients our region has to offer. A never-ending journey for the eyes, nose and palate and tantalizing wine and food pairings: our Carte Blanche menu is an irresistible treat for the senses. We are on a continuous quest to strike a nuanced balance between the modern and the traditional, the simple and the elegant, precision and flair. In short, we aim to evoke ultimate emotion in its purest form.

name: Il Borro
description: On this superb 2,700-acre Tuscan estate, time seems to stand still. Owned by Ferruccio Ferragamo, Il Borro is a charming oasis reminiscent of a medieval hamlet comprising a 19th century villa, farm, and outbuildings. Here in this landscape, typical for the region, excellent wines and organic olive oils are produced using traditional methods. Whether exploring the hamlet's winding lanes, relaxing by the infinity pool, enjoying a treatment at the Il Borro spa, or savoring the flavors of the refined cuisine at the Osteria Del Borro, an original and unique experience awaits. Authenticity, respect for the environment, culture, and the promotion of excellence are the foundations of Il Borro's sustainable philosophy.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas

restaurant.name: Osteria Del Borro Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Osteria del Borro restaurant, chef Andrea Campani perfects his signature Tuscan-inspired approach to ethical cuisine. He harvests vegetables from his own organic garden and insists on the freshness of any raw ingredients he uses, with seasonal dishes cooked in a large artisanal oven. Meals are enjoyed amid a warm atmosphere in this country setting.

name: Il Bottaccio
description: Skirting the sandy beaches of Forte dei Marmi, in the foothills of the Apuanian Alps, this 16th century olive oil mill has been artfully transformed into a gourmet traveller’s paradise. The harmony and comfort of the elegant suites, the artful combination of antique and modern furnishings and the excellence of its cuisine are crowned by the relaxation offered in the new Otzium Hammam to create a unique unforgettable experience. A subtle food made of perceptions and emotions can be enjoyed in suite or in the unmatchable surroundings. Enjoy the marble caves of Michelangelo in Carrara, the striking coastline of the Five Lands, and the historic cities of Pisa, Lucca and Florence.

restaurant.name: Il Bottaccio Restaurant
restaurant.description: Located between the sea and the mountains, the natural setting that surrounds Il Bottacio restaurant inspires chef Nino Mosca with a cuisine that's full of poetry. It complements a culinary art that merges tradition and innovation, intuition and modern techniques. Guests taste his creations in a unique interior that also similarly evokes different eras and styles.

name: Il San Pietro di Positano
description: This hotel is the dream child of Carlino Cinque, who was passionately in love with this place and commissioned a villa on a promontory with breathtaking views of Praiano, Positano, the coast and the rocks of Capri. In the course of time, this dream grew, as did the hotel. Its many rooms have each a private terrace and a wonderful sea view. The interior details include fine terracotta floors with ceramic decoration and doors adorned with frescoes. An elevator built into the rocks leads down to a private beach and to the Carlino restaurant. The spa and tennis court between the cliffs are also not to be missed.

restaurant.name: Zass Restaurant
restaurant.description: Overlooking one of the most beautiful panoramas on the coast, Zass offers signature cuisine that authentically reflects this terroir. The chef pairs fresh produce from the property's organic vegetable garden with ingredients from local artisan producers, composing them in culinary creations inspired by contemporary art. Each dish can be perfectly complemented by one of the restaurant's 600 wines.

name: In Lain Hotel Cadonau
description: A wonderful mountain landscape sets the scene for this beautiful inn, which has preserved its classic Engadine style for over 450 years. The latest buildings blend in harmoniously; their modern lines, clad in natural wood, draw inspiration from the surrounding pine and larch forests. This peaceful, quiet and welcoming address is the work of a family, the Cadonau. Whether as hoteliers, cooks or carpenters, several generations of its members passionately bring their talents to the table. Chef Dario Cadonau’s fine cuisine is based on fresh seasonal ingredients and gracefully sways from hearty mountain specialities and gourmet classics to surprises.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: VIVANDA Restaurant
restaurant.description: At the Gourmet Restaurant VIVANDA you will be treated to an exclusive surprise, degustation menu in the evening. You can select the wine to go with your gourmet meal yourself in the vaulted cellar or choose from the wine list. The open fire, many candles and the personal service will provide you with an unforgettable experience.

name: Jiva Hill Resort
description: Modern, architect-designed buildings show off their clean lines in the heart of 124 acres of magnificent parkland. Gaze through the huge picture windows of the guestrooms at a magnificent panorama of mountains, not to be beaten by the views of Mont Blanc to be enjoyed from the restaurant’s panoramic terrace. Between the French cuisine with a modern twist, Italian gastronomy, and the wellness rituals lavished by the spa, both mind and body will become soothed and relaxed. Outdoors, golf lovers will enjoy the synthetic greens and the 18 tees of the innovatively eco-friendly 9-hole course. Finally, an artificial lake is dedicated to water sports.

restaurant.name: Le Jiva Restaurant
restaurant.description: At the center of the modern, authentic and refined gourmet cuisine of chef Jean-François Vasseur, the aesthetics of dining are fundamental to his creative approach. In the bright atmosphere and contemporary wood decor of Le Jiva restaurant, let yourself be charmed by an audacious interplay of tastes, color and texture on the plate.

name: Kasteel van Ordingen
description: Beyond the sheer beauty of Kasteel van Ordingen, the remarkable hospitality of its owner, Richard Sleurs, is such that one feels immediately at home. Like the lord of the castle, enjoy a splendid holiday between the cities of Liege, Maastricht and Brussels. Gilding, antiques and Flemish Renaissance architecture set the scene for this exceptional hotel, which has been lavishly restored and modernised, as evidenced by its 32 rooms and suites designed by the famous interior designer Pieter Porters. Ideal for a relaxing family break or a romantic weekend, enjoy an intimate Vespa ride or a leisurely jaunt on an electric bicycle. Wind through the orchards and hills to discover the lush green nature and beautiful landscape of the province of Limburg. In the evening, Kasteel van Ordingen lights up and an unforgettable dinner awaits in the Aurum gourmet restaurant. Known for his flavoursome, responsible and highly creative cuisine, the talented chef Arne Beirinckx will delight all with his array of deliciously bold dishes.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Aurum Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Arne Beirinckx's cuisine balances boldness and authenticity. Inspired by the North Sea and the Eastern Scheldt, dishes at Kasteel van Ordingen combine marine influences, fine herbs and hints of citrus with local produce. The result is a unique and memorable dining experience, delivered in a particularly majestic setting.

name: Kendov Dvorec
description:
The story of this old mansion dates back to 1377, when its first landowners came to farm the land above the Idrijca River. Sitting amid the alpine peaks and Karst Plateau, Kendov Dvorec is now an elegantly furnished hotel with touches of traditional Slovenian style in its antique interiors of carved wood and decorative lace handmade in Idrija.
From the property's lovely landscaped gardens, guests will enjoy picturesque views over Spodnja Idrija. This medieval village is home to the famous pilgrimage church of St Mary on the Rock and crystalline rivers, great for fly fishing marble trout. Nearby, there is also the mercury mine, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
services: Unesco world heritage Mercury mine visit

restaurant.name: Kendov Dvorec Restaurant
restaurant.description: The chefs at Kendov dvorec prepare traditional dishes based on the rich Idrija culinary heritage. With a contemporary touch, requiring specific manual skills and experience the rediscovered authentic dishes become true culinary masterpieces.

name: KeyUrla
description: Conceived in the style of a traditional Anatolian village, with its own square and cypress-lined streets, KeyUrla’s villas are scattered across the tranquil natural setting of Izmir's Urla Peninsula, surrounded by vineyards. This destination is the perfect spot for relaxation, rejuvenation and rediscovering a certain art de vivre. Built using local dry stone and designed by architect Han Tumertekin, the villas open onto terraces and flower-filled gardens and offer refined, contemporary living space. Your experience is complemented by the Aegean-inspired cuisine of An Urla restaurant, prepared with seasonal produce and accompanied by a choice of the region's accomplished wines. Close to the beaches of the Turquoise Coast, KeyUrla offers guests access to myriad outdoor activities as well as relaxing massages and facials at the spa, using products derived from viticulture.

restaurant.name: An Urla Restaurant
restaurant.description: The abundance of the terroir surrounding An Urla restaurant provides Chefs Ali Çakmak and Seray Osturk with all the ingredients they need to create a menu that skillfully revisits Aegean classics. Their dishes are paired with the finest wines from the estate's own vineyards.

name: L' Île de la Lagune Thalasso & Spa
description: Surrounded by greenery on a small private island in the lagoon of Saint-Cyprien, this contemporary hotel is a veritable cocoon enveloped by the Mediterranean and the Pyrenees. Bathed in sunshine, the rooms invite you to leave your worries behind with their maritime decor inspired by Riva boats. On the roof, the year-round indoor heated pool offers a panoramic view over the entire lagoon, and the thalassotherapy spa boasts personalised treatments in an exclusive setting. At the gourmet Restaurant L'Almandin, the chef composes a seasonal Mediterranean cuisine of local products, magnified by their subtle pairings.

restaurant.name: Restaurant l'Almandin
restaurant.description: A sustainable, seasonal approach to cooking and a strong gastronomic identity: this is the philosophy of chef Frédéric Bacquié at restaurant L'Almandin, where he produces sensational Mediterranean cuisine. Magnified by subtle combinations of flavor, it opens up a realm of possibilities that leads you to unexplored culinary territories.

name: L’Albereta
description: The Albereta welcomes you to a refined setting, which is marked by good taste: antique-style lounges and terraces with extraordinary vistas of Lake Iseo, between the hills and vineyards of Franciacorta. From the first coffee in the morning to the last glass in the evening, the Albereta reinterprets the bistro tradition in an Italian way, whilst the new restaurant highlights the cuisine of yesteryear. Add to this the magic of the opening ceiling of your room to gaze up at the stars from the comfort of your bed and an exclusive visit to the family cellars at Bellavista and Contadi Castaldi. Finally the Albereta is a place dedicated to health and beauty thanks to its SPA Chenot Espace.

restaurant.name: L'Aurum Restaurant
restaurant.description: Nature is everywhere at L'Aurum: it's on show beyond the large, picture windows that frame the landscape, and it's also on every plate-the menu flexes with the seasons and the availability of ingredients from local artisans. Each exquisite dish reflects the authenticity of the moment and the chef's feel for the terroir.

name: L’AND Vineyards
description:
At L'AND Vineyards, everything revolves around the pleasures of the vine: 6 hectares/15 acres of organic vineyards and the possibility of taking oenology courses, or creating your own bottles with labels that grace your own name, all thanks to the Wine Club. At MAPA restaurant, cuisine is elevated to the level of a universal language, celebrating the fusion of culinary cultures born from the numerous sea voyages of the Portuguese. This is a journey conducted to the rhythm of exceptional wines, some of which are produced in the property's own vineyards. The oenological experience continues at the vinotherapy spa in which some of the treatments are grape-based.

Surrounded by vines, an outdoor infinity pool seems to extend into the waters of the lake. Ideal for relaxing, this family property is particularly appreciated by wine lovers as well as fans of the countryside and architecture. The castle of Montemor o Novo is within sight, and Évora, whose historic center is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is only about twenty minutes away. Discover an original and sensory way to explore Portugal.

restaurant.name: MAPA Restaurant
restaurant.description: At MAPA, gastronomy is expressed as a journey: that of Portugal, which has ever looked outwards. Chef Miguel Laffan draws inspiration from this veritable oceanic heritage to create inspired dishes, influenced by spices, chance encounters and clever contrasts. Each season opens new possibilities, where local culinary culture meets a sense of exploration.

name: L’Arnsbourg Restaurant et Hôtel
description:
In the heart of the forest there is a unique location, a place of unsurpassed serenity.
Yes, it is a restaurant but it is also more than that; it is an experience.
Our stars are in the sky; our rewards in the eyes of our guests.
A new vision of hospitality–a unique, well-appointed and genuine retreat.
services: at least one room and common areas are accessible

restaurant.name: L'Arnsbourg Restaurant
restaurant.description: Restaurant L'Arnsbourg welcomes guests into a room flooded with light, with incredible views over verdant, tree-filled surroundings. The creative cuisine of chef Fabien Mengus highlights local products that celebrate the seasons. Delight in dishes that reflect these natural surroundings and which thrill even the most experienced gourmets.

name: L'Auberge Basque
description: A few kilometres from the ocean, facing the Pyrenees and the Larrun, the Auberge Basque offers a calming pause in a territory full of character. A traditional inn on one side, a modernist cube on the other, the establishment offers a beautiful architectural contrast that can also be found in the cuisine. In love with the Basque Country and trained in the finest restaurants of Paris, chef Cédric Béchade joyfully blends the authenticity of regional products with the refinement of his dishes, conceived as contemporary works of art. Several elegantly decorated rooms will have you wishing to extend your discovery of the Basque mountains.

restaurant.name: La Table de l'Auberge Basque Restaurant
restaurant.description: At restaurant La Table de l'Auberge Basque, chef Cédric Béchade draws on his immediate environment for inspiration, in a cuisine that immerses diners in the local terroir. Surrounded by greenery, and amid the calm atmosphere and modern décor of the dining room, enjoy the authenticity of regional products and the visual subtlety of the dishes.

name: La Bastide de Saint-Tropez
description: Just a few minutes from the beaches of Ramatuelle and the port of Saint-Tropez, La Bastide is an elegant Mediterranean home. Far from the summer crowds, this marvellous mansion with its four Provençal farmhouses invites you to unwind in the heart of its paradisiacal park full of exotic fragrances. In the restaurant L’Isoletta, the cuisine reflects the colours of Italy and the Mediterranean. On sunny days, enjoy the conviviality of a barbecue for lunch, while staying cool by the pool.

name: La Bastide Saint-Antoine
description: An old country house from the late 17th century, La Bastide Saint-Antoine awaits you in the heart of a 25-acre olive grove. The rooms are either Provencal or contemporary style and the gourmet restaurant has a wide terrace overlooking the Cannes bay. Chef Jacques Chibois creates an alchemy of authentic mediterranean flavours under 1,000 year-old olive trees. What’s on the agenda? A meditative stroll around the grounds, exploring the botanical gardens, reading, playing petanque, and relaxing by the pool. Between oenology, an initiation to perfume creation, or the culinary and aromatic tasting school, you will fall in love with Provence at this property imbued with the personal passion of Jacques Chibois.

restaurant.name: La Bastide Saint-Antoine Restaurant
restaurant.description: Our cuisine is all about flavours, aromas and lightness It is personalised, refined, simple and precise, and gives priority to pleasure, taste and beauty. It's an unforgettable kind of cuisine that people like to share and tell each other about as a way of promoting a certain way of life.

name: La Bonne Étape
description: Countless generations of the Gleize family have looked after this 18th century post house. The balconies, the mosaiced walkways and the almond-green shutters are the work of Jany, the latest in the line. In the kitchen he reinvents the Provencal recipes of grandmother Gabrielle, with traditional flavours. Sweets are a tribute to Jany’s father, Pierre, a confectioner. Sample the delicious lavender honey ice cream served in it’s bee hive. A vegetable and a landscaped garden blend flowers, fruit and vegetables. Much more than simply a stopover, this is a voyage for the senses and a stepping stone to reaching perfect happiness.

restaurant.name: La Bonne Étape Restaurant
restaurant.description: Telling the story of his native land, and sharing it through reworked Provençal recipes: this is the culinary methodology of chef Jany Gleize at gastronomic restaurant La Bonne Etape. In its warm atmosphere, it combines family spirit with gastronomy, conviviality with authenticity.

name: La Chapelle Saint-Martin
description: Just outside Limoges, there is a peaceful haven where the lush vegetation is at its most beautiful. Time seems to stand still at La Chapelle Saint-Martin, where guests can step back in time and experience what life in the private residence of this 19th century porcelain manufacturer would have been like. Those who are partial to a beautiful landscape will instantly fall in love with the chapel surrounded by 35 hectares of 200 year-old trees. The surrounding countryside is delightful, and the grounds, ponds and old dock are the perfect place for quiet contemplation. While the house and gardens are historic, the inspirational cuisine is refreshingly modern.

restaurant.name: La Chapelle Saint Martin Restaurant
restaurant.description: Overlooking the woodland of La Chapelle Saint Martin park, Gilles & Henri Dudognon's gourmet restaurant offers a unique setting. His dishes are both an expression of the terroir and the culinary know-how that's distinct to the region. Even the creative plateware enhances the Limousin produce offered to guests.

name: La Cheneaudière - Hôtel Spa 5*
description: Located between Strasbourg and Colmar, nestled in the Vosges mountains, La Cheneaudière welcomes visitors in search of clean air and relaxation. A member of Relais & Châteaux for 50 years, this family-owned property perpetuates Alsatian hospitality in an authentic yet refined way, where the backdrop of nature offers a total disconnection. Elegant guest rooms offer breathtaking views of the picturesque Bruche Valley. There are two restaurants, guaranteed to satisfy gourmets, with delicious cuisine that respects the seasons and showcases the region's finest artisanal produce. Guests also come to La Cheneaudière for the spa. Spread over 27,000 square feet/2,500 square meters and with an expansive feel, it benefits from some more unusual facilities, including a flotation tank where you can experience weightlessness. Discover the perfect destination to put your body and mind to rest and awaken your senses.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Gastronomique "Le Feuillage"
restaurant.description: Fruits and vegetables, meats and fish, cheeses and dairy products... Passionate about the surrounding terroir, the chef Jean-Paul Acker of Le Feuillage works mainly with local produce, prepared and cultivated by trusted artisans. Equally creative and delicious, his cuisine does not neglect vegetarians, to whom the Le Potager menu is dedicated.

name: La Côte Saint Jacques & Spa
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: The Côte Saint Jacques, in the heart of Burgundy, is a captivating destination. Most of the pleasant rooms have nice terraces, and there are panoramic views from the jacuzzi on the banks of the river Yonne. With an array of activities available, from cooking lessons, to strolling along the canal, an indoor swimming pool, a spa and special programmes for children, you will not be at a loss for things to do. Chef Jean-Michel Lorain, the hotel’s talented and exuberant owner, will welcome you like a friend, and if you’re lucky, he will let slip the secrets of his recipes.

restaurant.name: La Côte Saint-Jacques Restaurant
restaurant.description: The menu of La Côte Saint-Jacques reflects the property's family heritage, while mirroring a modern approach to cuisine. The chef expresses his creativity by celebrating the quality and purity of produce sourced each day from the region's best artisans. From apparent simplicity, his dishes progressively reveal an exceptional interplay of textures and flavors.

name: La Ferme Saint-Siméon Hôtel & Spa
description: Once a refuge for impressionist painters, La Ferme Saint-Siméon Hôtel & Spa is celebrated for its bucolic surroundings and beautiful Seine estuary light. Monet and Courbet would come here and work on their colours, and Baudelaire would flee the melancholy of Paris in search of inspiration. La Ferme Saint-Siméon, close to the old harbour of Honfleur and the Eugène-Boudin Museum, provides relaxing treatments based on organic ingredients such as milk and Normandy apples. “Oh, Saint-Siméon”, exclaimed the painter Eugène Boudin in one of his journals. Indeed it is hard not to love the place, and even harder to leave it behind.

restaurant.name: Les Impressionnistes Restaurant
restaurant.description: Cooking is a passion that goes hand in hand with sharing, generosity and getting together. At the heart of food-loving Normandy, I combine my know-how with the spirit of the region, nurtured by its sincerity and tradition. By working alongside a passionate team, and thanks to carefully selected local producers, I find the inspiration to awaken the taste buds of every epicurean. I invite you on a delicious culinary journey to discover the Normandy region, punctuated by frequent nods to my native region, the Nord.

name: La Fonda Heritage Hotel
description: Casco Antiguo, Marbella’s historic district, is a charming area with a picturesque atmosphere and quaint little narrow streets. It is also the setting for La Fonda Heritage Hotel, an exceptional hotel composed of three magnificent historic buildings from the 16th century. Reviving the splendour and luxury of yesteryear, this remarkable boutique brings Andalusian charm to life in stylish surroundings, celebrating local culture, gastronomy and hospitality. Its 19 bright and spacious rooms are atypical in design and style, with each paying tribute to the skills of local craftsmen. The gourmet restaurant will delight epicureans with its avant-garde cuisine, blending Andalusian and Mediterranean produce with global flavours.

restaurant.name: La Fonda Restaurante
restaurant.description: The open kitchen of La Fonda Restaurante is home to chef Jorge González Carmona and his reinterpretation of seasonal Andalusian and Mediterranean cuisines. Amid dining rooms that honor water, earth and fire through the decor, his avant-garde dishes playfully introduce international influences to create incredible taste sensations.

name: La Grenouillère
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: La Grenouillère, originally a typical regional family restaurant, has become under the amazing starred Chef Alexandre Gauthier, one of France's most inventive chefs, a place which is definitely out of the ordinary. The century-old buildings have been preserved, but two sleek new metallic marquees by architect Patrick Bouchain have redesigned the property. The restaurant, which now opens out onto the garden and greenery, serves a radical cuisine, one that is rooted in the local area and uses freely and imaginatively ingredients from the sea and from hunting and gathering. You can extend your sensory experience by staying in one of the huts in the garden.

restaurant.name: La Grenouillère Restaurant
restaurant.description: La Grenouillère is a house of cooks, a house of freedom and of expression for those who live here. With my teams, I wish to offer my guests a place of exchange and sharing, to experience a timeless moment...

name: La Maison d'Uzès
description: This 300-year-old house, tucked away in an alley of the majestic city of Uzès, was once the home of the Chambon de La Tour family. Now a historic monument, it has recovered all of its former glory. Behind its imposing façade, a monumental 17th-century staircase and hidden passage lead to guest rooms featuring contemporary decor and picturesque views over the roofs of the old town. The spa is nestled under Romanesque arches and draws its inspiration from ancient traditions of health and well-being. At La Table d’Uzès, Michelin-starred chef Christophe Ducros serves Provençal market cuisine, which can be enjoyed on the patio during summer.

restaurant.name: La Table d'Uzès Restaurant
restaurant.description: Guided by his innate spirit of sharing, Chef Christophe Ducros crafts creative cuisine using market-fresh produce. Its authenticity is revealed in its sweetness, freshness and color, as well as its respect for local produce. Savor it in the dining room of the restaurant La Table d'Uzès or on the terrace in good weather.

name: La Maison des Têtes
description:
“Authentically up-to-date”, this is how Éric Girardin describes La Maison des Têtes in Colmar; undoubtedly one of the most beautiful historical buildings in Alsace. Built in 1609, this bourgeois residence owes its name to the one hundred and six small stone heads adorning its façade, behind which the couple has been dispensing their exceptional savoir-faire since 2015. After all, La Maison des Têtes is not only a traditional brasserie, but also a discreetly luxurious Michelin-starred restaurant and stylish hotel.
While the historic brasserie serves mainly local dishes, the restaurant transcends all boundaries. Within its sleek interior, indulge in a “journey into the world of plants” and savour a “puffed” Munster cheese with Marc de Gewurztraminer. Cap it off with the sweet “broutilles & canailleries”, before returning to your intimately charming room, where contemporary decor accentuates stunning traditional woodwork.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Girardin
restaurant.description: At Restaurant Girardin, everything is on point. Amid minimalist decor and a quietly expectant atmosphere, Chef éric Girardin has conceptualized a culinary adventure for just 18 guests. His cuisine is authentic and eclectic, elevating produce from the land and honoring products from Alsace. All that is good and beautiful about the region is expressed in every dish.

name: La Marine
description: A discreet white property with blue shutters, typical of the Vendée region, is home to La Marine, the restaurant of Céline and Alexandre Couillon. This property, located directly on the small port of Herbaudière, at the north-west tip of the island of Noirmoutier, has become something of a mecca for gastronomy. From his parents, the chef not only inherited a restaurant, but also their passion for the sea (his father was a shrimp fisherman) along with strong values of hospitality and respect for produce. He runs the establishment with his wife Céline, as Maître de Maison. For his bistro-inspired menu, Chef Couillon selects only the finest seafood cultivated sustainably. Even vegetables come from his own kitchen garden. Guests wishing to stay the night will appreciate Maison Moizeau which adjoins the restaurant–its rooms feature bright marine-inspired décor, polished parquet floors, white walls and natural materials, and open their windows onto the port and its boats.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: La Marine Restaurant
restaurant.description: Fresh, seasonal ingredients are at the heart of Chef Alexandre Couillon's culinary philosophy. In the contemporary surrounds of La Marine restaurant, its proud island identity is infused into refined dishes where flavors from the ocean are combined with produce from the vegetable garden with audacity and precision.

name: La Meridiana
description: Wake up in front of the golf course, explore the mountains, the medieval villages of Liguria or the white sandy beaches of the Riviera, enjoy a shopping spree along the paved alleys of Alassio or simply sunbathe beside the pool. La Meridiana is a dream place for an unforgettable stay, with gourmet cuisine and a collection of great Italian wines from nearby Piedmont in the restaurant, Il Rosmarino, and local cuisine in “Il Bistrot”. This charming property on the Italian Riviera, between Monte Carlo and Portofino, will delight you with its warm welcome and attentive service. Dog lovers will enjoy the garden suites and the pet-friendly atmosphere.

restaurant.name: Il Rosmarino Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the heart of the Ligurian hills, Il Rosmarino celebrates gourmet Italian cuisine that's fragrant, healthy, local and authentic. The chef works with the best seasonal produce, the catch-of-the-day, vibrant herbs and Taggia olive oil. For romantic occasions, the private dining space offers a particularly intimate setting.

name: La Nauve, Hôtel & Jardin
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Set in a bucolic haven just a few kilometres from Cognac, "La Nauve Hôtel & Jardin" invites you to relax and recharge. Enjoy a few lengths of the pool or take a stroll in the formal gardens, which extend over nearly 10 acres (four hectares) to the Charente river. You can also reach the center of Cognac, an iconic city of art and history, by private boat, via a canal that crosses the property. This Belle Epoque residence and former distillery, dating to the 19th century, have been meticulously restored by a local architect. Local materials, such as avy stone, bring soul and authenticity to the rooms and shared spaces. In the kitchen, Chef Anthony Carballo channels glorious nature, highlighting products from the hotel's vegetable garden and orchard.
