restaurant.name: Notes Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the intimacy of a Napoleon III salon, Notes offers a unique dining experience. Chef Anthony Carballo presents a four- or seven-course menu–each a carte blanche that promises subtle pairings and delicately balanced flavors. With just 20 diners accommodated, this is a particularly intimate setting where hospitality is synonymous with excellence.

name: La Pyramide Maison Henriroux
description: Just south of Lyon, in the Roman city of Vienne, second-generation members of the Henriroux family welcome you to La Pyramide, a warm and welcoming property set within manicured formal gardens. Once run by the legendary Fernand Point, and later by Chef Patrick Henriroux, cuisine reigns supreme in this bastion of French gastronomy. Today, Chef David Castagnet is renowned for highly finessed signature dishes, matched by a wine cellar boasting over 1,500 bottles and one of the world's finest collections of Chartreuse. PH3, the hotel's other restaurant, celebrates the joy of sharing with a menu inspired by family favorites. With its soft lighting, the Blue Pearl Lounge offers an intimate setting to complete your evening before retiring to elegant, contemporary accommodation, designed by Régis Dho. Discover the region's many attractions–including strolls through the vineyards of Condrieu and Côte-Rôtie and visits to Gallo-Roman ruins–as part of a rejuvenating break.

restaurant.name: La Pyramide Maison Henriroux Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef David Castagnet is renowned for creating particularly emotive cuisine, influenced by years spent working alongside Chef Patrick Henriroux. He showcases local produce with elegant flair, true to the restaurant's reputation for carefully finessed dishes. Desserts, crafted by a Franco-Japanese pastry chef duo, are irresistible.

name: La Signoria & Spa
description: Five minutes from downtown Calvi with its lofty citadel, La Signoria, an 18th century Genoese estate, is perfectly positioned at the foot of the Corsican mountains, but within close proximity to the beautiful sandy beach. The estate is set in a lush paradise of orange trees, palm and pine trees and gurgling fountains. Take in the intoxicating aroma of the maquis poolside, in the Jacuzzi or in the Spa Casanera. Guests will feel that they are vacationing at someone’s home. The charming, well maintained private beach “Signoria Mare” awaits them to sample the local flavours at the fisherman’s hut inspired restaurant.

restaurant.name: JOIA Restaurant
restaurant.description: At JOIA restaurant, the chef combines the best of Mediterranean flavors and Corsican culinary tradition. Its cuisine, served on the terrace facing the Bonifato mountains, is created using ingredients carefully selected from local producers, combined with vegetables and aromatic plants from its own kitchen garden.

name: La Sommità Relais
description: Hidden behind the main church, a beautiful 16th century palace blends into the whitewashed walls of the town. It is La Sommità (lit. The Summit), perched high above the historic centre of Ostuni. Behind the Catalan-style portal, a veritable oasis is unveiled. The harmony of white and cream tones, natural stone, and minimalist decor invite you to disconnect and recharge. Take the time to read under the shade of an alcove or at the edge of the little pool, get lost staring across the roofs of the city and the groves of olive trees stretching to the Adriatic, before savouring a refined and reinvented Puglian cuisine.

restaurant.name: Cielo Restaurant
restaurant.description: With the clear skies and whitewashed streets of Ostuni as a backdrop, enjoy cuisine that combines tradition and innovation. At Cielo Restaurant, the chef guides guests palates into unexplored culinary territory, where the comforting flavors of the land are complemented by an expert selection of local wines.

name: La Torre del Visco
description: Far away from the hustle and bustle of the world, you can enjoy at La Torre del Visco the glorious scenery of a valley untouched by man and the modern world. The estate house with its tower dating from 1449 is surrounded by its own eighty-nine hectare farm, romantic gardens full of roses and olive trees bordered by the river Tastavins and the mountain range known as “Ports de Beseit”. The owner grows her own organic fruit and vegetables and makes her own olive oil for the restaurant. Inside the beautiful labyrinthine buildings, immerse yourself in a novel by the fireside, play chess or piano. During the night you can observe the stars on the terrace.

restaurant.name: Restaurante El Visco
restaurant.description: Menus at El Visco are shaped by nature. Each day, the chef draws inspiration from the vegetables, fruit and herbs cultivated across the estate's organic farm and orchard, and combines them with the best seasonal produce from local farmers and growers. Expect a spontaneous and authentic style of cuisine, full of flavor, rich in emotion.

name: La Vella Farga
description:
It is in the foothills of the magnificent region of the Catalan Pyrenees — a natural setting worthy of the dreamy, contemplative films of the great Andrei Tarkosvky — where lies La Vella Farga, a large traditional farmhouse and estate built in the 11th century. Like a masterpiece, the view from its vast terrace, which includes a superb infinity pool, presents a striking contrast between the gentle greens of the surrounding nature and the cool blue of the sky.
An embodiment of pastoral elegance and tranquillity, the property is imbued with a sense of silence and calm that is deliciously intoxicating. The warm service and the restaurant’s innovative cuisine of local, natural ingredients only further confirm the exceptional, if not transcendental, stay that awaits.

restaurant.name: Restaurant de La Vella Farga
restaurant.description: Chef Paco González continually draws inspiration from nature. He conceives dishes with expertly balanced flavors, with locally sourced ingredients allowed to fully express themselves. In this peaceful setting, his dishes are an extension of the landscape, expressing respect for the terroir with accomplished refinement.

nearestAirport: Andorra–La Seu d'Urgell (LEU) 70 km, Barcelona-El Prat (BCN) 120 km

name: La Villa Calvi
description: A stay at La Villa is a statement. This hotel stands for good taste. It offers a particular art of living. La Villa, much like a private club, is a haven for lovers of beauty, tranquillity and culture. Art permeates the entire hotel, from the painter’s canvas to the violinist’s bow, not to mention the keys of the piano. Culinary art too has some well-tempered chords to play. The views over the Bay of Calvi, the scents of the sea and the pine forests, the sounds of music mingling with the song of the cicadas, the flavours of the local ingredients, beautiful plasterwork cool to the touch... Just enter La Villa, across from the Citadel, and all your senses will come alive.

restaurant.name: La Table by La Villa Restaurant
restaurant.description: Embark on a taste experience that celebrates Mediterranean flavors. At La Table by La Villa, the chef offers simple and sincere cooking. While enjoying his cuisine overlooking the citadel of Calvi, guests embark on a culinary journey into the gastronomic riches of southern France.

name: Landgoed Hotel Het Roode Koper
description: The Sörensen family warmly welcomes guests to the estate of Het Roode Koper, a mansion once owned by the Count of Dutch-Indonesia. Surrounded by classical English gardens and the lovely woods of the Veluwe, the 3,000 hectare estate is a wonderful destination for a getaway. Explore the magnificent woods, heaths, water and wilderness by foot or by bike. On cooler days, curl up fireside with a spot of tea or a glass of wine in the lounge. The restaurant offers excellent seasonal fare, which can be enjoyed with views of the stately garden.

restaurant.name: Het Roode Koper Restaurant
restaurant.description: Dennis van den Beld offers refined, French-inspired cuisine. Using meticulously selected ingredients, the chef of Landgoed Hotel Het Roode Koper creates dishes of great precision, presented like paintings inspired by nature. A singular dining experience that prioritizes finesse over showmanship.

name: Landhaus Stricker
description: Landhaus Stricker is the ideal place for a wonderful holiday on Sylt, an island with exceptional natural surroundings and particularly pure air. You will feel in top form there whatever the weather, whether browsing through the library, swimming in the heated pool or lounging on the beach, well protected by a strandkorb, the wickered beach chair used on the North Sea coasts. The 500 m² spa provides fitness, relaxation and treatments. And as a finishing touch, there are culinary delights created by chef Holger Bodendorf. You will appreciate his culinary creations with a Mediterranean flair: Tuna & Cucumber: marinated tuna, fried tuna, tuna tartar, cucumber drop, cucumber mousse, bergamot foam.

restaurant.name: BODENDORF'S Restaurant
restaurant.description: Whether you choose a menu consisting of five or seven courses, or you compose your own menu, the dining experience is guaranteed to be phenomenal throughout its three phases: Settle in, taste, enjoy. The traditional recipes will tickle your taste buds just as much as the creative flavours of modern recipes. The dishes all come accompanied by wines selected from the hotel's exceptional wine cellar.

name: Le Castel Marie-Louise
description: This Belle Epoque Mansion house with its sophisticated décor by Jacques Garcia overlooks the Atlantic Ocean and La Baule’s fine sandy six-mile-long beach. Every detail counts. This villa, now a hotel, is an ideal retreat for couples and families who want to spend some special time together. The delicious breakfast already hints at the chef’s treats in store, such as pigeon breasts in wild hay. You can enjoy all the activities offered by La Baule Resort: golf, tennis and the Thalassotherapy Centre, an evening at the Casino and water sports in the bay when the weather is fine. Here time stands still and you can get around on the bikes available at the hotel.

restaurant.name: La Table du Castel Restaurant
restaurant.description: At La Table du Castel, Chef Jérémy Coirier explores the unique culinary culture of the Guérande peninsula. Blue lobster, Mesquer pigeon, langoustine and coastal herbs anchor a menu that constantly changes, according to the tides and the seasons. Wine pairings are available for each dish, to fully reveal the nuance of its flavors.

name: Le Chalet d’Adrien
description:
In a postcard setting with unbelievable views of snowy peaks, Le Chalet d’Adrien seems to cling to the mountain. It overlooks Verbier, one of Switzerland’s most charming mountain resorts. An unrivalled ski paradise in winter, its mountain pastures host magnificent hiking trails in summer.
Inside the chalet, the matching wood panelling
and fabrics, antique furniture, books and unassumingly scattered artwork, create a charming and cosy setting from which you can enjoy the untamed beauty of the pristine landscape.
The pool has a view of the mountains, and Spa by Clarins offers many pampering treatments. The restaurants feature the culinary flavours of nearby Italy, mingled with Valais ingredients and wines.

restaurant.name: La Table d'Adrien Restaurant
restaurant.description: Using local products from Valais to create fine cuisine with Italian influences: this is the philosophy of chef Antonio Piattella. At La Table d'Adrien, enjoy a memorable gastronomic experience, seated amid refined woodwork or on the terrace overlooking snow-capped mountains.

name: Le Chambard
description: For over a century, the imposing red walls of this 18th-century property, home to Relais & Châteaux Le Chambard in the heart of Kaysersberg, have been a gourmet stop on the Alsace wine trail. Its proud tradition of family hospitality is today perpetuated by Chef Olivier Nasti, who reigns over the stoves of the establishment’s Michelin-starred restaurant. He serves cuisine that respects nature and the land, while his wife Patricia is responsible for the front-of-house atmosphere, supported by their daughters Manon and Clara. This unmissable gastronomic rendezvous, between the region’s vineyards and the Ballons des Vosges, is also a friendly inn, where a chic, Zen-like vibe pervades guest rooms with contemporary décor. Enjoy the homely, traditional side of Alsace in the Winstub, experience a culinary immersion as you discover the chef's secrets, then enjoy a moment of relaxation in the spa...

restaurant.name: La Table d'Olivier Nasti Restaurant
restaurant.description: At La Table d’Olivier Nasti restaurant, the chef redefines Alsatian gastronomy with sublime creativity. His personal approach to the region’s cuisine is driven by the availability of the finest local produce, reflecting the changing seasons, and by paying attention to the smallest details. Each dish beguiles through its balance of flavors and exquisitely crafted sauces, accompanied by a selection of the best local wines.

name: Le Château de Beaulieu
description: The Domaine de Beaulieu is an architectural gem with elegant facades and interiors in a romantic park, one hour from Le Touquet, between the Opal Coast and Belgium. The subtle balance of classic architecture and contemporary art makes this the perfect setting for what is one of the top restaurants in the region. The Chef pays tribute to Pas-de-Calais producers by using ingredients like Boulogne-sur-Mer scallops, Étaples bass and other fish from the North Sea. Meurin believes that “because of the currents in the nearby sea, the fish become strong and daring, which enhances the quality of their taste”. Savour his creations in two glass-roofed restaurants.

restaurant.name: Christophe Dufossé Restaurant
restaurant.description: The decor of the Domaine de Beaulieu's gourmet restaurant balances classic architecture and contemporary art. A similar harmony can be found in the dishes of chef Christophe Dufossé, reflecting his taste for inventive cuisine that pays tribute to the local producers of Pas-de-Calais.

name: Le Clos des Sens
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description:
Overlooking Lake Annecy and facing the Bauge Mountains, Le Clos des Sens is a discrete historic manor built in 1866. Between contemporary preciosity, a rustic touch and true authenticity, the table setting continually dialogues with the works of art.
The lounge-fireplace design, the beautiful terrace with old chestnut trees, as well as the swimming pond that borders a remarkable garden of herbs, lend themselves to dreaming. As for the spacious rooms, they distinguish themselves by their strong character.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Clos des Sens
restaurant.description: Chef Franck Derouet pursues a unique, lake-inspired and plant-based approach to gastronomy. Freshwater fish, wild plants and garden herbs blend harmoniously in bold cuisine that’s deeply rooted in this terroir. The elegance of Clos des Sens, combining nature and contemporary architecture, creates a profound setting for the experience.

name: Le Clos Vauban
description: After some three decades and an equal number of stars in the Michelin Guide for the Relais & Châteaux property Le Clos des Sens in Annecy, Laurent Petit returns to his homeland of Haute-Marne to open Le Clos Vauban in Langres: The chef wanted to at last place the Grand-Est region in the gastronomic spotlight. On the ramparts of Europe’s largest fortified enclosure, Laurent and Martine Petit acquired an historic residence surrounded by an herb and vegetable garden. They continue their devotion to passing on their savoir-faire by working hand-in-hand with the chef, Valentin Loison, and the head sommelier – his partner –, Anaïs Bercegeay, the maîtres de maison, a collaboration that first began at Le Clos des Sens. It is the terroir’s essence that is the inspiration for the chef who composes dishes rooted in the local land. A particularly poetic, delicate cuisine is served amid the intimate surrounds of Bulle d'Osier, while more accessible interpretations are on the menu in Mirabelle's dining room. All dishes are complemented by a choice selection of accomplished wines, from Champagne to Burgundy, from Jura to Alsace. Promising a meaningful and rejuvenating stay, Le Clos Vauban offers a handful of rooms and suites, all personalized in their Haussmannian home.

restaurant.name: Bulle d'Osier Restaurant
restaurant.description: Cuisine inspired by Chef Laurent Petit is served at the gourmet restaurant of Clos Vauban. Here, the three-Michelin-starred chef has entrusted the property to Chef Valentin Loison, who celebrates the terroir of the Haute-Marne forest and its artisanal producers by conceiving highly original culinary combinations accompanied by remarkable local wines.

name: Le Couvent des Minimes, Un Hôtel & Spa L'Occitane en Provence
description: Nestled on the hillside near the idyllic village of Mane and drenched in Provencal sunshine, this convent was home to nuns before it was converted into an upscale hotel. Protected from the Mistral wind by the statuesque Volx Rock, the building, which dates back to 1613, is a blend of medieval architecture and contemporary design. You will be delighted by the period facades, 100 year-old terraced gardens, and the station of the cross overlooking the environs. The rooms have been decorated with great attention to detail and have a gentle Mediterranean style, as does Le Couvent des Minimes, un Hôtel & Spa L’Occitane en Provence a temple to wellness and a promise of renewed well-being.

restaurant.name: Le Feuillée Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Le Feuillée restaurant, chef Louis Gachet creates honest, accessible cuisine, combining authentic flavors with finessed execution. He draws inspiration from Provence and Burgundy to create heartfelt, seasonal dishes with a strong identity. An uplifting experience, centered on the essentials.

name: Le Domaine de la Klauss
description: Wake up in a stunning natural setting in the Lorraine countryside. Behind its thick stone walls, whose charm recalls that of a medieval castle, Le Domaine de la Klauss hides a world of pure relaxation. Subtly decorated, the rooms and suites offer you a luxurious cocoon of elegance and comfort. With soothing views of the rolling green hills beyond and immaculate bathrooms, they are the perfect prelude to the estate’s 800-square-metre spa. Imagine a day spent basking in the stone-vaulted spa with its sensory swimming pool, jacuzzi and Finnish steam showers, followed by a round in the fitness room or a pause on a Balinese bed, before satisfying your taste buds with the inventive and refined cuisine of Le K restaurant or the generous traditional cuisine of L’Auberge.

restaurant.name: Le K Restaurant
restaurant.description: The lightness, refinement and flavors of local products are revealed in the contemporary cuisine of Benoit Potdevin. The chef likes to surprise guests with a unique play on textures. On the menu: meat and fish infused and flavored with herbs, citrus, fruit and vegetables from all around the Domaine de la Klauss.

name: Le Domaine de Verchant
description: Just outside Montpellier, Domaine de Verchant is a particularly serene destination, with its 16th-century manor house and outbuildings surrounded by 40 hectares of vineyards. These historic buildings have been carefully preserved and updated with bold architectural touches, such as its modern glass-walled additions. Whether you stay in the original house, rustic outbuildings, contemporary accommodation or its private villa, you will appreciate the innate harmony of the place. Here, contemporary decor sits alongside ancient stone and antique mosaics, creating an elegant and welcoming atmosphere. A stay at Domaine de Verchant is also a chance to enjoy an escape in the heart of a Mediterranean wine estate. Its wines pair beautifully with the refined cuisine of gourmet restaurant Marcelle or sunny lunches at the La Plage brasserie, overlooking the pool. After your meal, take a stroll through the gardens or relax in the sumptuous 23,000-square-foot spa.

restaurant.name: Marcelle Restaurant
restaurant.description: Marcelle Restaurant flourishes under Chef Guillaume Cocault, who draws his inspiration from seasonal produce and artisanal savoir-faire. Whether cultivated in the estate's own vegetable garden or sourced from trusted local producers, the quality of every ingredient shines through in cuisine that's bursting with character-a celebration of the terroir and human creativity alike.

nearestAirport: Montpellier Méditerranée 8 km, Marseille Provence 148 km

name: Le Domaine du Mas de Pierre
description: It is the very heart of the Côte d’Azur that beats at Domaine du Mas de Pierre, a luxury resort located in the emblematic and picturesque village of Saint-Paul-de-Vence in the South of France. In this exclusive corner of paradise, spread over eight hectares of greenery, nine contemporary bastides clad in natural stone welcome you into an enchanting setting abound with Mediterranean gardens. The 76 spacious rooms and suites of this luminous hotel, with its generous openings, harmonise magnificently with the Provençal and eco-responsible soul of this peaceful haven. It is a quality that extends as much to the 2,000 square metre spa replete with panoramic views of the surrounding hills as to the Table de Pierre gourmet restaurant and the Dolce Provenza bistro helmed by the chef. Ideal for a family stay or a weekend of relaxation at the spa, this wonderful abode also boasts a fantastic lagoon pool with a sandy bottom, as well as a vast array of activities that will delight young and old alike.

restaurant.name: La Table de Pierre Restaurant
restaurant.description: La Table de Pierre reflects the spirit of the south: flavorful, sunshine-filled and refined. The Chef specializes in seasonal cuisine that tells a story of the local terroir, inspired by surrounding nature. Fresh produce from the kitchen garden creates nourishing gourmet dishes, blending Provençal tradition with contemporary style.

name: Le Mas des Herbes Blanches
description:
Overlooking a picture-postcard landscape, Le Mas des Herbes Blanches redefines Provençal elegance with a contemporary attitude. This tranquil haven offers 360-degree panoramic views of the Luberon Valley–this is the perfect opportunity to reconnect with yourself and nature, combining relaxation, well-being and gentle strolls with gourmet delights.
Behind its stone façades, the hotel boasts bright interiors where natural materials juxtapose with modern design. Its calming rooms and suites open directly onto nature, while some villas feature private pools–a rarity in the region. Le Table du Mas restaurant celebrates the flavors of the terroir through finessed cuisine, complemented by local wines. For a moment of absolute relaxation, the Jocadae Spa by CODAGE invites you to experience its indoor pool and relaxation space. After a rejuvenating break, explore the hilltop villages of Gordes, Bonnieux and Roussillon, visit the Cistercian Abbey of Sénanque, or take in the scents of the lavender trail.

restaurant.name: La Table du Mas Restaurant
restaurant.description: The soul of the Mediterranean permeates each plate at La Table du Mas, where the chef shares his passion for the region in sunny, vibrant cuisine, full of color and flavor. Focused on local artisans and seasonal ingredients, both are celebrated in imaginative ways that never become too distorted from their origins.

name: Le Petit Nice-Passedat
description: To love Marseille means to love the Mediterranean, inseparable from the city, just like Gérald Passedat’s three-star cuisine. First come sea anemones, bonito, sea urchins, crabs and shellfish. Then fish occupies the heart of his cuisine. More than 65 varieties are fished locally over the year, such as tub gurnard, rascasse, red porgy, comber fish, common dentex, in compliance with the guidelines for the protection of maritime resources. The cult dish is “Bouille Abaisse”, light, iodized and zephyr-like, and also the famous Lucie Passedat sea bass. Sea views are everywhere… from the terrace, from the restaurant and from your spacious bedroom, overlooking the distant horizon.

restaurant.name: Le Petit Nice Restaurant
restaurant.description: "My cuisine is an instinctive, pure cuisine, giving flavour and texture with every mouthful. A cuisine of depth, it reflects the slow progress towards the abyss  in stages, to better discover forgotten and abandoned fish, a unique approach to the Mediterranean and respect for the maritime domain. My garden is the Mediterranean."

name: Le Phébus & Spa - Villa des Anges
description: This beautiful stone farmhouse, so typical of the Provençal landscape, is located on the Luberon hills between the famous villages of Gordes and Roussillon. The Phébus & Spa–Villa des Anges hotel occupies a historic site whose origins date back to the Knights of the Order of Malta. From the light-colored walls to the ocher of the tiles, the changing hues of the pines and olive trees and the blue of the panoramic swimming pool, a true symphony of colors welcomes guests into the soft light of Provence. In the gourmet restaurant of this family-owned property, Chef Xavier Mathieu prepares regional produce–including vegetables cultivated on site–which diners can enjoy at the Table du Jardinier. This exclusive dining spot, located directly in the kitchen garden, is the ideal spot to sample specialties like vegetable pesto, basil and garlic soup. A similar sense of authenticity can be found in the spa, where treatments and massages use essential oils from the surrounding lavender fields.

restaurant.name: La Table de Xavier Mathieu Restaurant
restaurant.description: Born in Marseille, Chef Xavier Mathieu admits Provence runs in his blood. He personifies a passion for local cuisine that he combines with impeccable technique. In the bright setting of La Table de Xavier Mathieu, he uses freshly harvested vegetables from his kitchen garden as he reinvents Provençal culinary tradition in a festival of flavor.

name: Le Ponant - Mediterranean
description: Le Ponant invites you aboard for different intimate odysseys, sailing off the beaten paths of standard seafaring routes. Whether you love the Mediterranean or are simply a curious traveler by nature, discover or rediscover iconic sites and hidden treasures on an all-inclusive cruise, a moment in time to be carried by sea breezes from port to port. Choose to travel the Greek islands from Athens, the Croatian coasts from Dubrovnik, or a combination of these destinations: Whatever course you set, it is sure to be a thrilling, satisfying adventure. Surrounded by the incomparable scents of the Mediterranean basin, marvel at the diversity of the landscapes, savor the rich cultural traditions. Reconnect with nature – and yourself – on a three-masted ship of an intimate size that has you feeling like you are on your very own sailboat. Committed to nautical tourism that is ever more sustainable and exclusive, Le Ponant provides guests with tailor-made service and authentic, locally sourced cuisine.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Le Diamant
restaurant.description: On board Le Ponant, passengers are invited to set sail for new frontiers of gastronomy. Its panoramic restaurant Le Diamant welcomes guests with buffet lunches and gourmet dinners, made with cuisine featuring local produce from each of the cruise's ports of call.

name: Le Prieuré Baumanière
description: Le Prieuré in Villeneuve-lez-Avignon, tucked away in the mediaeval village, with its cottage garden and its trailing wisteria, emanates a rare charm. Jean-André and Geneviève Charial, who also own two properties in Les Baux-de-Provence, say that this former convent epitomises silence and relaxation. In the different rooms and suites contemporary style meets ancient tradition. The menu also pays tribute to the region, with dishes such as duck foie gras with Gariguette strawberries from Nîmes and pot-roasted Costières wood pigeon with fresh sage. Le Prieuré’s mouth-watering cuisine is served in surroundings which bring all the senses to life.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Gastronomique Le Prieuré
restaurant.description: Amid the tranquility of a former monastic setting, Prieuré Baumanière restaurant epitomizes the elegance of Mediterranean-inspired dining. Chef Christophe Chiavola offers vibrant, well-balanced dishes using seasonal ingredients and garden-grown vegetables. A delightful interlude that fuses culinary heritage and emblematic southern flavors.

name: Le Relais Bernard Loiseau – Spa Loiseau des Sens
description: The name Bernard Loiseau evokes a unique sensory voyage and an authentic culinary experience. Chef Louis Philippe Vigilant has added his talent to the legacy left by his master, with cuisine based on simplicity, powerful tastes and new flavours. But the food isn’t the only attraction at this stunning Burgundy location: owner Dominique Loiseau has made this hotel exceptional in every way. The breakfast room is a listed historical monument. The dining rooms offer a panorama view of the gardens, the rooms are elegantly decorated and at the spa, the welcome is warm and attentive. This is a magical and timeless place where the idea of well-being takes on its full meaning.

restaurant.name: La Côte d’Or Restaurant
restaurant.description: Authentic flavors, delicious sauces and culinary savoir-faire have been the hallmark of La Côte d'Or for several generations. Today, this gastronomic institution of Saulieu appeals to lovers of perfectly executed classics and curious people in search of new gourmet experiences alike. In the proud tradition of Bernard Loiseau, Chef Louis-Philippe Vigilant has established meaningful connections with local artisans, whose exceptional products he showcases. Alongside innovative pairings like lobster and beetroot, fans of nostalgia will delight in the restaurant's signature dishes, such as frog legs with garlic puree, or pan-fried pikeperch in a red wine sauce.

name: Le Saint-James Bouliac
description: The Saint-James occupies buildings designed in 1989 by the famous architect Jean Nouvel. They nestle in the delightful little village of Bouliac, in a garden fragrant with lemon trees and antique roses. Inspired by the old tobacco drying lofts, four pavilions, linked by a gallery, create a modern pure space. Far away, you can see the Landes of Gascogne forest, the biggest collection of maritime pines in Europe. The gourmet cuisine is one of the major attractions of the Saint James: the chef serves cuisine which is full of flavour and based on local market ingredients, rounded off by the selection of wines from an outstanding cellar and panoramic views of Bordeaux.

restaurant.name: Le Saint-James Bouliac Restaurant
restaurant.description: Known for its subtle nuances, the chef's cuisine reflects his expertise around vegetables and his deep connections to Aquitaine terroir. At the Saint-James restaurant, his dishes showcase seasonal ingredients from artisan producers, for a sustainable and authentic culinary approach that focuses on the intensity of flavor.

name: Le Saint-Paul
description: In a secret and sunny Eden, located within the ramparts of Saint-Paul-de-Vence, a famous artists’ haunt, the Saint-Paul casts its timeless spell. This is the place to stay if you want to fully appreciate French-style hospitality and sophistication. The listed mediaeval promontory where the hotel stands transports you, in supreme comfort, to a world of myth, legend and history. The guestrooms and suites offer stunning views over the village or over the plain up to the Mediterranean. Savour delicious regional dishes on an exquisite, flower-bedecked, sunny terrace – paradise indeed.

restaurant.name: Le Saint Paul Restaurant
restaurant.description: In an elegant setting, Le Saint Paul reinvents Mediterranean cuisine with Italian influences. Chef Pietro Ingrande uses carefully selected local ingredients to construct contemporary styled dishes characterized by fresh flavors.

name: Le Suquet, Sébastien Bras
description: Sébastien Bras has been at the helm of Le Suquet since 2009, and is forging his own path, reminiscent of the one his father also took. His intuitive and carefully crafted exceptional cuisine provides the perfect showcase for the treasures of the vegetable world offered by the Aubrac plateau. Imagination is given free rein in subtle pairings and harmonies which express this multifaceted living world. The pearly white monkfish and its black olive sauce tell of Gods battling in the skies over Aubrac: your plate is a fragment of the universe. Nature is at the very heart of the Bras family’s daily endeavours.

restaurant.name: BRAS Restaurant
restaurant.description: My love of fine cuisine is like the Aubrac region or my family: it's more than a job. It means everything to me!

name: Le Vieux Logis
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Author Henry Miller knew and loved France better than many French people. He was always in search of, inspirational locations. He originally planned to spend a week at this hotel in the heart of the black Périgord but ended up staying a month. Inside the former tobacco drying barn that has been converted into a dining room or outside under the linden trees, Miller’s spirit lives on. Like the author, you will appreciate the tranquillity of this former priory with its lovely gardens, babbling brook and natural pool in which you can swim. While away the hours in a relaxed atmosphere until dusk approaches and it is time to do a tasting of some fabulous regional wines.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas

restaurant.name: Le Vieux Logis Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the estate's former hunting lodge, Le Vieux Logis combines rustic elegance with the charm and refinement of the Périgord region. Chef Vincent Arnould celebrates the richness of the local terroir in creative, considered cuisine that’s full of character. In summer, you can dine beneath the garden's century-old lime trees.

name: Lenkerhof gourmet spa resort
description: At the end of the 17th century, the first spa hotel, the Bad Lenk, was built somewhat further downhill from the richest Alpine sulfur springs north of the Alps, the Balmen. Up until the turn of the millennium, sulfur was used as a remedy, and you can still drink it to this day. Historical documents and a mix of nostalgic spa room elements and modern architecture shape the Lenkerhof Gourmet Spa Resort, a first-class hotel offering a wide range of wonderful wellness treatments. The “7 Sources Beauty & Spa” is named after “Siebenbrünnen” (“Seven Springs”), the place from which the Simme River emerges, and is thus inspired by the energy and vitality of the spring. Enjoy genuine rejuvenating treatments in a setting of natural wood and natural stone. The splendid landscape of the surrounding area beckons you to enjoy the special joy of the mountains at any time of the year.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Spettacolo
restaurant.description: Im Spettacolo erwartet die Gäste ein einzigartiges Gourmet-Erlebnis: Sie können sechs Gerichte aus einer täglich wechselnden Auswahl von 15 Speisen wählen. Die Menüauswahl variiert je nach Saison, der Verfügbarkeit frischer Marktprodukte und der kreativen Inspiration des Küchenchefs. Ein besonderes Highlight für Käseliebhaber: Ein Buffet mit 30 erlesenen Käsespezialitäten rundet das kulinarische Erlebnis ab.

name: Les Bergeries de Palombaggia
description: The hotel basks in the scents of the maquis, perched on a hillside overlooking Palombaggia Beach. This is one of the finest beaches in southern Corsica, with its turquoise waters and white sands fringed by stone pines, and the Cerbicales islands in the distance. The elegant stone bastide boasts guest rooms overlooking the surrounding countryside which are simply decorated in natural colours and materials so as not to upstage its rare environment. La Table de Mina ("grandmother" in Corsican), the restaurant, offers an introduction to the cuisine and ingredients of Corsica. It occupies a peaceful terrace next to an infinity pool that reflects the sky and the stars.

restaurant.name: La Table de Mina Restaurant
restaurant.description: In Corsican, Mina means granny'. While the restaurant's culinary approach is undeniably modern, dining at La Table de Mina is nonetheless as comforting as the name suggests. The chef relies on the authenticity of seasonal ingredients from the best local producers to create imaginative dishes full of contrasts, perfectly reflecting the look and feel of the île de beauté.

name: Les Etangs de Corot
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description:
Just a short distance from central Paris, in Ville-d'Avray, Les Étangs de Corot invites guests to unwind in a picturesque and verdant setting. This exceptional hotel, famous as a historic guinguette tavern, is flanked by a forest and ponds, lending it a special aura that feels like a step back in time. Favored by painter Camille Corot, whose former home still stands nearby, and visited by many other celebrated writers and painters, its rooms and suites are elegantly decorated in the style of an artist’s studio, with designs that reflect its remarkable gardens. A charming place to stay for couples or families, Les Étangs de Corot is renowned for its relaxed approach to life–its spa, Les Bains de Corot, is the perfect place for rejuvenation. With a terrace that opens directly onto the garden, Le Café des Artistes offers authentic, gourmet cooking, while Le Corot restaurant invites you to enjoy the delicious cuisine of Chef Rémi Chambard, whose gastronomy is inspired by the surrounding nature. 
The spa is closed for renovation until the end of June. Private jacuzzis and treatment rooms remain available.

restaurant.name: Le Corot Restaurant
restaurant.description: Chef Rémi Chambard draws inspiration from surrounding nature to craft exquisite dishes, teeming with details, that pay tribute to local producers and the quality of their products. In the calm atmosphere and refined decor of Les Etangs de Corot's restaurant, all the beauty of the terroir comes to life.

name: Les Hauts de Loire
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Along the wine route, among approximately 3,000 chateaux that have led the region to be known as the ‘Land of Kings’, stands Les Hauts de Loire. A former hunting lodge built in 1860, this property is an idyllic refuge for couples, family or friends, less than three hours from Paris. Its elegant ivy-covered silhouette nestles in the heart of a remarkable 173-acre/70-hectare park, which invites exploration throughout the year. Relax at the spa while the children enjoy the kids’ club, or set off together to discover the riches of the Loire region–the chateaux of Blois and Chambord are located just a short distance away. Like refined cocoons, the rooms and suites of the Les Hauts de Loire charm with their exposed beams and half-timbered walls. And to satisfy the appetites of young and old alike, the chef of the gourmet restaurant combines simplicity with sophistication is dishes using local, seasonal produce.

restaurant.name: Le Restaurant des Hauts de Loire
restaurant.description: Focused on a reinterpretation of traditional Loire cuisine, the culinary sojourn offered in the cozy atmosphere of Restaurant des Hauts de Loire is striking in its delicacy and inventiveness. His creations, both neoclassical and modern, highlight local producers and pay homage to nature.

name: Les Lumières
description:
Hotel Les Lumières, located directly opposite Louis XIV’s Palace of Versailles, is housed in two 17th-century pavilions given by the Sun King to Edouard Colbert and Antoine de Gramont. An historic building that combines well-chosen contemporary décor with the welcoming comfort of a private residence.
The spacious rooms and suites bear the names of major figures from the Age of Enlightenment, who ensure that 21st-century travelers have the rest they require. Amid precious woods and streamlined furniture, La Table des Lumières is where chef Erwan Le Thomas serves creations born of culinary excellence and a deep love of the region. In the spa, the gentle rippling of the marble water walls mingles with the fragrance of treatment oils, promising guests many moments of pure well-being in a soothing, refined setting, all the more enjoyable after a private tour of the château arranged by the hotel concierge.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost

restaurant.name: La Table des Lumières Restaurant
restaurant.description: French terroir meets modern elegance at La Table des Lumières, where the cuisine celebrates vegetables alongside other locally sourced seasonal produce. The menu draws inspiration from the history of Versailles to present a series of indulgent dishes, exquisitely executed with contemporary finesse.

nearestStation: Versailles Rive Gauche - RER C 1 km

name: Les Maisons de Bricourt
description:
Caressed by salt spray, Château Richeux dominates the bay of Mont-Saint-Michel with its imposing yet elegant 1920s silhouette. It is one of four Maisons de Bricourt, which comprise the Roellinger family's hospitality ventures, and an essential stopover to experience Brittanys unique landscapes and tides, as well as the region’s incomparable flavors.
The property’s twelve, tastefully decorated rooms offer breathtaking views of the sea or look over the 7.5-acre parkland estate. The first floor is home to restaurant Le Coquillage, where cuisine reflects the Roellingers’ passion for local seasonal products and global flavors. With freshly harvested produce from the kitchen garden and orchard, Chef Hugo Roellinger uses vegetable and maritime flavors to create dishes of remarkable delicacy. The close relationship with the water continues in the Celtic Baths, where you can swim facing the waves or and enjoy reflexology treatments provided by talented practitioners. The woodland gardens, shoreline and horizon inspire a combination of daydreaming and relaxation.

restaurant.name: Le Coquillage Restaurant
restaurant.description: Since 1982, when Le Bricourt restaurant first opened, Jane and Olivier Roellinger have been dedicated to protecting marine resources and upholding biodiversity. Their commitments are fully shared by the whole family. At restaurant Le Coquillage, Chef Hugo Roellinger builds dishes around the freshness and salinity of the sea, vegetables and spices, in a signature style of cuisine which excludes all meat products. His unique menu is dictated by the daily fishing catch, and complemented by a comprehensive wine list centered around natural wines.

name: Les Maisons Marcon
description: With a panorama open to the horizons of the Valleys of Ardèche and Mont du Velay, father and sons Chefs Régis, Jacques and Paul Jacques Marcon’s hotel has “Ecolabel” approval. The décor is minimalist and there are plenty of spots from which to admire the surrounding mountains. Enjoy natural swimming in the pure water of the pool. The restaurant is dedicated to seasonal gastronomy. Mountain lovers Régis, Jacques and Paul will offer you tastes of mushrooms, wild vegetables and herbs alongside the Puy green lentils, river fish.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Marcon
restaurant.chef: Chef Régis Marcon, Jacques Marcon, Paul Marcon & Luca Zomini
restaurant.description: Régis, Jacques and Paul Marcon are perpetuating a family legacy established by Régis Marcon. Their cuisine celebrates both the local terroir and the seasons, from wild herbs to autumn mushrooms. Classic dishes–like green Puy lentils with truffle or hay-baked lamb–bring to life a careful balance between tradition and innovation.

name: Les Prés d’Eugénie
description: Nestled in the heart of the Landes region, amid the foothills of the Pyrenees, Les Prés d’Eugénie is an idyllic retreat where the owners’ family spirit has been carefully nurtured over three generations. This grand hotel, whose meticulously restored historic buildings are surrounded by lush gardens, invites you to slow down, reset your senses and take a moment for yourself. Guest rooms, lounges and beautifully curated spaces are all filled with works of art and mementos, creating a particularly charming setting. Three destination restaurants, including the renowned, eponymous restaurant founded by the late Michel Guérard, offer cuisine made with local ingredients that tell an authentic story of the surrounding terroir. In the grounds, a thousand-year-old geothermal spring is the centerpiece of Spa La Ferme Thermale, where rituals and treatments use local botanicals.  At Les Prés d’Eugénie, these singular experiences combine to create a truly exceptional and unforgettable getaway.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Michel Guérard
restaurant.description: An enchanting setting, attentive service and exceptional cuisine: at Restaurant Michel Guérard, Chef Hugo Souchet channels the inventive spirit of the late icon of French gastronomy. Like a conductor leading an orchestra, he honors his predecessor’s legacy by perpetuating his playful culinary style and reputation for uniting contrasting flavors.

name: Lešić Dimitri Palace
description: People say that the Silk Road started from this island; Korčula has good reason to be proud to be home to Marco Polo's house, a stone’s throw from Lešić Dimitri Palace. The building's history dates back to the 17th century, when the Lešić family, rich property owners and merchants combined a number of semi-detached houses to create a palace worthy of that name. It has been meticulously renovated yet with a touch of contemporary design; its historical character and original layout have been retained and it now offers a small number of independent suites. Each evokes a step in Marco Polo's journey towards China, in a harmonious décor, with top-quality materials.
services: Breakfast À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: LD Restaurant
restaurant.description: Firmly committed to sustainable gastronomy, the chef at LD Restaurant champions local and seasonal produce. His contemporary cuisine showcases the abundance of the Dalmatian coast–both from the sea and the land–in dishes that are precise yet visually arresting, and perfectly complemented by local wines. Some of his creations, such as the ‘zero-waste’ gyoza, directly call out his resource-conscious approach.

name: Londra Palace Venezia
description: Londra Palace Venezia enjoys a rich history spanning more than 170 years. Its 100 windows offer breathtaking views of the Basilica della Salute, the islands of San Giorgio and Giudecca and the lagoon beyond. The hotel is situated a short walk from St. Mark's Square, along the picturesque Riva degli Schiavoni promenade. Surrounded by museums, churches and palaces, it occupies a truly privileged position in the heart of the floating city. Over the years, the hotel, a true meeting place for artists and intellectuals, has hosted eminent figures such as Tchaikovsky, Verne, Borges and D'Annunzio. Throughout your stay, the staff will do everything possible to make your getaway to Venice unforgettable. The property has a spacious terrace on the ground floor where you can enjoy Venetian specialties or taste creative cocktails. The astonishing roof terrace, the highest in Venice, offers 360-degree panoramic views from the islands of the lagoon to the Dolomites.
services: In at least one room and common areas
Sightseeing: San Mark's Square (2 min) / Rialto Bridge (10 min)

restaurant.name: LPV Ristorante
restaurant.description: At LPV Restaurant, Chef Daniele Galliazzo revisits the classics of Venetian cuisine. Expect a true gastronomic masterpiece that celebrates seasonal, local produce. In true Venetian style, enjoy the outdoor terrace and admire breathtaking views over the lagoon. Inside, you can appreciate the refined elegance of the hotel from its emblematic bar.

name: Longueville Manor
description: Independently owned and managed, Longueville Manor is a true “home-from-home” experience. Graced by woodland walks, an ornamental lake and a Victorian garden, the estate exudes tranquillity, to which the Cottage Garden Spa, outdoor pool and al fresco terrace act as compliments.Understated service, fine food and wine combine to deliver sheer indulgence, with inspiration for the seasonal menus coming from local farmers and fishermen.As wine tasting is at the heart of any memorable meal at the manor, the state-of-the-art wine cellar boasts an impressive collection of over 4,000 bottles, including some of the world’s most remarkable and rarest vintages.Lastly, Fizz Too, the hotel’s private yacht charter, is ideal for a luxurious day on the Channel Islands’ waters.

restaurant.name: Longueville Manor Restaurant
restaurant.description: Fresh produce from across the island-and harvested directly from Longueville Manor's own Victorian garden-inspires Chef Andrew Baird to create fresh and seasonal cuisine. Rooted in tradition yet innovative, dishes can be enjoyed in two dining rooms: one intimate and romantic, the other ideal for food with family and friends.

name: Lympstone Manor Hotel, Restaurant and Vineyard
description: Located in the elegant Devon coastal town of Exmouth, Lympstone Manor is a quintessential Georgian manor turned contemporary country house. Driven by the enthusiasm and talent of renowned chef Michael Caines, this haven of peace and tranquillity, set in an exceptional natural environment, is anchored in the salt-rich terroir of a region whose coast gives way to the English Channel. Overlooking the Exe Estuary in South West England, it features luxurious rooms and suites, as well as fantastic shepherd huts for a unique experience set within the woodland, complete with the utmost refinement and privacy. Lympstone Manor also boasts a vineyard. Stretching to the shoreline, it is where Michael Caines celebrates his passion for wine. In his gourmet restaurant, enjoy a cuisine of great maturity and astonishing precision, which showcases the finest products from the sea, foreshore and salt meadows alongside produce from the verdant surroundings.

restaurant.name: Lympstone Manor Restaurant
restaurant.description: Precise, eclectic, timeless: this is the definition of Chef Michael Caines' modern British cuisine. In the elegant dining room of Lympstone Manor Restaurant, discover menus that highlight the sea, the shore, the salt meadows and the surrounding countryside.

name: Maison Decoret
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Maison Decoret is temporarily closed. Across from the Parc des Sources in Vichy, MOF (Best Craftsman of France) Chef Jacques Decoret and his wife Martine have taken over a brick chalet dating from the time of Napoleon III. The chalet has a conservatory and a planted and aromatic roof, which illuminate the dining room. With its gentle, spacious rooms and a kitchen with large picture windows, it blends contemporary and classical style, just like the Chef. Jacques Decoret has created a new art de vivre in a historical thermal spa town. He favours fine produce, to which he applies his innovative skills, concocting combinations of hitherto unsuspected flavours for a cuisine nourished by smiles. Gourmets can also sign up to introductory cookery classes.

restaurant.name: Maison Decoret Restaurant
restaurant.description: It is the very soul of Auvergne that chef Jacques Decoret presents in his gourmet restaurant: Maison Decoret is a luminous setting, where the chef plays a culinary score made up of contrasts and new combinations. Expect innovative cuisine which celebrates the esteemed products of the region.

name: Maison Doucet
description:
Maison Doucet is the childhood home of chef Frédéric Doucet, who inherited his parents’ passion for Burgundian cuisine and hospitality. To experience the essence of Burgundy in refined relaxation, step across the threshold of this hotel brimming with character, set in the pretty village of Charolles, known as “The Little Venice of Charolais.”

This French property, just thirty miles from Mâcon, has preserved the warm, welcoming atmosphere of a family home, where you can freely amble through the rooms, even the kitchen. The Maison Doucet’s restaurant, looking out onto the pleasant garden and heated swimming pool, has an ambiance in keeping with its cuisine: understated, elegant, and designed to bring people together. The chef cares deeply about his homeland and showcases local, in-season ingredients. His richly flavored dishes capture his commitment to emotion and shared enjoyment, while his love for the region extends even to the guest-room décor, where contemporary comfort is dotted with interesting, authentic objects from the surrounding area.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Frédéric Doucet
restaurant.description: A rare culinary experience awaits guests in the bright surrounds and contemporary stylings of Restaurant Frédéric Doucet. The chef's approach to cuisine gives pride of place to local produce, especially that harvested from its own vegetable garden. Prepare to be delighted by new flavors that explode in your mouth.

name: Maison Lameloise
description: A true institution of French gastronomy and a charming hotel, the Maison Lameloise has cultivated art de vivre since more than 90 years. In medieval times the cuisine attracted already the Dukes of Burgundy. This long and delightful story is continued by Chef Éric Pras, MOF (Best Craftsman of France), and Frédéric Lamy at the head of the former coach house dating back to the 15th century. The fresh, precise and delicate cuisine of Éric Pras gives this iconic restaurant a new, young image. With the fine tart of Burgundy snails and the potagère of fresh herbs or the suckling veal chop rubbed with marjoram and orange, it pays a delicious tribute to the region and the heritage of the property.

restaurant.name: Maison Lameloise Restaurant
restaurant.description: My cuisine is inspired by tradition, yet dynamic and demonstrates respect for the ingredients. Refined, emotional, and ever so slightly playful, in keeping with the current trend.

name: Manoir de Lan-Kerellec
description: Surrounded by the immense sea and the reefs of the Pink Granite Coast, the Manoir de Lan-Kerellec in Trébeurden is a jewel in Brittany’s crown. From your balcony, drink in the magical panoramic view of the islands of Milliau, Molène and Losquet. Then you might want to take a walk along the coastal "path of the customs officers" to taste the sea spray, or scuba dive for a closer look at the underwater nature reserve. Enjoy your dinner showcasing the produce of the sea in the dining room built in the shape of an upturned boat, a masterpiece by the Compagnons du Devoir.

restaurant.name: Manoir de Lan-Kerellec Restaurant
restaurant.description: The full taste and spirit of the sea are celebrated at the gourmet restaurant of the Manoir de Lan-Kerellec. Chef Anthony Avoine's seafood cuisine is both modern and rooted in tradition. Dining in the restaurant, underneath a sculptural upside-down boat hull, promises you a unique culinary experience.

name: Marlfield House
description: To arrive in this part of Ireland from Dublin, pass through the magical countryside of Wicklow, a county nestled between the sea and the mountains and boasting some of Ireland’s most stunning landscapes. Rosslare port is also very close to Marlfield House. It is in this unspoilt, yet easily accessible region that the Bowe family chose to create their gem of a hotel, a model of conviviality and elegance complete with a rose garden and woodland walks. Dining in the conservatory is delightful. Here, life is about enjoyment, don’t we call the Irish “the Latin people of the North”?

restaurant.name: Marlfield House Conservatory Restaurant
restaurant.description: I count myself lucky to be from such a beautiful part of Ireland and to work with such good produce every day. Our vegetables and herbs are grown in our own kitchen garden and the produce from the sea and the land around us here is Wexford is as good as it gets. Less is more as they say, and that is what we apply to the cooking of our food, we let it speak for itself.

name: Mas de Torrent Hotel & Spa
description: The Mas de Torrent is located in the heart of Empordà, the most authentic and chic region on the Costa Brava, with its beautiful beaches and little medieval villages, in between olive and cypress trees. This 18th century masía delights with its spectacular views, delightful gardens and impeccable, attentive service. The welcoming interior is home to both classical and contemporary suites, some with their own private pool. Two extraordinary restaurants and the Mas Spa make for a magical stay. Don’t miss the treasures in a region so beloved by the artist Salvador Dalí: Cadaqués, Pals, Peratallada and Sant Martí d’Empúries.

restaurant.name: El Mas Restaurant
restaurant.chef: Chef Eugeni de Diego & Marc Cabezas
restaurant.description: Chefs Eugeni de Diego and Marc Cabezas reinterpret the famous ‘land and sea’ spirit of Catalan haute cuisine, made famous by Ferran Adrià. Full of freshness, their dishes harmoniously blend coastal and inland flavors. Nestled amid the property’s carefully tended but wild Mediterranean gardens, the cuisine at El Mas pays tribute to the Empordà landscape that stretches out below.

name: Maslina Resort
description:
It is said that beauty is a promise of happiness; this sentiment takes on its full meaning at the magnificent Maslina Resort. Located in Stari Grad, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, this vast property hosts a number of rooms as well as several suites and villas, all of which boast seafront living spaces with private gardens or pools.
Founded on the principles of ecologically responsible hotel management, the property is dedicated to preserving the pristine environment and cultural heritage of the island of Hvar. Its sustainably built accommodations stretch along the Adriatic Sea, where enchanting vineyards converse with charming olive groves under an exalted azure sky. On warm summer days, this elegant Dalmatian setting, nestled in a pine forest, enjoys a refreshing breeze that brings with it an undeniable sense of calm and repose, offering guests a glimpse of eternal bliss.

restaurant.name: Terra Restaurant
restaurant.description: The minimalist elegance of the Terra restaurant at Maslina Resort is in perfect harmony with its surroundings. The chef combines French techniques and Mediterranean flavors in dishes that celebrate the local terroir, including aromatic herbs and vegetables grown in its kitchen garden.

name: Matca Hotel
description:
In Transylvania, not far from Bran Castle, the Matca Hotel is embraced by an environment of forests and meadows, a breathtakingly beautiful landscape that seems to have escaped the reaches of civilization. This sturdy, traditional farm that blends so comfortably with the surroundings presages a stay that is wholesome and in touch with the land.
The Matca Hotels’ rooms, in large outbuildings with pointed shingle roofs, as well as the two independent villas boasting the added luxury of a private jacuzzi, have refined, carefully considered décor. White walls, light wood, warm fabrics, and breathtaking views of the rolling hills, the summits of the Piatra Craiului Mountains, and the Bucegi Natural Park. Enjoy long walks in the mountains, a dip in the indoor swimming pool with lovely panoramic views, massages, and halotherapy session, followed by friendly, relaxed dinners crafted by a creative chef who reinterprets the recipes of Romanian cuisine with true inspiration.

restaurant.name: STUP Restaurant
restaurant.description: Large bay windows bathe the dining room in light, inviting guests to enjoy creative cuisine alongside magnificent views of the mountainous landscapes of Bucegi Natural Park. The chef, who sources many of their ingredients from the region's farms, offers reinterpretations of traditional Romanian recipes.

name: Meneghetti Wine Hotel & Winery
description: The superb property build in istrian white stone mansion is a foretaste of the country stay to come: the singing of the crickets, the tranquil beauty of the vines and olive trees, the gentle caress of the sea breeze and an entrancing Mediterranean atmosphere. Merely a handful of guest rooms are available in this heavenly setting guaranteeing a secluded stay at Wine Hotel. This will give you total peace and quiet to soak up the sun beside the pool, stroll through the grounds, or sample the restaurant’s delights: healthy, fragrant Mediterranean cuisine which showcases local ingredients (including home-produced olive oil) served with the property’s own wine.

restaurant.name: Meneghetti Restaurant
restaurant.description: The nature that surrounds the property is echoed in the dishes of Meneghetti Restaurant. The chef redefines Istrian cuisine with organic seasonal ingredients, all produced within a 30 mile radius. Wines produced directly on the estate, alongside four varieties of olive oil, complete a gastronomic picture that is as refined as it is delectable.

name: Mirador de Dalt Vila
description: A 19th century Lord’s manor, the Mirador de Dalt Vila was until recently a family property. When it was converted into an intimate and welcoming hotel, none of its historical values were lost. Perched on the heights inside the famous walled city of Dalt Vila – declared a Unesco World Heritage site –, the building offers, as suggested by its name a magnificent panoramic view of the city of Ibiza, its historical port and the never ending horizon of the Mediterranean sea. Far from the bustle of the town, sample the charms of this peaceful haven with its excellent service and highly dedicated staff.
services: main restaurant and common areas are accessible

restaurant.name: Can Font Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Can Font Restaurant, Chef Andoni San Martin reinvents tradition with local ingredients and flavors. From the terrace, his dishes are bold without ever betraying their roots, served against a panoramic view of Ibiza’s old town. Market produce, olive oils and sustainably caught seafood contribute to a creative, uninhibited style of cuisine that pays tribute to the sea, perfectly encapsulating the island’s culinary heritage.

name: Molino de Alcuneza
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: The walls of a 500-year-old flour mill are home today to a delightful family hotel where you can enjoy total peace troubled only by birdsong and the sound of the brook rippling through the grounds. The garden, lovely pool and spa are all invitations in themselves to take it easy in this peaceful ambiance. However, it would be a pity not to explore the Guadalajara area: the historic city of Sigüenza, mediaeval villages, natural reserves, salt mines and rivers in canyons – each more beautiful and interesting than the last. At nightfall, the mill restaurant serves Slow Food cuisine inspired by traditional recipes produced from seasonal, local ingredients.

restaurant.name: Molino de Alcuneza Restaurant
restaurant.description: From his parents, he has learned the dedication of this trade, Michelin Star chef Samual Moreno worked first in an artisanal' way and has also grown by training in the best kitchens.

name: Monaci delle Terre Nere
description: Nestled in the heart of the Etna National Park, an undeniable aura takes hold as you enter the Monaci delle Terre Nere estate. Composed of an 18th-century aristocratic villa and several private houses spread over 25 hectares — a part of which includes a vineyard — this exceptional hotel celebrates the rural charm of a bygone Sicily by combining it with timeless architectural style. Furthermore, it embraces an eco-responsible ethos that is perfectly reflected in its gourmet restaurant Locanda Nerello, where each dish strikes a meticulous balance between the exaltation of the culinary landscape and the authenticity of traditional products. A member of the Slow Food movement, the latter offers a wide range of dishes enhanced all the more by the Etna wines produced on the estate. At the Convivium Bar, discover cocktails concoted from local ingredients and carefully prepared. Staying at Monaci delle Terre Nere generates such incredible energy that, in addition to feeling regenerated, you will be left with the delicious impression of floating like a feather through the air.

restaurant.name: Locanda Nerello Restaurant
restaurant.description: Creativity and sustainability are the culinary pillars of Locanda Nerello restaurant. The chef reinterprets classic Sicilian cuisine, without ever deviating too far from the region's culinary heritage, using seasonal products from nearby farms. This elegant and tasty cuisine can be enjoyed on the terrace in the summer, offering panoramic views, or by the fire in the winter.

name: Monte-Carlo Beach
description: Just a few minutes from the center of Monaco, the Monte-Carlo Beach hotel boldly reinvents the spirit of the French Riviera. Renovated by India Mahdavi in her characteristic vibrant palette, this 1930s gem combines timeless glamour with sustainable living, in the heart of a magnificent pine forest overlooking the Mediterranean. A private beach opens to an Olympic-sized swimming pool lined with vintage parasols and the horizon beyond: here, the blue of the sea is everywhere, inviting you to relax and escape. Guest rooms, which also open to the sea, blend clean lines, graphic art, sensorial textures and other delightful details. Padel enthusiasts can learn or improve their technique on the courts, or join group classes for yoga, pilates and HIIT (high-intensity interval training) sessions. Nature provides inspiration for dishes served across the hotel’s four restaurants, including Elsa Marcel Ravin, its gastronomic destination that focuses on local, ethically produced cuisine. Discover a hedonistic refuge whose authenticity is imbued in every touchpoint.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible
Breakfast À la carte, In-room for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Elsa Marcel Ravin Restaurant
restaurant.description: With breathtaking views towards the horizon and over the principality, Elsa Marcel Ravin restaurant pays tribute to the seasons and local producers with the delicate creativity of chef Marcel Ravin. Her cuisine is inspired by the Mediterranean: its art de vivre (art of living), its pine forests and its unique ecosystem. Diners enjoy refined cuisine in a warm atmosphere.

name: MÖRWALD Hotel am Wagram
description:
The family-run MÖRWALD Hotel am Wagram welcomes you to the picturesque village of Feuersbrunn, in Lower Austria’s Wagram wine region. Modern architecture, a bucolic setting and a gourmet restaurant are the hallmarks of this retreat that’s popular with nature lovers and wine aficionados alike.
Far from the hustle and bustle of Vienna or the Wachau valley, discover the charms of an authentic Austrian inn, off the beaten path. The light wood parquet flooring adds to the brightness of generously sized rooms, featuring contemporary decor and soothing minimalism. The chef-proprietor of the property is passionate about gastronomy and cherishes the culinary heritage of the region, reinventing classics of Austrian cuisine as well as serving more modern dishes. During your stay, be sure to check out the village’s renowned Kellergassen–charming alleys lined with wine cellars–and the Stupa of Wagram, a place of Buddhist meditation. Its majestic silhouette emerges from the vineyards, which can be explored on foot or by bike.

restaurant.name: Mörwald Gourmet "Toni M." Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a timeless, elegant setting, Toni M. restaurant reimagines the traditions of Austria’s culinary heritage. The chef, who also owns the restaurant, reinterprets classic dishes with creativity and finesse, using the finest local ingredients, which the wine list is expertly paired with.

name: Museum Hotel
description: The unusual experience offered by the Museum Hotel, located in Cappadocia, a region of supremely singular beauty, is captivating. The architecture of the hotel, built on former ruins, and partially troglodytic as are so many buildings in Cappadocia, is what first catches your eye. The bare stone décor of the rooms, lounges and the restaurant, which serves delicious Turkish cuisine, is brightened by the bold colours of the carpets, furnishing fabrics and objets d'art. The Museum Hotel houses a fabulous collection of Turkish works of art and antiques. The terrace overlooking the valley offers a 180° panorama of a landscape that is unique in the world.

restaurant.name: Lil'a Restaurant
restaurant.description: The philosophy from farm to table » is our motto. I work with seasonal products and local ingredients from our large garden. I revisit the authentic dishes of Anatolia in a contemporary spirit to offer a cuisine that combines unforgettable tastes and refined presentation.

name: Myconian Ambassador Thalasso Spa
description: Right above the famous Platis Yialos beach and surrounded by gardens, this temple of excellence and wellness has an exceptional location with breathtaking sea views near the finest sandy beaches and Mykonos town. Here you will be delighted to enjoy the exceptional services and charming Myconian hospitality. After exploring the treasures and beautiful landscapes of the Greek islands, indulge in some highly deserved pampering in the superb thalassotherapy centre, which offers massages, baths and divine treatments. Also on site are three indoor seawater pools, each with rigorously monitored salinity and temperature.
services: Individual cooking classes given by the chef

restaurant.name: Efisia Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a setting inspired by the landscape, Efisia, Greek cuisine is expressed with finesse. Chef Ilias Maslaris revisits culinary tradition with a contemporary perspective, showcasing the island's seafood and the abundance of the land. Elegant dishes, confident flourishes and bold flavors make for an experience that’s both authentic and sophisticated.

name: Myconian Korali
description:
This privileged spot that claims the island’s most iconic views onto the old port of Alefkandra and 16th-century windmills on the edge of the city of Mykonos, also happens to be the site of the first hotel built by George Daktylides in 1979. Inherited and renovated by his youngest son, the Korali exudes the essence of boutique luxury that draws both on the spirit of its past as the family’s first guesthouse, and the celebrated service excellence of the Myconian Collection.
Entirely sea-facing, the Korali is the perfect lookout from which to let the hours wash away and be dazzled by the spectacle of the deep blue Aegean Sea. The panoramic terrace invites to sunset drinks that lead into sumptuous dinners prepared with care by the chef from locally sourced produce and the day’s catch.

restaurant.name: BAOS Restaurant
restaurant.description: Intricate flavors, crafted composition and calculated preparation are all in an impeccable presentation. At Baos, inventive gourmet cuisine challenges classicism with differentiating techniques while cultivating groundbreaking freshness in other dishes with the same passion for perfection. Seasonal alternate innovative classic recipes made of the finest raw materials, locally produced and exclusively selected for Korali.

name: Myconian Sunrise
description: Perched on the rocky hills of the southern coast, the Myconian Sunrise overlooks the sun-warmed sands of Agrari Beach, treating guests to an exceptional panoramic sea view and direct access to a private beach. The architecture and interior décor showcase the traditional Cyclades style. The rooms and suites – with their whitewashed walls embracing natural materials like stone and wood – are arranged in a succession of patios and terraces planted with palm trees and bougainvillea. All guest rooms are bathed in the coast’s exquisite light, offer a view of the Aegean Sea, and have sleek décor with a few retro touches from the 1970s. In addition to providing an idyllic setting, the Myconian Sunrise serves guests the finest in Greek cuisine. The creations of Chef Ilias Maslaris are served in the Taverna and the Sunrise Bar, the perfect place to savor a salad or some grilled fish with your toes in the sand.
services: Unheated outdoor pool not specially secured for children

restaurant.name: Almiriki Restaurant
restaurant.description: Authentic Greek cuisine is served at the Almiriki during lunch and dinner. The heir to a 4,000-year-old culinary tradition, Chef Ilias Maslaris reinterprets the region's gastronomic culture through a delicious selection of mezze and dishes made using fresh, local produce, all complemented by olive oil and a beautiful wine list.

name: Myconian Utopia Resort
description: Whitewashed blocs clinging to the rock above Elia Beach, offer stunning views down over the sea and distant islands. This ‘place of perfection’, with its traditional architecture, modern materials, original artwork and contemporary furniture, invites you to enjoy the best that Mykonos has to offer: watersports on the private beach, lounging beside the infinity pool, which appears as if suspended over the sea, or relaxation on the terrace. All hotel guest rooms boast stunning sea views. And a regular shuttle to the picturesque port of Mykonos offers you the opportunity to enjoy island life.

restaurant.name: Pavilion Restaurant
restaurant.description: The cuisine embodies the typical art de vivre of Mykonos. At Pavilion Restaurant, Chef Nikos Moroglou pays homage to the sea and the land, revering each ingredient with creativity and precision. Fresh-caught fish, succulent meats and sunshine-filled vegetables: innately Greek flavors are savored against a backdrop of Mediterranean blue.

name: Palazzo Ripetta
description:
For more than four centuries this former orphanage has stood in the heart of Rome, just a few streets from Piazza di Spagna and Ara Pacis. Palazzo Ripetta today is an elegant hotel that has been in the same family since the 1960s. Cross its threshold to discover the history of the Eternal City through a collection of antique and contemporary works of art that adorns its vast spaces: a majestic sphere, hewn from black and white marble by the artist Arnaldo Pomodoro, welcomes you in the hall, while a third-century Roman sarcophagus, transformed into a fountain, murmurs in the courtyard patio.

In the evening, after enjoying the city's main attractions–which are all within walking distance–savor the serenity of this charming piazzetta over an aperitif. At San Baylon restaurant, the executive chef offers his interpretation of the classics of Roman gastronomy, served in the warm atmosphere of a wood-paneled dining room. Relax in your opulent and refined guest room, featuring contemporary décor that contrasts with age-old materials and invites you to extend la Dolce Vita even your dreams.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: San Baylon Restaurant
restaurant.description: Located in the former dining hall of a 17th-century convent, San Baylon restaurant is a Mecca of taste, where contemporary design blends with the warmth of traditional Roman trattorias. On the menu, discover the classics of Italian cuisine, perfectly executed with the best seasonal produce from the region.

name: Palazzo Seneca
description: Norcia, the town of St. Benedict, nestling in the National Park of the Sibillini Mountains, is the Italian capital of the black truffle. The Palazzo Seneca tells the story of this region from the 16th century onwards, and the Bianconi family can look back on a glorious, more than 160 year-old tradition of hospitality. The best Umbrian artisans worked on the detailed refurbishing of the property and used unique materials and decorative objects to create perfect harmony between colours and lights, tradition and design. The fragrances of the herb garden and the quality of the local products delight in the restaurant Vespasia.

restaurant.name: Vespasia Restaurant
restaurant.description: Everything prepared at Vespasia comes fresh from local farms, carefully chosen for their respect for nature and animal welfare. Each dish draws on authentic Umbrian culinary tradition, enriched with the chef's personal creativity, for a vibrant interpretation of the region's gourmet culture.

name: Palé Hall
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Standing on the shores of Lake Snowdonia, near Bala in North Wales, Palé Hall is a Victorian manor house dating from 1871, surrounded by 15 acres of parkland. This property boasts a rich heritage, having hosted renowned historical figures including Queen Victoria, who stayed here for almost 10 days in 1889, and Prime Minister Winston Churchill, who visited in the 1950s. Inside, expect eclectic decor–including plenty of antiques–and a wonderfully cozy atmosphere.

restaurant.name: The Henry Robertson Dining Room Restaurant
restaurant.description: To me the quality of the ingredient is key, and we are fortunate to enjoy fantastic Welsh produce from a variety of local suppliers. We follow the seasons which means that our food is at its prime in the way that nature intended. I am working closely with the Palé gardeners to create a working kitchen garden, using organic methods and heritage varieties to be the mainstay of our vegetable offering.

name: Park Hotel Kenmare
description: The Heritage town of Kenmare, in mystical Kerry, is home to this Victorian gem. Dating from 1897, the hotel enjoys a splendid location, with manicured gardens running to the lapping shores of Kenmare Bay. This is the Ireland of which you have always dreamed with its rugged coastal drives. Outdoor lovers will enjoy the many mountain, coastal walks and fishing spots in the area. The new extensively refurbished spa offers a wide range of personalised treatments. At the end of an idyllic day you can watch a classic in the hotel’s small private cinema, enjoying a very rare aged, peaty malt.
services: At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Landline Restaurant
restaurant.description: Simple dishes, punctuated by flavors that reflect the environment: welcome to the Landline. As a guest of chef James O`Sullivan, diners enjoy a marriage of local products from land and sea. Taste treasures from the hotel's vegetable garden and the surrounding region in a dining room that exudes elegance.

name: Park Hotel Sonnenhof
description: This remarkable family hotel, surrounded by a bucolic park, offers panoramic views of Vaduz Castle, the Alps and neighbouring vineyards. Only a stone’s throw from the hustle and bustle of the town centre, this is a piece of heaven on earth for all those in search of exceptional culinary experiences as well as dolce far niente in a unique atmosphere, guaranteeing a fabulous stay in the heart of Europe. The rooms and suites boast extravagant decor, designed in various themes and styles, and offer stunning views. Sonnenhof is an ideal location to let go and enjoy a sensual weekend; you will feel like an eagle flying over the romantic Rhine Valley and its castles.
services: In at least one room at extra cost
