restaurant.name: Marée Restaurant
restaurant.description: I like to compose my dishes with the greatest simplicity. With fresh ingredients that are authentic, tasty, seasonal and always the best quality.

name: Petra Segreta Resort & Spa
description: Tiny traditional stone houses, tucked away in five hectares of forest, in the shade of juniper bushes and holm oaks, have been converted into sophisticated rooms where you can relax. The air is fragrant with the scents of the garrigue, and there are delightful views to be enjoyed over the Gulf of Arzachena and the Archipelago of Maddalena. The wellness centre, housed in natural granite grottos, offers a treatments specialising in Oriental techniques. The Italian regional cuisine served in the restaurant is light and appetising. Unless lunch on board a boat on a private excursion on the clear waters of the Costa Smeralda exercises an even greater attraction.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Il Fuoco Sacro
restaurant.description: The setting of Il Fuoco Sacro is particularly enchanting, offering breathtaking views of the coast, while the modern Mediterranean cuisine conceived here is just as compelling. On the menu: local ingredients, including aromatic herbs grown on the hotel's own farm, which also supplies vegetables, cheeses and some of the meat it serves.

name: Pic
description: Taking inspiration from the world of perfume, and drawing on the aromatic composition of each ingredient, to create sauces of astonishing complexity; awakening childhood memories by marrying orange blossom with carrot to enhance its flavor; or constructing a dessert like a monochrome painting: Anne-Sophie Pic's approach to cuisine is a sensitive and evocative one. Enriched by her travels and personal encounters and conveying emotions she wants to share, this celebrated chef’s dishes are rich storytelling creations. In unison, the hotel is a refined destination, contemporary yet warm, and surrounded by delightful Mediterranean gardens where you can swim surrounded by olive trees and roses.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Pic
restaurant.description: "At the heart of the Maison PIC, in Valence, I experiment with combinations of flavours. In some dishes, I present up to five aromatic groups, while making each taste perceptible and legible. All the elements count. In the blue lobster with red berries recipe, I propose discovering the side of smoked bonito with red berry dashi, a few iodine notes in the broth but also tangy flavours present in the strawberry-barberry chutney. I came up with this recipe ten years ago, but it continues to evolve as I work on it. It is this game of balance and audacity that interests me and I wish to present with my teams."

name: Pieve Aldina
description: Nestled in the heart of the Chianti region, the Pieve Aldina embodies the spirit of a Sienese hamlet. This hotel, located on the Chiantigiana, the picturesque wine route that links Siena to Florence, invites you to fully enjoy Tuscany’s charms. Surrounded by vineyards, cypress and olive trees, the main building dates back to the 10th century and was once a summer residence to the bishops of Siena. Most of the historic building’s terracotta floors, beamed ceilings and charming archways are original. They highlight the local style of this authentic address beautifully. The region’s rich culinary heritage is showcased in the two restaurants, both with terraces, including the oenoteca. After a day well spent exploring the area’s wine cellars or taking part in a cooking class to learn the art of making delicious fresh pasta, you can enjoy a relaxing massage and lounge by the pool overlooking the valley.

restaurant.name: Le rondini restaurant
restaurant.description: The Florentine chef of Le Rondini reworks traditional Tuscan cuisine with finesse, highlighting the culinary treasures of the region. From its wild herbs to its colorful vegetables, truffles, fresh pasta, beef and Panzanella salad, rediscover these delicious ingredients accompanied by the best wines from Chianti.

name: Post Lech Arlberg
description: The historic inn Gasthof „Post“ is located in the centre of Lech, a world-renowned winter sports resort. The atmosphere of the “Post” is marked by the long family history of the place, its country house charm and local antiques as well as loving attention-to-detail in the décor, which create a welcoming ambience. Culinary delicacies await in the restaurants that have won several awards for their outstanding cuisine. Relaxation is complete at the Post Beauty & Spa and its spectacular panoramic pool, with stunning mountain vistas and treatment suites that are wonderfully located in the heart of the beautiful Ebra garden.

restaurant.name: Jägerstube Restaurant
restaurant.description: The warm, rustic yet refined atmosphere of the Jägerstube perfectly complements the chef's cuisine. Inspired by traditional Austrian recipes and regional and seasonal products, he reinterprets them using modern techniques and creative ideas from around the world.

name: Prestonfield House
description: Prestonfield House, with its towering Georgian façade, stands regally on a 20-acre estate shaded by ancient trees, land where a few Highland cows graze peacefully, yet all just minutes from downtown Edinburgh. This authentic Scottish manor is an urban oasis, a 17th-century residence that has had only three owner families in the more than three centuries it has stood on this site. Today, it belongs to James Thomson, who has lovingly preserved the property’s treasures: the ornate Cordoba leather panels, Mortlake tapestries, antique silks and velvets, artwork, fireplaces, period furniture, and other gracious details. As the ideal place to experience your first taste of the true Scotland, Prestonfield House offers the splendor of tradition with the warmth of a genuine Scottish welcome. While soaking up the sumptuous ambiance, enjoy the must-be-tasted afternoon tea, the treasures of the wine cellar, a fine collection of malt whiskies, and farm-to-table cuisine at the Rhubarb Restaurant.

restaurant.name: Rhubarb Restaurant
restaurant.description: The name of the property's restaurant, Rhubarb, recalls how the Prestonfield estate was the first in Scotland to cultivate the botanical in the 18th century. It is always served alongside the finest produce from neighboring farmers, in a farm-to-table cuisine skillfully prepared by Chef John McMahon.

name: PURS Luxury Boutique Hotel & Restaurant
description: On the left bank of the Rhine between Cologne and Frankfurt, Germany, discover a property that blends history with modernity: PURS Luxury Boutique Hotel & Restaurant combines elegance, refined aesthetics, gastronomy and a warm welcome. Located in a former residence of the Archbishops of Cologne, this boutique hotel has just eleven rooms and suites as well as a renowned restaurant featuring contemporary interior design by Axel Vervoordt. The Belgian designer and collector of antiques has subtly married the rich heritage of the hotel with works of art, creating a contemporary style that simultaneously makes guests feel at home and conjures a haven of tranquility. This canvas is home to the creative cuisine of chef Peter Fridén.

restaurant.name: PURS Restaurant
restaurant.description: Art is most certainly on the menu at PURS-you'll see it on the walls as well as on each and every plate. Chef Peter Fridén's dishes reflect the property's history, with a bold and perfectly mastered fusion of Japanese, Scandinavian and French influences. The cellar is up to the task for such multicultural cuisine, featuring a wide selection of sake and kombucha alongside more than a thousand vintages.

name: Quadrille
description: On the shores of the Baltic Sea, the port city of Gdynia is in full resurgence. Within one of its magnificent parks, Quadrille sits in a small 18th-century palace to which the Górski family has brought new life. They imagined a decor that would surpass the ordinary, inspired by the world of Alice in Wonderland. The tunnel that connects the two sections of the hotel invites guests to pass from one side of the mirror to the other, where the rooms, each bearing the name of a famous writer and his novel, are found. Their choice for originality has not diminished the quality of the service, with the magic of the atmosphere evident in every detail. The restaurant, carried by the enthusiasm of its young chef, invites you to follow the white rabbit into the dunes of the Pomeranian Voivodeship.

restaurant.name: Biały Królik Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Biały Królik (which means white rabbit'), Polish culinary tradition comes in for a contemporary, almost poetic treatment. The chef draws inspiration from traditional Pomeranian recipes and produce to create a bold yet refined style of cuisine. Each dish tells a story, to be enjoyed in a fairytale-like setting.

name: Quinta Nova Winery House
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: In the heart of the Douro Valley, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Quinta Nova Winery House overlooks terraced vineyards and the river below. On this 300-acre, family-run estate, the property produces its own Port and Douro wines in perfect harmony with the landscape. In this sympathetically restored 18th-century mansion, the rooms boast contemporary, elegant decor, conducive to relaxation. The warm and intimate atmosphere immerses guests in the gentle pace of Portuguese life in a truly inspiring setting. In the kitchen, Chef André Carvalho showcases local produce with a contemporary interpretation of regional culinary classics. Quinta Nova Winery House also boasts a private villa in the Dão region, about 75 miles away: this offers a chance to discover ‘Portugal’s Burgundy,’ one of the country's oldest wine-growing regions, set amid the mountains.
services: at least one room and the main restaurant are accessible

restaurant.name: Terraçu's Restaurant
restaurant.description: We believe that creating wine and food is also about creating unique moments. Discover our restaurant Terraçu's Winery Restaurant and you will see that you have found a true slice of paradise in Douro Valley.

name: Relais Christine
description: Some places embody the soul of a city, of a mythical neighbourhood. This is the case of the Relais Christine, a luxurious boutique hotel with an ultra central location on the Left Bank in Paris. In Saint-Germain-des-Prés, this is the ideal place for a staycation or Parisian city getaway. Guests come here to soak up the capital as immortalised by the illustrious figures of the Latin Quarter. From the street, this private home looks like the perfect secret hideaway with its small paved courtyard, its swings and ivy climbing up its walls. Designed by interior designer Laura Gonzalez, the decor of this former mansion brilliantly combines antiques with touches of modernity. Most of the 48 rooms and suites feature high ceilings, and all are adorned with magnificent wallpaper and original fabrics. Some of them even boast terraces directly overlooking a garden, making the Relais Christine a lush setting, a unique haven of peace, only a few minutes’ walk from the banks of the Seine and Notre-Dame Cathedral. And for an exclusive moment of wellness, the Guerlain Spa and its sauna await under the vaults of a former 13th-century Cistercian abbey — an enchanting setting for enjoying lavishly wonderful treatments.
services: In at least one room and common areas
at least one room and common areas are accessible

name: Relais de la Poste
description: This 19th century mansion combines all that is great about the southwest of France and the Landes, the country of dunes and wide open spaces. The scent of the sea pines drifts over those relaxing by the swimming pool or on the white sandy beaches, only a few kilometres from the Relais de la Poste, or those surfing the famous waves of the Landes coast. Chef Jean Coussau concocts with great talent flavorful gourmet recipes originating from the region between the Basque Country and Bordeaux, with its wealth of exceptional products, like Adour lamprey, hot duck foie gras with grapes, sand asparagus from the Landes or Gillardeau oysters on a bed of chanterelles.

restaurant.name: Relais de la Poste Restaurant
restaurant.description: I was born near the sand dunes of Landes, surrounded by producers, farmers, gatherers, hunters, and fishermen, and I cook with all the produce from this Land of Cockaigne. This produce picked and farmed from land and sea is my daily inspiration.

name: Relais Il Falconiere & Spa
description:
Cypresses, umbrella pines, olive trees and vineyards as far as the eye can see; stone buildings and terracotta-colored walls; Relais Il Falconiere & Spa somehow concentrates all the sensations and beauty of Tuscany in a single destination. Located halfway between Arezzo and Perugia, near the beautiful Etruscan city of Cortona, the property of Silvia and Benedetto Baracchi is a rare property which combines discretion and refinement. Arranged around a 17th-century country house, and spread between an old farmhouse and a new contemporary villa, rooms and suites each have their own characteristics and unique decor.
Il Falconiere restaurant occupies the former conservatory, where chef duo Silvia Baracchi and Richard Titi present their local and sustainable approach to gastronomy. The olive oil and wine produced at the heart of the family property can be found in the treatments provided at Thesan Etruscan Spa. Well-being here comes in all of its many forms.

restaurant.name: Il Falconiere Restaurant
restaurant.description: Located in the former conservatory of Villa Baracchi, gourmet restaurant Il Falconiere has elegant interiors and a panoramic terrace, covered by a glass roof, with views overlooking Cortona and its undulating landscape. The sunshine-filled, creative cuisine of chef Silvia Baracchi pays homage to Tuscany: carefully selected local and seasonal produce take pride of place on the plate. The property also offers a large selection of wine, including 12 vintages from the Baracchi family's own wine estate.

name: Restaurant Serge Vieira
description: Working as a duo, Serge and Marie-Aude Vieira have mastered the art of marrying contrasts to create harmony. Enamoured with Château de Couffour, they added a playful twist to its medieval charm by creating a sleek angular building to house their restaurant. This same creative freedom is expressed by the ever-evolving cuisine, which finds its roots in the riches of Auvergne. A native of the region, Serge Vieira passionately works to blend the flavours and textures of the products he knows so well. And as he once wished to be a designer, he puts great effort into the composition of his dishes. A stay in one of the château’s rooms promises you will enjoy this multisensory experience to the fullest.

restaurant.name: Restaurant Serge Vieira
restaurant.description: Enhancing a raw product, like a stone mason, to create a dish of apparent simplicity, to cultivate the field of possibilities without restriction, to return to the taste of what is essential in the respect of our local produce... A life is made to experience emotions and above all we want to be those people who pass it on.

name: Saint James Paris
description: In the 16th arrondissement of the French capital, Saint James Paris lovingly cultivates a world of unmatched refinement and art de vivre. Close to the Arc de Triomphe and Avenue Foch, this exceptional hotel reveals a discreet character imbued with pastoral charm and an aura unlike any other. Graced with 5,000 square metres of greenery, it holds court amidst marvellous gardens made all the more remarkable by the landscape designer Xavier de Chirac. This former private mansion is now bridging its romantic neoclassical soul with modernity, thanks to the talents of interior designer Laura Gonzalez, who has achieved a perfect symbiosis of plant life and art deco. The decorative contrasts exhibit a rare elegance, as evidenced by the fifty rooms and suites which are at once chic and poetic, grandiose and intimate. At the Bellefeuille gourmet restaurant, Chef Grégory Garimbay pays tribute to French gastronomy and the richness of its terroirs by honouring the raw produce he works with sincerity and passion. A stay at the Saint James Paris is a true parenthesis of well-being, which is confirmed in the Guerlain spa: a world of relaxation, with areas dedicated to treatments, a hammam, a sauna, a Jacuzzi, a fitness room and an indoor pool bathed in natural light.

restaurant.name: Bellefeuille Restaurant
restaurant.description: The earthy, nature-inspired cuisine of chef Grégory Garimbay reflects his dedication to responsible gastronomy: plants take precedence over meat and seafood in the interests of the planet. Accessible exclusively by Club members at lunchtimes, Bellefeuille restaurant is accessible to all in the evening.

name: Saint-Delis Hôtel & Spa
description: Home to Henri de Saint-Delis during the last century, the painter’s soulful spirit lives on in this private mansion located in Honfleur, Normandy — a city whose pictorial sensibility is apparent in the property’s elegant garden and rooms of noble materials, bespoke furniture and steam showers. Art, in its most authentic and singular form, is found everywhere here: in the wood, the stone, the marble. Each element adds to this living painting whose beauty is sublimated by the natural light that illuminates every corner. In the heart of the historic center of this iconic city, the timeless charm and intimate atmosphere of the Saint-Delis Hotel & Spa blend beautifully with the local cultural heritage. From the picturesque houses and small streets to the many art galleries and traditional restaurants near the port, your days will surely be filled with wonderment and a renewed sense of happiness.
services: In at least one room and common areas at extra cost

name: San Montano Resort & Spa
description: Perched on a rocky hill overlooking the beach and marina of Lacco Ameno, San Montano Resort & Spa offers an elegant island retreat away from the crowds. The De Siano family cultivates the fine art of Italian hospitality every day to welcome guests. Shades of blue from the sky and sea echo throughout the decor of its lounges, rooms and suites, as well as in the refined table settings. Everything here seems designed to showcase its exceptional 360-degree panorama, taking in the islands of Capri and Procida, the Gulf of Naples and Mount Vesuvius beyond. From the terraces, the three restaurants of San Montano Resort & Spa highlight the abundance of the sea with dishes typical of Ischia. These moments of culinary joy are complemented by sublime well-being experiences in its nine pools or under the expert hands of therapists at the Ocean Blue Spa.

restaurant.name: La Veranda Restaurant
restaurant.description: At La Veranda restaurant, Chef Francesco Scotto Di Minico reinterprets classic island dishes, served in a stunning dining room that offers panoramic views over the landscape through floor-to-ceiling glass. A native of Ischia, he blends regional tradition–and locally sourced produce–with culinary modernity. Fish and seafood are served alongside vegetables from the restaurant’s own kitchen garden and a fine selection of Campanian wines.

name: Schlosshotel Burg Schlitz
description: Burg Schlitz is a noble country residence and the perfect place if your dream is to live the castle lifestyle. This classical palace is tucked away from the stress of everyday life in 400 acres of landscaped parkland, only an hour and a half hour from Berlin and Hamburg. True connoisseurs will appreciate the meals served in the gourmet restaurant "Wappen-Saal", the Café-Brasserie “Louise” or the Davidoff Cigar Lounge. The very intimate wellness & spa lounge is supremely elegant and invites to relaxation. It is a tiny piece of heaven on earth where you can relax in an ambiance of delightful good taste. Life can’t get any better than that!

restaurant.name: Gourmetrestaurant Wappensaal
restaurant.description: 41-year-old Maik Albrecht comes to us with plenty of international experience and great passion for grande cuisine. At the legendary Traube Tonbach Hotel, he spent 7 years stirring things up as Master Chef at the Köhlerstube and Bauernstube restaurants. At the Palazzo Harald Wohlfahrt in Hamburg, he wowed hundreds of guests when he was head of the kitchen there. Finally, he sailed the seas for 12 years as Executive Chef for SeaCloud Cruises. But now, he has moored his boat in the castle kitchen of Burg Schlitz, so now it's your turn to be dazzled with his culinary ideas, dear guests!

name: Schlosshotel Chastè
description: Schlosshotel Chastè is brimming with character and set in particularly peaceful surroundings, at the foot of Tarasp Castle in the Engadin, a region renowned for its national parks and stunning mountain landscapes. This former farm turned into a modern and comfortable inn, managed by Daniela, Rudolf and Gian-Andrea Pazeller, embodies all that is gentle about Switzerland: the warmly welcoming living and guest rooms are panelled in Alpine pine in traditional fashion. The spa offers a variety of regenerating saunas. Its inventive cuisine is served with superb wines from the cellar in bright, friendly dining rooms.

restaurant.name: Bocca Fina restaurant
restaurant.description: I prepare my dishes using local, seasonal products as found in the Engadine at an altitude of 1450 meters. Basing them on tradition and local cuisine, I adapt them to the modern world.

name: Sheen Falls Lodge
description: Surrounded by purple heather-covered mountains, Kenmare is one of the spots in Ireland that the Irish themselves like best. Sheen Falls Lodge is not only perfectly placed for exploring the southwest, the Ring of Beara, Ring of Kerry and the famous Killarney Lakes, but it is also known for its piano-jazz ambience, its collection of old Irish whiskeys, and the dishes prepared with the salmon caught in the nearby river. There are unrivalled views of Kenmare Bay, the McGuillicuddy Reeks and cascading Sheen waterfalls. All of this, combined with outstanding Irish hospitality and Celtic legends, creates a sense of magic that pervades.

restaurant.name: The Falls Restaurant
restaurant.description: Our unique flair and own style of modern Irish cooking is constantly evolving, with innovative dishes continually exciting both regular guests and new diners. I strive to bring the very best out of my talented team and, by keeping one eye on the marketplace, competition and nurturing progression, we want to keep producing quality dishes that satisfy every guest.

name: Son Brull Hotel & Spa
description: In this former hillside monastery in Mallorca, asceticism lives on in the décor, where white walls shine forth against the deep blue sky. The spa is a water and teakwood paradise using essential oils derived from the island flora. The swimming pool, a chic camping ground, provides large white parasols to ensure deckchairs remain in the shade. The rooms offer views of the sea, the olive tree orchards and the bay of Pollença. Organic produce, including olive oil, wine, citrus fruits and vegetables, is cultivated on the grounds and served in the restaurant. There is little inclination to venture out of this pleasant hotel, unless it is to head for the beaches or the golf course.

restaurant.name: 3/65 Restaurant
restaurant.description: At 3/65 Restaurant, Chef Pedro G Martin serves ever-evolving, locally sourced cuisine. Three menus-one of them vegetarian-make the most of the finca's harvests and Mallorca's artisanal savoir-faire. This exacting culinary philosophy is showcased through the food and wine pairings, too: Over 200 wines are available, including a hand-picked array of local labels.

name: SPA-HOTEL Jagdhof
description: Facing the impressive Stubai glacier, the Jagdhof offers a spacious welcome with its rooms and vast suites (up to 115 m2), all beautifully decorated. Numerous activities are available, and you can enjoy fine dining in a unique, convivial atmosphere. The Vitalwelt spa of 3,000 m², one of the best in Austria, offers twenty different types of bath, relaxation and sauna. A modern fitness room and a programme of activities are likewise available. The gourmet restaurant and the à-la-carte restaurant “Hubertusstube” feature local and regional products. The wine cellar boasts further culinary delights.

restaurant.name: Gourmet Restaurant Hubertusstube Jagdhof
restaurant.description: The elegant Tyrolean decor and warm atmosphere of Hubertusstube perfectly complement its cuisine, which celebrates seasonal produce from the region. The chef pays homage to the natural beauty of the landscape in highly finessed gourmet dishes, which bring to life the gastronomic heritage of the Austrian Alps.

nearestAirport: Innsbruck 25 km, Salzburg 174 km, Munich 204 km

name: Stikliai Hotel
description: The ancient city of Vilnius is a Unesco World Heritage site and had a profound influence on the cultural and architectural development of a large part of Europe. Despite the invasions and destruction that it suffered in its history, it still has many renaissance, gothic, baroque and classical architectural gems. “Stikliai” is Lithuanian for glass blowers and this hotel is ideally situated in the trade quarter, where, from the 17th to the 19th centuries, glass blowers had their workshops. Today, the Stikliai Hotel is a delightful little house, with a winter garden. The attentive staff will leave you with a lasting memory of a warm and welcoming Lithuania.

restaurant.name: Stikliai Restaurant
restaurant.description: Stikliai Hotel's restaurant has been attracting gourmets for almost 40 years with its refined cuisine. Its menu focuses on local produce: most of the vegetables, fruit, berries and herbs served come from a nearby farm.

name: Stockholm Stadshotell
description: Located in Södermalm, a fashionable district known for its boutiques and art galleries, Stockholm Stadshotell is the ideal place from which to soak up local life and explore the Swedish capital. It is housed in a magnificent 19th-century residence, listed as a national heritage site. Its owners, experts in renovation, art and design, have conceived an intimate and refined atmosphere, where décor combines soft tones, artisanal furniture and works of art. This identity is also expressed through photographic exhibitions curated by the resident artistic director. Traditional, locally inspired gourmet cuisine is served in the former chapel, while more informal dining can be found in the bistro and bar, whose tables overflow into the hotel courtyard. In the wellness area, you will find the essentials of Swedish spas, including a sauna, ice bath and sensory shower.

restaurant.name: Matsalen Restaurant
restaurant.description: Just a short distance away, the beautifully restored former chapel is now home to Matsalen, the hotel’s acclaimed gourmet restaurant. It offers cuisine with Nordic flavors and global influences, inspired by seasonal local ingredients, under the guidance of acclaimed chef Henrik Wahlberg. His unique menus are composed as an ode to the region, with carefully selected produce paired with herbs grown in his own kitchen garden.

nearestAirport: Stockholm Arlanda Airport and Bromma Stockholm Airport

name: Storfjord Hotel
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description:
This grand establishment of dark-timbered buildings, typical of traditional Norwegian architecture, is set upon a hill overlooking the fjord of the same name. Its log walls shelter warmly welcoming interiors: Comfortable furniture, antiques, and objets d’art create a chic, rustic ambiance in the living rooms and library, as well as in the rooms and suites blessed with spectacular views of the fjord and surrounding forest.
Whatever the season, Storfjord offers an authentically Norwegian stay: skiing in wintertime, cruises and fishing trips on the fjord, kayaking adventures, scenic hikes, bike rides, Nordic wildlife observation, and, of course, relaxing moments at the wellness center with panoramic views of the landscape.
Fine dining, too, is part of the experience, ensured by Florian Harnisch: This environmentally enlightened chef selects ingredients that are picked, fished, or aged within a few miles of the property. His expressive, down-to-earth cuisine reflects the rugged beauty of the region.

restaurant.name: Storfjord Hotel Restaurant
restaurant.description: The cuisine at Storfjord Hotel is profoundly connected to the land: Chef Florian Harmish focuses on ingredients picked, fished or matured from within just a few kilometers. Each dish tells the story of a season, a producer or a facet of the landscape, in a delicious dialogue between nature, local savoir-faire and Nordic simplicity.

name: Taubenkobel
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: On crossing the threshold of Taubenkobel, you will be welcomed by the owners, the Eselböck family, in the unique atmosphere of this cosmopolitan haven in a Burgenland village. This charming 19th century vintner’s house is dedicated to well-being, the appreciation of art and love of the surrounding nature of the Pannonian lowlands. The remarkable cuisine of Chef Alain Weissgerber will enchant your palate. Created in 1984, the former farm hosts not only one of the best culinary addresses but is also a romantic hotel, a grocery store in the middle of a beautiful garden that will seduce you.

restaurant.name: Restaurant taubenkobel
restaurant.description: The aromas of food cooking on an open fire tantalize guests' senses. Dishes center around herbs, meats and artisanal organic produce from the Pannonian region, in cuisine that's both authentic and creative. Rarely have local produce and culinary refinement been so successfully blended as showcased at Taubenkobel restaurant.

name: Terra - The Magic Place
description:
One day, a guest described Terra Hotel as “the magic place.” The name stuck, as the magic can be felt everywhere: in the serenity of the landscapes and the purity of the alpine air, and in the sincere hospitality of the hosts and their creative cuisine, inspired by nature. It offers a complete culinary  change of scene, immersed in an enchanting setting.
At over 5,000 feet above sea level, not far from Bolzano, the contemporary architecture of the Terra Hotel-The Magic Place blends harmoniously with its wider environment. Inside, massive tree trunks, natural wood and soft colors create an elegant, contemporary yet warm atmosphere that instantly channels a sense of relaxation. Its dining room–bathed in light streaming through its floor-to-ceiling windows–reveals spectacular panoramic views of the Dolomites, a hiker's paradise. The perfect setting to appreciate the chef's distinctive gastronomic score, which changes with the seasons and is complemented by fine wines carefully selected by his sister Gisela, an expert sommelier. Both perpetuate the family legacy in a place that feels like home... beneath a carpet of stars.

restaurant.name: Terra Restaurant
restaurant.description: Almost suspended between the sky and the mountains, Terra restaurant offers inventive cuisine that feels profoundly connected to these exceptional natural surroundings. The chef, passionate about wild herbs, creates elegant, fragrant dishes that reveal the subtleties of seasonal Alpine ingredients.

name: The Amauris Vienna
description: The majestic 19th-century facade of The Amauris stands proudly on the main boulevard of the Ringstrasse, encapsulating all of the charm of Vienna’s historical centre. A stone’s throw away from the opera and just 150 metres from the Musikverein, in the bustling heart of the city, The Amauris is both an elegant and contemporary treasure, combining the old with the new throughout its interior. The beautiful original elevator provides access to the well-appointed rooms and suites from which you can gaze upon the cheerful procession of red and white tramways. The hotel’s crown jewel, the Opera suite, features a balcony with an exclusive view of the famous state opera. In the evening, the gourmet restaurant, Glasswing, offers a perfect mix of innovation and tradition in which only the best products are used; be it those from the region or the sea, the main focus is always on delivering the highest of quality.

restaurant.name: Glasswing Restaurant
restaurant.description: With my love for tradition and an urge to experiment, I blend the cultural heritage of Vienna with bold new flavours. Dining here, you'll experience meals with a perfect mix of innovation and tradition, worthy of Vienna. Whether the ingredients come from the nearby region or the sea, our main focus is always on the highest quality.

name: The Bath Priory
description: Ivy-clad and golden-hued, The Bath Priory is a refined country house hotel nestled in four acres of tranquil grounds, just moments from the cultural treasures of Bath. Built in 1835 and steeped in Georgian charm, the former priory exudes warmth and elegance. Each room is named after a flower–Lilac, Heather, Hyacinth–and thoughtfully styled with a blend of antique furnishings and contemporary fabrics. Set discreetly behind a tree-lined residential street, it offers the rare experience of peaceful seclusion in a UNESCO World Heritage city. Sheltered by stone walls, the fragrant herb garden hints at the culinary delights to come, while the Garden Spa by L’OCCITANE promises the most restorative of returns. After a day exploring Roman baths and Georgian streets, indulge in afternoon tea among curated artworks in the lounge or unwind in a sanctuary   where calm, culture, and comfort converge.

restaurant.name: The Priory Restaurant
restaurant.description: At gourmet restaurant The Priory, a view of the hotel's flower garden is a sweet prelude to the expressive cuisine of chef Jauca Catalin. Discover inventive and seasonal dishes, guided by his passion for French culinary art and embellished with delicate Asian touches.

name: The Chelsea Townhouse
description: Nestled in London's prestigious Royal Borough of Kensington & Chelsea, The Chelsea Townhouse has created spacious rooms and suites across three neighboring redbrick Victorian properties. They are part of an identical enclave of buildings surrounding Cadogan Gardens, one of a small number of private parks in the capital normally only accessible by residents with a key. However, in a highly exclusive arrangement, the hotel’s Drawing Room and select suites offer guests direct access to the leafy gardens. Meticulously restored, the hotel seamlessly blends British charm with contemporary elegance. With marble fireplaces, gilded mirrors, old engravings, plush carpet, and eclectic juxtapositions–an Art Nouveau pedestal table paired with an Empire dressing table, say–the decor evokes a cozy and quirky British style, making the townhouse feel like a particularly high-end guest house. Under its high ceilings, large windows offer abundant natural light and views over the trees in the square. Chelsea's charms are not far beyond, with iconic shopping destinations including Sloane Street and The King’s Road just footsteps away, and a host of cultural attractions including the Saatchi Gallery, Buckingham Palace and the Natural History Museum close by. The service is as discreet as it is attentive, and with its blend of timeless charm, modern luxury, and access to London's finest attractions, The Chelsea Townhouse offers an unparalleled experience in the heart of the city.

restaurant.name: The Garden Room at the Chelsea Townhouse Restaurant
restaurant.description: In a charming London setting, The Garden Room effortlessly captures the elegance of contemporary dining, made with seasonal produce. The chef Nigel Mendham tips his hat to tradition while skillfully reinventing classic English gourmet cuisine in a fresh, bold way.

name: The Idle Rocks
description: Waking up in a bright, spacious room overlooking the sea, with the sound of waves lapping gently against the shore below, is an invitation to enjoy a morning swim, take a boat trip, join a sailing lesson on the dinghy or visit one of Cornwall’s internationally renowned gardens. Owners Karen and David Richards have transformed this historic building in the enchanting port of St Mawes, instilling it with the soul of a wonderful holiday home. On the waterfront terrace, guests can savour the Cornish sunshine and the freshest fish caught by local dayboat fishermen. Refined, imaginative and sustainable cuisine with genuine hospitality in a relaxed setting — no wonder the staff ’s joie de vivre is so contagious!

restaurant.name: The Reef Knot Restaurant
restaurant.description: A veritable paradise for epicures, The Reef Knot Restaurant gives pride of place to fresh and seasonal Cornish produce. The chef emphasizes respect for the environment and support for local suppliers, while guests can enjoy British cuisine in a unique setting-at the water's edge as the sun sets.

name: The Torridon
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Set on a 58-acre estate, The Torridon was once the hunting lodge of Earl William KingNoel. This Victorian mansion’s tall sash windows open out onto the deep blue of Loch Torridon and the breathtaking beauty of the Highlands, inviting you to explore the wonders of the surrounding nature. Inside, from the entrance hall to the Eilean Chasgaig Room–a dining room dedicated to Queen Victoria–you’ll feel an atmosphere of serenity and rustic elegance. This impression is enhanced by the patina of the wood-paneled library and the Whisky Bar, which boasts an extraordinary collection of single malts. The hotel’s few rooms and suites, spread across the main house and its former stables, combine traditional decor with touches of originality. At Restaurant 1887, Chef Danny Young serves creative Scottish cuisine that celebrates the richness of local produce and vegetables grown in the kitchen garden, just behind the house.

restaurant.name: 1887 Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the comfortable, wood-paneled dining room of 1887 Restaurant, guests are invited to savor the creative conceptions of Chef Danny Young. His Scottish cuisine pairs farm- and garden-grown produce with freshly caught seafood, showcasing everything from hand-harvested Orkney scallops and wild Shetland turbot to Scottish blackberries.

name: The Vineyard Hotel & Spa
description:
CURRENTLY CLOSED FOR RENOVATION – REOPENING WINTER 2026

The Vineyard Hotel & Spa has been described as a temple to Californian wine. Just outside Newbury, an hour from London, it definitely deserves its reputation. The cellar holds wines from both the New and Old Worlds, as well as award-winning estate wines from owner Sir Peter Michael. The hotel is also celebrated for its dining experience, using local and sustainable seasonal produce to deliver a blend of old-world tastes and new-world approaches and techniques. As if the bar had not been set high enough, the traditional guest rooms, spacious contemporary suites, a spa and an enviable private art collection conspire to make The Vineyard Hotel & Spa the perfect retreat for gourmands and the culinary.

restaurant.name: The Vineyard Restaurant
restaurant.description: For the chef at The Vineyard, a truly memorable meal depends on three essential components: fresh and seasonal products, inspired flavor pairings and delightful wine to accompany. To deliver on this promise, his contemporary cuisine combines French and English influences and the wine cellar offers an astonishing 30,000 bottlings.

name: The Xara Palace
description: This 17th century palazzo is inextricably linked to the medieval city of Mdina, its walls forming part of the bastion walls that surround the former capital of Malta, with its remarkable baroque architecture. All rooms have been individually designed with romantic décor, original paintings, antique tapestries and furnishings. They are reminiscent of the noble origins of the Xara Palace and boast spectacular 180 degree panoramic views of Malta. Enjoy attentive service and gastronomic delights at the award-winning de Mondion restaurant, or dine at Trattoria A.D. 1530, situated on the charming Piazza.
services: Group cooking classes given by the chef

restaurant.name: The de Mondion Restaurant
restaurant.description: Xara Palace presents an exceptional restaurant with breathtaking views of the silent city’s golden rooftops. Chef Clint Grech reinterprets Mediterranean flavors in refined cuisine, combining Maltese culinary heritage with more contemporary inspirations. Each dish reflects the elegance of the setting, with meticulous presentation and pure emotion.

name: The Yeatman
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Sit back on the terrace of your room and admire the panoramic view: beneath your eyes, Porto, a Unesco World Heritage site, and the sinuous curves of the Douro river. The Yeatman, located in the heart of Vila Nova de Gaia–the historical headquarters of the Port wine houses–owes its name to a family of British traders who are still active: this legacy is told through the décor of the rooms and a great collection of Portuguese wines. The chef showcases contemporary gastronomy, with food and wine pairing an important part of the dining experience. The final touch is the choice of treatments available at The Yeatman Wine Spa–an oasis of harmony and well-being.

restaurant.name: The Restaurant
restaurant.description: “The tasting menu takes you on a journey of Portuguese and international flavours to create a truly sensory experience.

name: Thornbury Castle
description: With its aura of spectacular otherworldliness, Thornbury Castle stands proudly in the county of Gloucestershire, near Bristol, England. Sumptuously set within 15-acre grounds, the 16th-century Tudor architecture and historic charm of this exceptional hotel are embodied not only in its stone walls but also in its majestic interiors, as evidenced by its spacious rooms and suites. Remarkably peaceful, the hotel provides the perfect tableau to the lush countryside of the Severn Valley. It encapsulates the best of castle life, with days spent relaxing in front of the fire, being pampered in the spa treatment room, enjoying royal afternoon tea, basking in idle contemplation accompanied by spectacular views over the enclosed gardens and indulging in the many activities nearby. It is an enchanting setting, that can be savoured in the four dining rooms of Thornbury Castle’s gourmet restaurant, where the focus is on creative cuisine that celebrates the essentials. Chef David Williams brilliantly blends garden vegetables and produce from local farmers, offering both traditional and vegetarian menus that will delight the most discerning of connoisseurs.

restaurant.name: Tower Restaurant
restaurant.description: Amid the Gothic surrounds of Thornbury Castle, Tower Restaurant boasts an atmosphere that is unmistakeably majestic yet relaxed. Whether ordering à la carte, during lunch, or enjoying a full six-course meal, Chef David Williams reinvents English culinary tradition with an acute eye for detail. His cuisine is full of character, balancing imagination with finesse.

name: Troisgros
description:
Troisgros is located in the heart of the countryside, at the foot of a vineyard. It is made up of a farmhouse where guests are received, a kitchen workshop where products are transformed, a restaurant, Le Bois sans Feuilles, were sharing is experienced, and a large Italian-style house, Le Bois Dormant, where relaxation may be had. 
All of these spaces have a modern soul and are surrounded by fields, woods, a lake, garden, and orchard. The taste of César, all guided by the simplicity in cooking, was without a doubt inspired by this new setting.

restaurant.name: Le Bois sans Feuilles Restaurant
restaurant.description: The love of cooking has been passed from father to son. It's all we think about. In terms of taste, we like to keep it simple, pure and free from artifice. There is nothing more soul-stirring than an original fusion of three or four carefully selected and measured ingredients. Our careful attention takes it to another level. Explore local resources, hone your skills, break free of routine and find wonder every day. That's the recipe for happiness.

name: Valverde Lisboa Hotel & Garden
description:
Valverde Lisboa Hotel & Garden is hidden behind a discreet door on Avenue da Liberdade, in one of the most exclusive areas of Lisbon. All the more charming for its hidden character, the hotel evokes the air of a stately town house with its perfectly polished design.
The deep colours that shade the walls contrast with the bright tones of the fabrics and cushions. A clever mix of retro details and antiques, vintage furniture and art prints extends from the entrance to the rooms. Bougain Avenida Restaurant celebrates traditional Portuguese craftsmanship, creating a warm and intimate setting in which to savor local cuisine accompanied by a live fado or jazz performance.

restaurant.name: Bougain Avenida Restaurant
restaurant.description: Bougain Avenida asserts its culinary style within a setting filled with contemporary art and surrounded by lush plant life. Chef João Santos favors simplicity over dramatic impact: seasonal ingredients, a nod to the Mediterranean and precise execution. Effortlessly elegant without conceit, his cuisine explores the balance between clarity of flavor and attention to detail.

name: Valverde Santar Hotel & Spa
description:
Surrounded by mountains that protect the area’s vineyards from the wind, the Dão winemaking region, an hour from Porto, is popular for its preserved landscapes and its exceptional wines. The Valverde Santar Hotel & Spa is the ideal destination to enjoy the charms of the Portuguese interior, in a setting that celebrates art and the art of living. Located in the picturesque village of Santar, renowned for its magnificent century-old residences and beautiful gardens, the property is a vast typical 17th-century manor house, partly belonging to the royal family.

At the entrance stands an imposing baroque fountain, which sets the tone for the decor. In the shared spaces, as in the guest rooms, period furniture and master paintings co-exist under painted or paneled ceilings. Gastronomy is also in the spotlight at the Valverde Santar Hotel & Spa, where you can enjoy modern cuisine that reinterprets traditional regional recipes. The property has collaborated for several years with the best local farmers, market gardeners and winegrowers, with all of whom it shares a taste for natural, seasonal products. Enjoy a bucolic, cultural and gourmet break, far from the daily grind.

restaurant.name: SÍTIO Santar Restaurant
restaurant.description: Here, the past simmers, transforms, then invites itself to the table. In the kitchens of SÍTIO Santar, the chef summons the flavors of the Beira region with skill and delicacy. Serra da Estrela cheese, Caramulo goat cheese and Viseu hazelnuts help tell the story of a vibrant culinary landscape, perpetuated by savoir-faire and a highly developed palate.

name: Vetera Matera
description: In the city of Matera, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Vetera Matera has installed its guest rooms and lounges in some of the Sassi, troglodyte dwellings dug into the mountainside in the Paleolithic era. This original undertaking lets guests explore the city and region while staying in this admirably restored world of stone. The contemporary décor, all of understated elegance, perfectly complements the rough, natural rock and the rooms’ broad windows look out onto scenery that has been the backdrop for countless film productions. The accommodations boast large terraces and are set on the heights of the city’s historic center, a district woven together by a tangle of alleys and spectacular staircases. The gourmet restaurant at Vetera Matera showcases specialties of the Basilicata region, while the spa invites you to slip into relaxation pools carved into the rock itself.

restaurant.name: ARTEMA Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the vaulted rooms that look over the visual spectacle of a city that appears to cling to the rock, the chef of Vetera Matera presents the specialties of the Basilicata region. Local meats, vegetables and cheeses take center stage, not forgetting the famous Matera bread, a renowned-and protected-regional delicacy.

nearestAirport: Bari Palese- International Airport Karol Wojtyla/Brindisi Airport (161 km)/Naples International Airport - Gesac (253km)

name: Villa Abbazia
description: The subtle charms of this 17th century palazzo and a Liberty-style villa will capture your heart. Romantic and intimate, this charming structure in the Venetian countryside is in a unique location at the crossroads of numerous Unesco treasures. At the juncture of the rose-coloured peaks of the Dolomites and Cortina, their pearl, the villas of Palladio, the Prosecco vineyards and near the fortified city of Treviso, known as the Gardens of Venice, there is plenty to see. Follina is the ideal starting point for visits to the most beautiful towns in Venetia and for active holidays.

restaurant.name: La Corte Restaurant
restaurant.description: In the elegant, intimate and warm setting of Villa Abbazia's gourmet restaurant, discover fine cuisine that combines simplicity and sophistication. Here at La Corte restaurant, chef Giuseppe Francica meticulously refines the authentic flavors of local produce, driven by a perpetually innovative approach.

name: Villa Cordevigo
description: In the hinterlands of Verona, above Lake Garda, a private drive lined with cypress trees leads to Villa Cordevigo. This 17th-century country mansion and chapel sit in a dreamlike setting amid the majestic trees of the park, vineyards and a characterful Italian garden. Charm, art and history, the splendid legacies of the noble families who have lived here over the centuries, await guests. Today, the Delibori and Cristoforetti families preserve the property's beauty and natural tranquillity while also producing fine wines on the surrounding estate. The creative energy and Mediterranean roots of Chef Marco Marras is at the heart of the cuisine of Oseleta restaurant, which is located in a picturesque outbuilding at the entrance to the estate.

restaurant.name: Oseleta Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Oseleta restaurant, chef Marco Marras directs every voice and every gesture to compose a culinary symphony. This creative energy makes all the flavors of the Mediterranean resonate. Located in an outbuilding of the Villa Cordevigo Wine Relais, guests attend a unique show combining tradition and authenticity with evert meal.

name: Villa Cortine Palace
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: On the shores of Lake Garda, in a vast park populated with cypress trees, and where ancient statues emerge from ornamental pools or cascades of roses, Villa Cortine Palace is arguably the perfect example of Italian romanticism. Located just a few minutes from the medieval city of Sirmione, the elegant building retains its original, 19th-century charms: high colonnades, coffered ceilings, wall frescoes, marble floors and intricate marquetry. Amid these surroundings is cultivated an atmosphere of refinement and joyful conviviality. Discover it in the Mediterranean cuisine served beneath the golden decor of Le Gardenie restaurant, on the terraces of Al Molo restaurant, Maria Callas cocktail bar and bistro and Belvédère restaurant, and in the guest rooms which face the stunning blue mirror of the lake.
services: at least one room is accessible
Breakfast Buffet, À la carte for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Le Gardenie Restaurant
restaurant.description: The chef at Le Gardenie explores the delicate nuances of Italian cuisine, with a focus on seasonal produce typical of the region. Its vegetarian menu combines a sense of indulgence with astonishing freshness, while the four-course flavors of Lake Garda' menu practically plunges you into its depths.

name: Villa Crespi
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Just a few steps from Lake Orta, Villa Crespi is a spectacular property built in 1879. The villa’s arabesque architecture, stunning gardens and the hospitality of Chef Antonino Cannavacciuolo and his wife Cinzia make a stay here magical. Antonino’s masterful cuisine is deeply influenced by the places and flavours experienced during his professional journey. His menu is an itinerary from southern to northern Italy, starting with a brochette of scallops and scampi and ending with pigeon and foie gras. From Villa Crespi you can contemplate a magnificent Italy, just 45 minutes away from Milan Malpensa: the medieval village of Orta and the Island of San Giulio, the famous site of Sacro Monte, and the Lake Maggiore.
services: Visit to our wine cellars and kitchens

restaurant.name: Restaurant Villa Crespi by chef Cannavacciuolo
restaurant.description: The cuisine served in the restaurant at Villa Crespi is a gastronomic link between the chef's home city of Naples and his adopted region of the Italian Alps. In celebrating the region's organic seasonal produce, he conceives dishes of astonishing flavor and the utmost refinement.

name: Villa della Pergola
description: Just an hour from the beautiful city of Genoa and on the heights of the Italian Riviera — famous for its posh seaside resorts, picturesque medieval towns and fishing villages — Villa della Pergola cultivates its deliciously timeless aura of architectural elegance. In perfect harmony with the two hectares of botanical gardens surrounding the estate, the fifteen luxurious suites and the numerous panoramic terraces invite you to savour the intensely sweet moments offered by this earthly paradise in the charming town of Alassio. Drink in spectacularly sublime views of the Ligurian Sea and fall under the spell of the unspoilt landscapes of the "Riviera di Ponente" coast. With its bounty of delicious wines and a healthy gastronomic mix, this region is one of the most romantic destinations in Italy — perfect for living la dolce vita to the fullest!

restaurant.name: Nove Restaurant
restaurant.description: Overlooking the Alassio coastline, Nove restaurant reflects the traditions and values of the region-a land of orchards, between sea and hills-with cuisine inspired by its neighboring Ligurian, Piedmontese and French culinary cultures. The chef and his team pay homage to the region's biodiversity by handling each ingredient with the greatest care. On the menu, discover delicate and balanced dishes featuring products grown in its own garden as well as the L'Orto Rampante farm-each an invitation to travel and discover the region.

name: Villa Florentine
description: Atop an enchanting hill, the Villa Florentine offers all the spirit and charm of the Italian Renaissance right in the heart of Lyon. The lush green terraces of this 17th century former convent offer exceptional views of the historical city of Lyon, a Unesco World Heritage site. Period furniture and contemporary works meet in the rooms, havens of peace and softness. An exquisitely calm pool invites you to a voyage of the senses. In the kitchen the chef deploys treasures of refinement and places the culinary focus on the product. At nightfall, the sparkling city lights join together with the stars.

restaurant.name: La Table de la Villa Florentine Restaurant
restaurant.description: Almost as much as guests, produce and ingredients are honored at La Table de la Villa Florentine. In every season, meals can be enjoyed while enjoying the panorama overlooking the rooftops of Old Lyon and the nearby hillside of Fourvière.

name: Villa Gallici
description: Paul Cézanne missed Aix terribly whenever he left, and never ceased to be inspired by its beauty. Many after him succumbed to the charm of this town and the ambience of this Florentine residence. The rooms are sumptuously decorated, and open onto a private garden filled with the fragrance of lavender. The cuisine pays tribute to Provence with dishes like sautéed loin of lamb and aubergine caviar with olives, black garlic sauce and fried basil. Try the famous local speciality of Calisson, sweets flavoured with melon and orange. Villa Gallici offers a special “Cézanne package”, which includes a visit to the painter’s studio.

restaurant.name: La Taula Gallici Restaurant
restaurant.description: The flavors of Provence and Italy marry on the plate at gourmet restaurant La Taula Gallici. Enjoyed on the terrace that feels like a Florentine patio or in the cozy atmosphere of the lounge, the inventive cuisine of chef Christophe Gavot makes for a particularly wondrous culinary experience. Tradition is tinged with exoticism to open the door to real culinary discoveries.

nearestAirport: Marseille (Intl) 30 km, Nice - Côte d'Azur (Intl) 168 km

name: Villa Grand Voile – Restaurant Christopher Coutanceau
description: Imagine a world apart where everything is designed for your pleasure. Here, along Relais & Châteaux’s Route du Bonheur, the clocks have stopped so that you may share in this timeless seaside adventure. Located just a stone’s throw from the chef’s eponymous restaurant, La Villa Grand Voile is the exciting realisation of Christopher Coutanceau and Nicolas Brossard’s ultimate dream: to offer their guests a unique, fully encompassing experience that conveys the true brilliance of this wonderful marine destination. Not far from the bustle of La Rochelle’s Old Port and tucked away along a quite street, this historic building invites you to relax and relish in the heart of this majestic maritime city of cobblestones, timber-framed facades and houses clad in white stone.
services: In at least one room, the restaurant and common areas at extra cost
At least one room, common areas and main restaurant are accessible
Packed lunch for hotel guests only

restaurant.name: Christopher Coutanceau Restaurant
restaurant.description: “The Charente-Maritime forges my identity and feeds me with inspiration  and the sea is my garden. It is through the products of the sea that I wish to tell the story of this place. These meagre, scampi and scallops - they are the ocean revealed before your eyes when you dine with us. The culinary approach of Christopher Coutanceau, a remarkable fisherman since childhood, is steeped in ancestral family traditions as well as an ardent commitment to sustainable and eco-responsible fishing practices. Through his passion and technique, his cuisine is a gastronomic cruise showcasing the marvellous wonders and aromas of the sea.

name: Villa Korta Katarina & Winery
description:
While travelling through the region on a humanitarian mission in 2001, philanthropists Lee and Penny Anderson found their way to the Croatian city of Orebić. It was love at first sight — a place where they could bring together both their passion for wine and their admiration for exquisite Dalmatian hospitality.

Standing proudly like an oppidum atop a hill, Villa Korta Katarina rises above the Adriatic and surrounding vineyards with undeniable majesty. Its rooms and suites, each boasting sea views, offer modernity in a setting of classical elegance. With a soothing palette of pale blue, grey, white and cream, their decor is a reflection of the fascinating Pelješac Peninsula and its skies of enchanting pastel hues.
services: Individual cooking classes given by the chef
Group cooking classes given by the chef

restaurant.name: The Dining Room Restaurant
restaurant.description: As a native of Croatia, I use my knowledge of local recipes and ingredients to bring out the best flavors and highlight the charms of Pelješac Peninsula cuisine. The creative blending of traditional dishes and new cooking trends will awaken your senses and take you on a gustatory journey around the world from ancient times to today.

name: Villa Miraé - Cap d'Antibes
description: In this blue paradise, the tranquility is disturbed only by the sound of waves carried from the Anse de la Garoupe below–the cove can easily be reached via a short private path. Amid this unique landscape of umbrella pines and ochre rocks–scenery that inspired artists including Nicolas de Staël and Pablo Picasso–stands a large, beautiful mansion: Villa Miraé. Its guest rooms, adorned with natural materials and a palette of yellow, green or blue, evoke the surroundings. Each one features a terrace or balcony that opens onto the lush Mediterranean garden, where a swimming pool is nestled within. In the summer, the private sundeck on the beach, lined with succulents and laurel, epitomizes this oasis of coolness and calm. The Mediterranean vibe continues with the cuisine served at Villa Miraé’s two restaurants, where Chef Mauro Colagreco offers vibrant, refined cuisine.

restaurant.name: Amarines by Mauro Colagreco Restaurant
restaurant.description: Inspired by the Mediterranean and the rhythm of nature, Amarines by Mauro Colagreco celebrates the finest produce sourced from responsible fishing and sustainable agriculture. Guided by the seasons, the chef creates vibrant dishes, where each ingredient expresses the beauty of its origin, whether coast or land.

name: Villa Pétrusse
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: At the foot of the Old City of Luxembourg, encircled by grounds more than a century old crafted by celebrated landscape designer Edouard André, Villa Pétrusse has made its home in a private residence, an architectural jewel that is emblematic of the city’s late-19th-century transformation. Travel back in time in the refined elegance and old-world charm of a property that exudes the subtle, unostentatious warmth of a well-appointed family home inhabited by discerning art lovers. The room décor includes reproductions of watercolors by illustrious Luxembourgish painter Sosthène Weis; all have a breathtaking view of the grounds and the adjacent Pétrusse, the river for which the hotel is named. This subtle combination of past and present that defines the Villa’s atmosphere also serves to inspire Kim de Dood, chef at the gourmet restaurant Le Lys, serving traditional Luxembourgish cuisine tinged with Asian influences and inspired by the seasons.

restaurant.name: Le Lys Restaurant
restaurant.description: At restaurant Le Lys, Chef Kim de Dood fuses traditional Luxembourg cuisine with delicate Asian influences. Having returned to his home country after gaining international experience in Singapore and Kuala Lumpur, the young chef has transformed the restaurant at La Villa Pétrusse into a must-visit culinary destination.

name: Villa Principe Leopoldo
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: Ticino, Switzerland, is blessed with a balmy climate, stunning scenery and the infinite pleasures of “la dolce vita”. It is a land of lakes and mountains, fabulous food, fine wines and endless leisure pursuits. The elegant Villa Principe Leopoldo is situated on the “Collina d’Oro”, with stunning views over Lake Lugano Bay and the mountains. The villa is characterised by its strong Italian spirit and Mediterranean style, accommodating spacious rooms and suites. The La Luce Gourmet Restaurant offers a cuisine that reveals authenticity, passion and respect for its ingredients, not forgetting tradition and territory.

restaurant.name: La Luce Gourmet Restaurant
restaurant.description: At the heart of Villa Principe Leopoldo, La Luce Gourmet blends Italian culinary tradition with contemporary inspiration. Chef Christian Moreschi skillfully reveals each product, respecting the purity of its flavor and innate texture. He takes diners on a journey of carefully composed dishes that combine creative freedom with precision.

name: Villa René Lalique - Hôtel & Restaurant
description: Built in 1920 in Wingen-sur-Moder in the Bas-Rhin by René Lalique, the great art nouveau jeweller and celebrated master of art deco glass, the Villa René Lalique - Hôtel & Restaurant is now an exceptional hotel and refined restaurant — a living and vibrant tribute to its creator. Nestled within the heart of the Northern Vosges Regional Natural Park, the six exclusive suites, each inspired by an original design by the avant-garde artist, tell the story of a name that has become the crowning jewel of the French crystal industry. Within the gourmet restaurant’s large glass-walled dining room, Austrian Chef Paul Stradner works with his team to prepare intense and highly colorful creative cuisine. He's joined by pastry chef Jonathan Bunel, who draws inspiration from French tradition and modern influences for his sweet creations, and Alsatian head sommelier Romain Iltis, who oversees Villa René Lalique - Hôtel & Restaurant’s exceptional cellar, one of the finest in Europe, with no fewer than 60,000 bottles. Hervé Schmitt, the restaurant’s manager, directs the Lalique’s table service and setting like a ballet.

restaurant.name: Villa René Lalique Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Villa René Lalique, chef Paul Stradner prioritizes flavor over complexity. He develops a personal cuisine rooted in the Alsatian terroir, driven by a constant pursuit of precision, where each dish expresses a delicate balance between intensity and clarity.

name: VILLINO
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: VILLINO is an exquisite hotel with award-winning cuisine, located above Lindau, in the heart of lush fruit orchards, at the crossroads of Germany, Austria and Switzerland. Chef Toni Neumann has achieved extensive acclaim for the restaurant with his Italian-Asian cuisine, complimented by an international wine list with no less than 900 different selections. A charming garden, based on traditional English and Italian designs, surrounds the hotel and invites to a stroll. The deep, true blue of the iris is the hotel’s logo. The diversity of the artistic, cultural and leisure activities on and around Lake Constance ensures there is something for everyone here.

restaurant.name: VILLINO Restaurant
restaurant.description: Between the shores of Lake Constance and the peaks of the Alps, Villino explores the culinary intersection between Italy and Asia. Chef Toni Neumann selects local ingredients, devises unexpected combinations and expertly blends flavors. Every detail contributes to the experience, from the refined presentation of each dish to the table setting.

name: Waldhotel Doldenhorn
description:
Large chalets surrounded by evergreen trees, a heavenly view over the mountains, this family hotel in the small ski resort of Kandersteg makes for a postcard perfect setting. 
Summer or winter, you are invited to savour the delights of the surrounding nature directly accessible from the hotel or to enjoy the many spa facilities (swimming pool, sauna, baths, relaxation area…) built to the latest environmental standards. The rooms are lovely arranged and offer expansive views of the forest or of the mountains. The fine reception, the warm atmosphere, and the wonderful traditional cuisine contribute all the more to reinvigorating the body and soul.

restaurant.name: Au Gourmet Restaurant
restaurant.description: Here, refinement doesn't seek to be sensational: instead, it dwells in the details, in its respect for provenance and authenticity of taste. Chef René Maeder cultivates the spirit of Au Gourmet with precise, flavorful cuisine, inspired by the seasons and Alpine ingredients. In this intimate setting, each course demonstrates that tradition is always ready to be reinvented.

name: Walliserhof Grand-Hotel & Spa
description: In every direction, the jagged peaks of the Valais Alps pierce the skies. This is the perfect setting for a serene high-altitude retreat combining sports, wellness and culinary delights. Walliserhof Grand-Hotel & Spa is located in the center of the car-free village of Saas-Fee, an intimate ski resort surrounded by mountains over 13,000 feet above sea level and known as ‘the pearl of the Alps.’ Throughout the year, the hotel invites guests to relax and recharge, indoors and out. Decorated with sustainable and natural materials such as local wood, slate and wool, guest rooms exude comfort and warmth as well as offering breathtaking views of the glaciers. There are three restaurants for you to choose from, each showcasing local, seasonal produce. Finally, its immense spa, with a relaxation area bathed in light, promises an unforgettable moment.

restaurant.name: Cäsar Ritz Restaurant
restaurant.description: We believe that sustainable development and respect for the environment should start with the supply chain. This is absolutely necessary in order to offer experiences that are pleasurable, authentic and responsible.

name: Weeshuis Gouda
description: Next to the famed Jerusalem Chapel, this 16th-century orphanage is now home to 25 intimate, atypical and fairly eccentric suites. With elegant original details and a wink to the 21st century, Weeshuis Gouda celebrates the discrete charm of this city nestled in the country’s “Green Heart” and in easy reach of The Hague, Rotterdam and Utrecht. Carefully conceived to underscore the city’s rich heritage, it is not only the old and authentic that you will find at this design hotel. A voyage through the ages, it blends beautifully with the soul of Gouda’s historic district — its maze of narrow streets, bridges and canals, its fascinating museum, its cheesemakers and its sweet aroma of hot syrup and waffles in the air. Delighting your senses with world-class dining, chef Remco Kuijpers’ fresh creations and focus on local ingredients add the final touch to your stay at this magical place.

restaurant.name: LIZZ Restaurant
restaurant.description: Restaurant LIZZ stands for garden-fresh, light, seasonal and sustainable cuisine. The chef's love for healthy food is reflected in incredible vegetarian options on each menu. He carefully selects only the best ingredients, ideally from local suppliers and producers. All are harvested at the perfect moment and used in an exploratory way  authentic but always with a modern twist - taking into account the local setting of Weeshuis Gouda and its historical references. Chef Remco Kuijpers, maître/sommelier Louis Grizeau and the rest of the dedicated WSHS team hope that a visit to our restaurant will always remind you of the unique taste of Holland, and instill lasting positive memories.

name: Weissenhaus Private Nature Luxury Resort
description: Bordered by a long beach on the Baltic Sea, the Weissenhaus Private Nature Luxury Resort invites guests to rest and restore a sense of well-being. With its 185-acre park, its award-winning spa and four different restaurants, this 19th-century residence offers an ideal setting for restorative breaks away from the frenzy of the city. Stay in cottages, suites and rooms spread throughout the chateau itself and its delightful outbuildings and lushes private gardens. Enjoy the sunshine and sea breeze from a cozy beach cabana, take a plunge in the warmth of the thermal spa's indoor pool, or relax with a yoga session in the garden. With a relaxed state of mind and an appetite for culinary adventure, count on BOOTSHAUS to stimulate all your senses: Weissenhaus Private Nature Luxury Resort’s gourmet restaurant takes its name from the painter whose works adorn its opulent interiors. Discover excellent cuisine that blends classical refinement with the creativity modern French gastronomy.

restaurant.name: BOOTSHAUS Restaurant
restaurant.description: Its large terraces, sea breeze and glorious sunsets lend Bootshaus Italian restaurant an atmosphere reminiscent of summer nights overlooking the Mediterranean. Located directly on the beach, and offering panoramic views over the sea, it's the perfect setting for unforgettable memories. Local, seasonal products are at the heart of its cuisine, which reinterprets Italian classics in innovative and contemporary ways. Savor a wide variety of delicacies for lunch, or coffee and sweet treats in the afternoon. In the evening, let yourself be seduced by the combination of the cuisine, locale and quality of service.

name: Yoann Conte
locationType: Near a river, lake or stream
description: This fine early 20th-century mansion on the banks of Lake Annecy is a rhapsody of blue and gold: the blue of the façade and the lake viewed from the terrace meets the golden yellow of the wood cladding the walls and the sun that shines into the guestrooms through the large picture windows. This house offers an intimate setting with a warm, cosy ambiance. And all around is resplendent nature, as celebrated by the Chef's cuisine. Since 2010, Yoann Conte, following in the footsteps of Marc Veyrat, has drawn his inspiration from the passing seasons and their plants to reinterpret local cuisine, serving, for example, fish from the lake with fresh herbs and alpine flowers.

restaurant.name: La Table de Yoann Conte - Restaurant Gastronomique
restaurant.description: I'm trying to make sense of my life trajectory with my cuisine. Breton by birth and Savoyard by adoption, I combine produce from both of these regions in balanced creations where the seasonality and simplicity of the ingredients are retained. I'm also interested in human relationships with regard to our preferred producers, whether they be local or further afield, as well as my team, so as to achieve perfect symbiosis for our guests and with it, some degree of intimacy

name: Ytri Island Retreat
description: In the heart of the Træna archipelago in northern Norway, Husøy is home to a fishing community that has lived in harmony with nature and cultivated its unique sense of tradition for generations. This powerful cultural heritage permeates Ytri. Its wooden architecture, drawing inspiration from traditional Norwegian fishers’ houses, blends seamlessly into the natural surroundings and offers panoramic views of the rugged beauty of the archipelago’s mountains. Guests can enjoy these vistas from spacious lounges as well as from rooms and suites decorated in a cozy yet minimalist Nordic style. Here, you are immersed in the traditions of the Arctic Circle. This authentic Scandinavian experience–including hiking and whale watching–extends to Ytri’s restaurant, where every dish is inspired by the day’s catch and local ingredients.

restaurant.name: ALMA Restaurant
restaurant.description: At Alma restaurant, Chef Sean Ryan invites you to discover the freshness and intensity of local produce, not least from the daily catch. His culinary philosophy is showcased in a 12-course tasting menu that combines impeccable technical finesse with remarkable creativity.
